## PAGE 00129: Calling operations from the model

- page_id: `189142853`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189142853/Calling+operations+from+the+model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:39:35.195+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Specifying criteria for querying model > Creating new operations
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1325379609

1325379611

1325379608

**On this page**

****

1325379610

In a structured expression, you can call an operation defined in the model. An operation from the model can be either built-in or custom. The built-in operations, such as Union, Find, or Filter, are stored as executable opaque behaviors in the *Built-In Operations* package, which is owned by *UML Standard Profile::MagicDraw Profile*.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Be sure the **Exclude Used Projects** command on the Filter Options button[IMAGE alt='' src=''] menu is unchecked!

1325389280

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating new operations' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating script operations' space='']

****

An operation from the model can be either built-in or custom. The built-in operations, such as Union, Find, or Filter, are stored as executable opaque behaviors in the <em>Built-In Operations</em> package, which is owned by <em>UML Standard Profile::MagicDraw Profile</em>.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_opaque_behavior_dialog.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1669fbc7-3eea-4fe1-9ceb-e3edc9bfdf3b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Be sure the <strong>Exclude Used Projects</strong> command on the Filter Options <span>button</span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="filter.gif" /></ac:image> menu is unchecked!</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e81bef9b-1159-4066-ba5a-61ec11c4b409"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1325389280</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating new operations" /></ac:link><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating script operations" /></ac:link></li></ul></li></ul><p><strong><br /></strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189143049 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00130: Case 1. Coloring Ports by Type using Contains operation

- page_id: `189143049`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189143049/Case+1.+Coloring+Ports+by+Type+using+Contains+operation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:39:39.685+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Specifying criteria for querying model > Case Studies for Querying the Model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

967284465

967284478

967284468

To color ports by their type

1. Create a **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Legends' space='']** and a **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating and managing Legend Items' space='']**.
2. Right-click the Legend Item to open its Specification window .
3. C lick three dots [IMAGE alt='' src=''] next to the Adornment property to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying Legend Item adornment' space=''] . Click OK .
4. Click three dots [IMAGE alt='' src=''] next to the **Elements by Condition**property to edit it. **Elements by Condition** dialog opens.
5. Select Create operation > **Metachain Navigation** .
6. Click **Insert**.
7. Under **Metaclass or Stereotype**, select **Port**, and under**Property**, select****Type****. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
8. Select Create operation > **Operation from Model** > **Contains** .
9. Drag-and-drop Metachain Navigation onto Input parameter. [ATTACHMENT filename='image2021-12-28_11-52-55.png']
10. Select Obj > Element > select the port type to verify. [ATTACHMENT filename='image2021-12-28_11-58-25.png']
11. Click OK .

967284464

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Case Studies for Querying the Model' space='']true

## PAGE 00131: Case 10. Filtering Diagrams by Modification Date

- page_id: `189142892`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189142892/Case+10.+Filtering+Diagrams+by+Modification+Date
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:39:36.884+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Specifying criteria for querying model > Case Studies for Querying the Model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Groovy

1360697410

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Groovy

1360697431

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Groovy

1360697421

To filter diagrams by modification date

1. Create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Generic Table' space=''] .
2. Set the Element Type to Diagram . Make sure to select the Include Subtypes check box. Click OK .
3. Set the Scope where the diagrams will be searched for.
4. Click the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] icon****in the**Scope**field and s elect **Custom** . The Query dialog opens.
5. Select **Create operation** > **Filter**. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
6. Select Filter > Input > Find . [ATTACHMENT filename='3_case_10.png']
7. Set the Type to Diagram . [ATTACHMENT filename='4_case_10.png']
8. Expand Find > Scope > Contextual Variable . [ATTACHMENT filename='5_case_10.png']
9. Select **Filter**> **Predicate** > **Operation from Model** > **GreaterThanOrEquals**. If you cannot see the **GreaterThanOrEquals** operation, make sure the**Apply Filter**box is not checked. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
10. Select Predicate > Body > A > Script . [ATTACHMENT filename='7_case_10.png']
11. From the Language drop-down list, select **Groovy**.
12. Insert the following script as the **Body**: Groovy [IMAGE alt='' src='']
13. Select A > arg1 > Reset . [ATTACHMENT filename='9_case_10.png']
14. Select A > arg1 > Simple Navigation . [ATTACHMENT filename='10_case_10.png']
15. Select Modification date and set Is Applied to true . [ATTACHMENT filename='11_case_10.png']
16. Select Predicate > Body > B > Script . [ATTACHMENT filename='12_case_10.png']
17. From the Language drop-down list, select **Groovy**.
18. Insert the following script as the **Body**: Groovy [IMAGE alt='' src='']
19. Click **OK**. To view the column, make sure to display it via the **Columns** button in the diagram toolbar > **Select Columns** > set the **Modification date** column to **true**.

1360697408

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Case Studies for Querying the Model' space='']true

````html
import java.util.Date;

if (arg1) {
Date date1 = DateFormat.getDateInstance(DateFormat.SHORT).parse(arg1.iterator().next()); 
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="8_case_10.png" /></ac:image></p></li><li>Select <strong>A </strong>&gt; <strong>arg1</strong> &gt; <strong>Reset</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="9_case_10.png" /></ac:image></li><li>Select <strong>A </strong>&gt; <strong>arg1</strong> &gt; <strong>Simple Navigation</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="10_case_10.png" /></ac:image></li><li>Select <strong>Modification date </strong>and set <strong>Is Applied</strong> to <strong>true</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="11_case_10.png" /></ac:image></li><li>Select <strong>Predicate</strong> &gt; <strong>Body </strong>&gt; <strong>B</strong> &gt; <strong>Script</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="12_case_10.png" /></ac:image></li><li><p>From the Language drop-down list, select <strong>Groovy</strong>.</p></li><li><p>Insert the following script as the <strong>Body</strong>:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="84e57745-fee0-486b-8739-fb0bc50925b9"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Groovy</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Date M1 = new Date();
M1.setMonth(M1.getMonth() - 1);
M1]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="13_case_10.png" /></ac:image></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">OK</strong>. To view the column, make sure to display it via the <strong>Columns</strong> button in the diagram toolbar &gt; <strong>Select Columns</strong> &gt; set the <strong>Modification date</strong> column to <strong>true</strong>.</p></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4f0052cb-1f55-4f85-8d46-48785e9a4820"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1360697408</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt-include" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fc224fd4-55dd-45d0-a18c-365f86d8ad24"><ac:parameter ac:name=""><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Case Studies for Querying the Model" /></ac:link></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="nopanel">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189142907 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00132: Case 11. Server Project Version in Diagram

- page_id: `189142907`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189142907/Case+11.+Server+Project+Version+in+Diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:39:37.394+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Specifying criteria for querying model > Case Studies for Querying the Model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: JavaScript Rhino

1476040892

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: JavaScript Rhino

1476040912

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: JavaScript Rhino

1476040902

To retrieve the Server Project Version in a Diagram

1. Right-click the empty space on the diagram pane and open the Specification window .
2. On the left side of the dialog, select Traceability > Create . Expression dialog opens.
3. Select **Create operation**>******Script**. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. Specify the Name for the derived property.
5. Set the Type as String .
6. Specify the Operation Name .
7. From the Language drop-down list, select JavaScript Rhino .
8. Insert the following script as the **Body**. JavaScript Rhino [IMAGE alt='' src='']
9. Click **OK**.

To display the Server Project Version in a Diagram

1. Create the **Note** symbol in the diagram and anchor it to the diagram.
2. Right-click the **Note** symbol and deselect **Represent Diagram Context (Package)**.
3. (Optional) Right-click the **Note** symbol and deselect **Apply SysML Callout Style**.
4. Right-click the **Note** symbol and select **Edit Compartments**.
5. In the **Element Properties** tab of the **Compartment Edit** dialog, select the name of the property you created. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
6. Click OK . You can see the property name and the server project version in the **Note** symbol. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1361035242

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Case Studies for Querying the Model' space='']true

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e097c4b1-b463-4d21-ae0a-c523a6c4048b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1476040892</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ac4b068f-5ee1-4baa-b399-0cc53ce9af2b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1476040912</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0705b70d-5668-46d9-bd64-3b74603d81fc"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1476040902</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>To retrieve the Server Project Version in a Diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the empty space on the diagram pane and open the <strong>Specification</strong> <strong>window</strong>.</li><li>On the left side of the dialog, select <strong>Traceability</strong> &gt; <strong>Create</strong>. <strong>Expression</strong> dialog opens.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Select <strong>Create operation </strong>&gt;<strong> </strong><strong>Script</strong>.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="1_create_operation_script.png" /></ac:image></p></li><li>Specify the <strong>Name</strong> for the derived property.</li><li>Set the <strong>Type</strong> as <strong>String</strong>.</li><li>Specify the <strong>Operation Name</strong>.</li><li>From the <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Language </strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">drop-down list, select </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">JavaScript Rhino</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">.</span></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> Insert the following script as the <strong>Body</strong></span></span>. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7534e3e7-a876-4dd7-92cb-a43fd7a08dde"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">JavaScript Rhino</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[var prj = com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Application.getInstance().getProject().getPrimaryProject();
var ver = com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.ProjectUtilities.getVersion(prj);
ver.getName();]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="1_expression_dialog.png" /></ac:image></p></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> Click <strong>OK</strong>.</span></li></ol><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><br /></span></p><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">To display the Server Project Version in a Diagram</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Create the <strong>Note</strong> symbol in the diagram and </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">anchor it to the diagram.<br /></span></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Right-click the <strong>Note</strong> symbol and deselect <strong>Represent Diagram Context (Package)</strong>.<br /></span></li><li><span>(Optional) Right-click the <strong>Note</strong> symbol and deselect <strong>Apply SysML Callout Style</strong>.</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><br /></span></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Right-click the <strong>Note</strong> symbol and select <strong>Edit Compartments</strong>.</span></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">In the <strong>Element Properties</strong> tab of the <strong>Compartment Edit</strong> dialog, select the name of the property you created.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="5_edit_compartment.png" /></ac:image></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color: rgb(62,63,64);">OK</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">. You can see the property name and the server project version in the <strong>Note</strong> symbol.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="6_display_server_project_version.png" /></ac:image></span></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ea815d25-8915-4e8d-8447-68b1b6b3121d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1361035242</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt-include" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7cf102d3-32cc-4d79-9f1d-6e4906d9ae99"><ac:parameter ac:name=""><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Case Studies for Querying the Model" /></ac:link></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="nopanel">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189142918 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00133: Case 12. Activity Decomposition Table

- page_id: `189142918`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189142918/Case+12.+Activity+Decomposition+Table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:39:37.720+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Specifying criteria for querying model > Case Studies for Querying the Model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1660070392

1660070413

1660070403

To create an Activity Decomposition Table

1. Create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Generic Table' space=''] .
2. Set the Element Type to Activity .
3. Set the Scope to the Activity element for which decomposition hierarchy will be displayed.
4. Open the Specification window of the Generic Table and click three dots [IMAGE alt='' src=''] next to the Query property to edit it.
5. In the **Query** dialog, select **Custom**.
6. Select **Metachain Navigation**.
7. Click **Insert**.
8. Under Metaclass or Stereotype , select CallBehaviorAction , and under Property , select Behavior . [ATTACHMENT filename='1_metachain_navigation.png']
9. Select **Context** > **Reset**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
10. Select **Context** > **Operation from Model**>**Behavior Decomposition**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
11. Select **Element** > **Reset**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
12. Select **Element** > **Contextual Variable**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
13. Select the **Build Hierarchy** check box to recursively execute the specified expression for building a multilevel hierarchy starting from the scope as the root.
14. Click **OK**.

1361105380

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Case Studies for Querying the Model' space='']true

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189142929 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00134: Case 13. Coloring Associations between Block and Use Case

- page_id: `189142929`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189142929/Case+13.+Coloring+Associations+between+Block+and+Use+Case
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:39:37.908+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Specifying criteria for querying model > Case Studies for Querying the Model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1904151418

1904151438

1904151428

Prerequisites:

To color Associations between a Block and a Use Case

1. Create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Legends' space=''] and a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating and managing Legend Items' space=''] .
2. Right-click the Legend Item and open its Specification window .
3. Click three dots [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] next to the Adornment property to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying Legend Item adornment' space=''] . Click OK .
4. Click three dots [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] next to the Elements by Condition property to edit it. Elements by Condition dialog opens.
5. In the Elements by Condition dialog, set the Element Type to Association .
6. Select **Create Operation** > **Operation from Model** > **IfThenElse**. If you cannot see the **IfThenElse** operation, make sure the **Apply Filter** box is not checked. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
7. Select IfThenElse > Condition > Reset . [ATTACHMENT filename='2_Coloring_Associations_between_Block_and_Use_Case.png']
8. Select Condition > Type Test . [ATTACHMENT filename='3_Coloring_Associations_between_Block_and_Use_Case.png']
9. Set the Operation Name to ‘1st End is Actor’ and Type to Block . [ATTACHMENT filename='4_Coloring_Associations_between_Block_and_Use_Case.png']
10. Select Condition > Context > Reset . [ATTACHMENT filename='5_Coloring_Associations_between_Block_and_Use_Case.png']
11. Select Condition > Context > Operation from Model > First End of Association . [ATTACHMENT filename='6_Coloring_Associations_between_Block_and_Use_Case.png']
12. Select **Condition** > **Context** > **Association** > **Reset**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
13. Select **Association** > **Contextual Variable**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
14. Select IfThenElse > Then > Type Test . [ATTACHMENT filename='9_Coloring_Associations_between_Block_and_Use_Case.png']
15. Set the Operation Name to ‘2nd end is Use Case’ and Type to UseCase . [ATTACHMENT filename='10_Coloring_Associations_between_Block_and_Use_Case.png']
16. Select Then > Context > Reset . [ATTACHMENT filename='11_Coloring_Associations_between_Block_and_Use_Case.png']
17. Select Then > Context > Operation from Model > Second End of Association . [ATTACHMENT filename='12_Coloring_Associations_between_Block_and_Use_Case.png']
18. Select **Then** > **Context** > **Association** > **Reset**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
19. Select **Association** > **Contextual Variable**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
20. Select IfThenElse > Else > Operation from Model > IfThenElse . [ATTACHMENT filename='15_Coloring_Associations_between_Block_and_Use_Case.png']
21. Select Else > Condition > Reset . [ATTACHMENT filename='16_Coloring_Associations_between_Block_and_Use_Case.png']
22. Select Condition > Type Test . [ATTACHMENT filename='17_Coloring_Associations_between_Block_and_Use_Case.png']
23. Set the Operation Name to ‘1st End is Use Case’ and Type to UseCase . [ATTACHMENT filename='18_Coloring_Associations_between_Block_and_Use_Case.png']
24. Select Condition > Context > Reset . [ATTACHMENT filename='19_Coloring_Associations_between_Block_and_Use_Case.png']
25. Select Condition > Context > Operation from Model > First End of Association. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
26. Select **Condition** > **Context** > **Association** > **Reset**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
27. Select **Association** > **Contextual Variable**. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
28. Select Else > Then > Type Test . [ATTACHMENT filename='23_Coloring_Associations_between_Block_and_Use_Case.png']
29. Set the Operation Name to ‘2nd End is Actor’ and Type to Block . [ATTACHMENT filename='24_Coloring_Associations_between_Block_and_Use_Case.png']
30. Select Then > Context > Reset . [ATTACHMENT filename='25_Coloring_Associations_between_Block_and_Use_Case.png']
31. Select Then > Context > Operation from Model > Second End of Association . [ATTACHMENT filename='26_Coloring_Associations_between_Block_and_Use_Case.png']
32. Select **Then** > **Context** > **Association** > **Reset**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
33. Select **Association** > **Contextual Variable**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
34. Select Then > Else > Boolean > false . [ATTACHMENT filename='29_Coloring_Associations_between_Block_and_Use_Case.png']
35. Click OK .

1904151417

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Case Studies for Querying the Model' space='']true

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189142970 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00135: Case 13.1. First End Of Association

- page_id: `189142970`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`

1361152905

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Javascript Rhino

1361152895

To create the First End Of Association operation for coloring Associations between a Block and a Use Case

1. Create an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opaque Behavior' space=''] named 'First End Of Association'.
2. Right-click the Opaque Behavior element and open its Specification window . Do one of the following:
  1. Click the plus sign next to the **Owned Parameter** property to edit it. If you cannot see the****Owned Parameter****property, make sure the **Expert** mode is enabled.
  2. On the left side menu, select Parameters > Create .
3. In the Specification of Parameter window , set Name to Association , Type to Association , and Direction to in . Click Back .
4. In the Opaque Behavior Specification window , click three dots [IMAGE alt='' src=''] next to the **Body and Language** property to edit it.
5. In the Body and Language dialog, select Language > StructuredExpression .
6. Click **Create operation** > **Script**. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
7. Set the Operation Name to ‘First End’.
8. From the Language drop-down list, select Javascript Rhino .
9. Insert the following script as the **Body**: jsJavascript Rhino0 ? arg1.iterator().next() : null]]> [IMAGE alt='' src='']
10. Select First End > arg1 > Reset . [ATTACHMENT filename='3_First_End_Of_Association.png']
11. Select First End > arg1 > Metachain Navigation . [ATTACHMENT filename='4_First_End_Of_Association.png']
12. Click Insert .
13. Under Metaclass or Stereotype , select Association , and under Property , select End Type . [ATTACHMENT filename='5_First_End_Of_Association.png']
14. Click OK .

1361152883

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Case Studies for Querying the Model' space='']true

To create the Second End Of Association operation for coloring Associations between a Block and a Use Case

1. Create an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opaque Behavior' space=''] named 'Second End Of Association'.
2. Right-click the Opaque Behavior element and open its Specification window . Do one of the following:
  1. Click the plus sign next to the **Owned Parameter** property to edit it. If you cannot see the****Owned Parameter****property, make sure the **Expert** mode is enabled.
  2. On the left side menu, select Parameters > Create .
3. In the Specification of Parameter window , set Name to Association , Type to Association , and Direction to in . Click Back .
4. In the Opaque Behavior Specification window , click three dots [IMAGE alt='' src=''] next to the **Body and Language** property to edit it.
5. In the Body and Language dialog, select Language > StructuredExpression .
6. Click **Create operation** > **Script**. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
7. Set the Operation Name to ‘Second End’.
8. From the Language drop-down list, select Javascript Rhino .
9. Insert the following script as the **Body**: jsJavascript Rhino [IMAGE alt='' src='']
10. Select Second End > arg1 > Reset . [ATTACHMENT filename='3_Second_End_Of_Association.png']
11. Select Second End > arg1 > Metachain Navigation . [ATTACHMENT filename='4_Second_End_Of_Association.png']
12. Click Insert .
13. Under Metaclass or Stereotype , select Association , and under Property , select End Type . [ATTACHMENT filename='5_Second_End_Of_Association.png']
14. Click OK .

## PAGE 00137: Case 14. Incoming Association of Class

- page_id: `189142992`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189142992/Case+14.+Incoming+Association+of+Class
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:39:38.662+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Specifying criteria for querying model > Case Studies for Querying the Model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1361199137

1361199148

1361199147

To get Incoming Associations of a Class

1. Right-click a Class element and open the Specification window > Traceability > Create .
2. Set the Name to 'Incoming associations'.
3. Click **Create operation** > **Filter**.
4. Select Input > Metachain Navigation .
5. Click Insert .
6. Under Metaclass or Stereotype , select Class, and under Property , select _associationOfEndType . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2021-12-30_13-48-40.png']
7. Select Predicate > Operation from Model > Equals . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2021-12-30_13-49-54.png']
8. Select A > Metachain Navigation . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2022-1-4_17-25-30.png']
9. Click Insert .
10. Under Metaclass or Stereotype , select Property, and under Property , select Type . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2022-1-4_17-27-8.png']
11. Select Context > Reset . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2022-1-4_17-27-49.png']
12. Select Context > Filter . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2022-1-4_17-34-13.png']
13. Select**Input** > **Simple Navigation**. Select **Member End** and set **Is Applied** to **true**, and **Direction** to **Source To Target**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] If you cannot see the **Member End******property, make sure the 'Show relations criteria available only for context' check box is not selected.
14. Select Predicate > Nested Operation . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2022-1-4_17-37-28.png']
15. Select Body > Simple Navigation . Select isNavigable() (Port) and set Is Applied to true , and Direction to Source To Target . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2022-1-4_17-42-6.png']
16. Select B > Contextual Variable . Set the Value to THIS . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2022-1-4_17-42-51.png']
17. Click OK .

1361199136

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Case Studies for Querying the Model' space='']true

## PAGE 00138: Case 15. Requirements Coverage by Design Elements

- page_id: `189143012`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189143012/Case+15.+Requirements+Coverage+by+Design+Elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:39:38.965+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Specifying criteria for querying model > Case Studies for Querying the Model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: JavaScript Rhino

1361225459

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: JavaScript Rhino

1361225479

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: JavaScript Rhino

1361225469

Prerequisites:

To create a Requirements Coverage by Design Elements Legend

1. Create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Legends' space=''] .
2. Create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating and managing Legend Items' space=''] named '0%-25%'.
3. Right-click the Legend Item and open its Specification window .
4. C lick three dots [IMAGE alt='' src=''] next to the Elements by Condition property to edit it.
5. In the **Elements by Condition**dialog, set the **Element Type** to **Requirement**.
6. Rename **Custom** to **Query**.
7. Select Create Operation > Operation from Model > All Leaf Requirements Owned Recursively . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2021-12-31_10-1-24.png']
8. Select Input > Reset . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2021-12-31_10-1-45.png']
9. Select Input > Contextual Variable . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2021-12-31_10-2-20.png']
10. Select All Leaf Requirements Owned Recursively > Edit > Copy . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2021-12-31_10-4-49.png']
11. Select the root node Query > Edit > Paste . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2021-12-31_10-7-45.png']
12. Select the copied operation > Use as... > Input of a New Filter . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2021-12-31_10-10-10.png']
13. Select **Predicate** > **Nested Operation**. [ATTACHMENT filename='image2021-12-31_10-11-40.png']
14. Select **Body** > **Operation from Model** > **Not**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
15. Select **A** > **Reset**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
16. Select **A** > **Operation from Model** > **isEmpty**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
17. Select Input > Simple Navigation . Select Satisfied By and set Is Applied to true , and Direction to Source To Target . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2021-12-31_10-30-59.png']
18. Select All Leaf Requirements Owned Recursively > Use as... > arg of a new Script .
19. From the Language drop-down list, select Javascript Rhino .
20. Insert the following script as the **B****ody**: jsJavaScript RhinolowerBound && arg2.size()/arg1.size() <= upperBound]]> [IMAGE alt='' src='']
21. Drag-and-drop Filter onto Create parameter to create a second parameter for the Script . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2021-12-31_12-6-19.png']
22. Select Filter > Remove . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2021-12-31_12-7-26.png']
23. Select **Create parameter** > **Element** > select the **Legend Item** for which the expression is defined. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''][IMAGE alt='' src=''] If you cannot see the **Legend Item**, make sure the **Search for**option is set to**Any Element**.
24. Clone the created Legend Item and rename the new items accordingly:
  1. 25%-50%
  2. 50%-75%
  3. 75%-100%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1361225457

Model</strong> &gt; <strong>Not</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="417" ac:width="553"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2021-12-31_10-17-54.png" /></ac:image></span></li><li><span Model</strong> &gt; <strong>isEmpty</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="423" ac:width="480"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2021-12-31_10-23-48.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></li><li>Select <strong>Input</strong> &gt; <strong>Simple Navigation</strong>. Select <strong>Satisfied By</strong> and set <strong>Is Applied</strong> to <strong>true</strong>, and <strong>Direction</strong> to <strong>Source To Target</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="222" ac:width="814"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2021-12-31_10-30-59.png" /></ac:image></li><li>Select <strong>All Leaf Requirements Owned Recursively</strong> &gt; <strong>Use as...</strong> &gt; <strong>arg of a new Script</strong>.</li><li>From the <strong>Language </strong>drop-down list, select <strong>Javascript Rhino</strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Insert the following script as the <strong>B</strong><strong>ody</strong>:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b73665b4-b53a-4bea-bddc-c25ba66e91d7"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">js</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">JavaScript Rhino</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[var lowerBound = arg3.name.slice(0,arg3.name.indexOf("%"))/100 
````html
var upperBound = arg3.name.slice(arg3.name.indexOf("-")+1,arg3.name.lastIndexOf("%"))/100 

arg2.size()/arg1.size() > lowerBound && arg2.size()/arg1.size() <= upperBound]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="366" ac:width="944"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2021-12-31_12-1-59.png" /></ac:image></p></li><li>Drag-and-drop <strong>Filter</strong> onto <strong>Create parameter</strong> to create a second parameter for the <strong>Script</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="131" ac:width="250"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2021-12-31_12-6-19.png" /></ac:image></li><li>Select <strong>Filter</strong> &gt; <strong>Remove</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="147" ac:width="571"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2021-12-31_12-7-26.png" /></ac:image></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Select <strong>Create parameter</strong> &gt; <strong>Element</strong> &gt; select the <strong>Legend Item</strong> for which the expression is defined.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="226" ac:width="369"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2021-12-31_12-8-4.png" /></ac:image><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="225" ac:width="571"><ri:attachment cannot see the <strong>Legend Item</strong>, make sure the <strong>Search for </strong>option is set to<strong> Any Element</strong>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Clone the created <strong>Legend Item</strong> and rename the new items accordingly:<ol><li>25%-50%</li><li>50%-75%</li><li>75%-100%</li></ol></li></ol><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189143033 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
````
## PAGE 00139: Case 15.1. All Leaf Requirements Owned Recursively

- page_id: `189143033`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189143033/Case+15.1.+All+Leaf+Requirements+Owned+Recursively
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:39:39.270+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Specifying criteria for querying model > Case Studies for Querying the Model > Case 15. Requirements Coverage by Design Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1361231412

1361231432

1361231422

To collect all Leaf Requirements Owned Recursively

1. Create an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opaque Behavior' space=''] named 'All Leaf Requirements Owned Recursively'.
2. Right-click the Opaque Behavior element/symbol and open its Specification window . Do one of the following:
  1. Click the plus sign next to the **Owned Parameter** property to edit it. If you cannot see the****Owned Parameter****property, make sure the **Expert** mode is enabled.
  2. On the left side menu, select Parameters > Create .
3. In the Specification of Parameter window , set Name to input , Type to Class , and Direction to in . Click Back .
4. In the Opaque Behavior Specification window , click three dots [IMAGE alt='' src=''] next to the **Body and Language** property to edit it.
5. In the Body and Language dialog, select Language > StructuredExpression .
6. Click **Create Operation** > **Filter**. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
7. Select Input > Implied Relation . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2021-12-31_9-24-4.png']
8. Select Element and set the Value to input . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2021-12-31_9-24-25.png']
9. Select Criteria > Body > Reset . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2021-12-31_9-25-33.png']
10. Select Criteria > Body > Contextual Variable . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2021-12-31_9-26-13.png']
11. Select Source > Body > Simple Navigation > Clear All . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2021-12-31_9-28-17.png']
12. Select Source > Body > Simple Navigation . Select Owned Element and set Is Applied to true , Direction to Source To Target , and Result Type to Requirement (select Include Subtypes check box). [ATTACHMENT filename='image2021-12-31_9-29-7.png']
13. Select Target > Reset . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2021-12-31_9-29-57.png']
14. Select Predicate > Nested Operation . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2021-12-31_9-31-13.png']
15. Select **Body** > **Operation from Model** > **isEmpty**. If you cannot see the **isEmpty**operation, make sure the **Apply Filter** box is not checked. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
16. Select Input > Simple Navigation . Select Owned Element and set Is Applied to true , Direction to Source To Target , and Result Type to Requirement . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2021-12-31_9-32-28.png']
17. Click OK .

1361231410

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Case Studies for Querying the Model' space='']true

## PAGE 00140: Case 16. Using the Opposite Reference to find element usages as Tag Values

- page_id: `189143047`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189143047/Case+16.+Using+the+Opposite+Reference+to+find+element+usages+as+Tag+Values
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:39:39.516+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Specifying criteria for querying model > Case Studies for Querying the Model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

33975345

33975365

33975355

To use the Opposite Reference to find element usages as Tag Values

1. Create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generic table' space=''] .
2. Set the Element Type to the element types whose usages you want to find (e.g., Block, Part Property, etc.).
3. Set the Scope where the element usages will be searched for.
4. Click the Columns button and select New Custom Column . The Expression dialog opens.
5. Set the Name for the Custom Column (e.g., usages as Tagged Values ).
6. Select Metachain Navigation .
7. Click **Insert**.
8. Under Metaclass or Stereotype , select Element , and under Property , select _elementTaggedValue .
9. Click Insert again.
10. Under Metaclass or Stereotype , select ElementTaggedValue , and under Property , select Tagged Value Owner .
11. Click **OK**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

33975344

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Case Studies for Querying the Model' space='']true

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ea6cb086-a017-41fb-a8b8-b3f6eddd0c71"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">33975345</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="56e07331-a3ff-4860-90a7-5f403b204e55"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">33975365</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bc6c4be9-3247-47ab-b544-df01017dfe7b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">33975355</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>To use the Opposite Reference to find element usages as Tag Values</p><hr /><ol><li>Create a <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Generic table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Generic Table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>.</li><li>Set the <strong>Element Type </strong>to the element types whose usages you want to find (e.g., Block, Part Property, etc.).</li><li>Set the <strong>Scope </strong>where the element usages will be searched for.</li><li>Click the <strong>Columns</strong> button and select <strong>New Custom Column</strong>. The <strong>Expression</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Set the <strong>Name</strong> for the Custom Column (e.g., <em>usages as Tagged Values</em>).</li><li>Select <strong>Metachain Navigation</strong>.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click <strong>Insert</strong>.</span></li><li>Under <strong>Metaclass or Stereotype</strong>, select <strong>Element</strong>, and under<strong> Property</strong>, select<strong> _elementTaggedValue</strong>.</li><li>Click <strong>Insert </strong>again.</li><li>Under <strong>Metaclass or Stereotype</strong>, select <strong>ElementTaggedValue</strong>, and under<strong> Property</strong>, select<strong> Tagged Value Owner</strong>.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Using_the_Opposite_Reference_to_find_element_usages_as_Tag_Values.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3599ca08-9c66-4bb9-bff1-126c83e14c4c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">33975344</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt-include" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0420c8d1-e63b-4794-890f-58e01d55d45d"><ac:parameter ac:name=""><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Case Studies for Querying the Model" /></ac:link></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="nopanel">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189143060 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00141: Case 2. Finding Element Usages as Types

- page_id: `189143060`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189143060/Case+2.+Finding+Element+Usages+as+Types

- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

956364775

956364786

956364776

To create a Derived Property to find blocks used as types of a Part Property

1. Right-click a Block to open its **Specification window**.
2. On the left side menu, select **Traceability** > **Create**. **Expression** dialog opens. If you cannot see the **Traceability** node, make sure the **Expert** or **All**mode is enabled. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Select Metachain Navigation.
4. Click Insert .
5. Under Metaclass or Stereotype , select Block, and under Property , select _typedElementOfType .
6. Click Insert again.
7. Under Metaclass or Stereotype , select Part Property , and under Property , select Owner . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2020-12-31_14-12-7.png']
8. Click OK .

956364774

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Case Studies for Querying the Model' space='']true

## PAGE 00142: Case 3. Requirements from the Owning Package of a Smart Package

- page_id: `189143065`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189143065/Case+3.+Requirements+from+the+Owning+Package+of+a+Smart+Package
- version_number: 1

- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Specifying criteria for querying model > Case Studies for Querying the Model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

956399738

956399751

956399741

To create a Smart Package that collects requirements from its owning package

1. Create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating smart packages' space=''] .
2. Open its Specification window and click three dots [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] next to the Query property to edit it. Query dialog opens.
3. On the left side of the dialog, click Simple Navigation .
4. Select Owner and set Is Applied to true .
5. Click the **Use as...**button and select the**Scope of a new Find**command. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
6. Select Find and set the Type to Requirement . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2021-12-28_11-42-4.png']
7. Click OK .

956399737

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Case Studies for Querying the Model' space='']true

## PAGE 00143: Case 4. Properties that Satisfy System Requirements

- page_id: `189143075`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189143075/Case+4.+Properties+that+Satisfy+System+Requirements

- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Specifying criteria for querying model > Case Studies for Querying the Model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

956430184

956430195

956430185

To create a Generic Table showing only the properties that satisfy system requirements

1. Create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Generic Table' space=''] .
2. Set the Element Type to Property . Make sure to select the Include Custom Types check box. Click OK .
3. For the Scope , click the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] icon****and s elect **Custom** . Query dialog opens.
4. On the left side of the dialog, click Find .
5. Set the Type to Requirement . Make sure to select the Include Subtypes check box. Click OK .
6. Select Metachain Navigation > Context and drag-and-drop the previously created Find on the Context parameter. [ATTACHMENT filename='image2021-12-28_11-47-18.png']
7. Select Metachain Navigation .
8. Click Insert .
9. Under Metaclass or Stereotype , select Requirement, and under Property , select Supplier Dependency .
10. Click Insert again.
11. Under Metaclass or Stereotype , select Satisfy , and under Property , select Client . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2021-12-28_11-48-40.png']
12. Click OK .

956430183

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Case Studies for Querying the Model' space='']true

Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li>Select <strong>Metachain Navigation</strong> &gt; <strong>Context </strong>and drag-and-drop the previously created <strong>Find</strong> on the <strong>Context</strong> parameter.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2021-12-28_11-47-18.png" /></ac:image></li><li>Select <strong>Metachain Navigation</strong>.</li><li>Click <strong>Insert</strong>. </li><li>Under <strong>Metaclass or Stereotype</strong>, select <strong>Requirement,</strong> and under<strong> Property</strong>, select <strong>Supplier Dependency</strong>.</li><li>Click <strong>Insert</strong> again. </li><li>Under <strong>Metaclass or Stereotype</strong>, select <strong>Satisfy</strong>, and under<strong> Property</strong>, select <strong>Client</strong>. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2021-12-28_11-48-40.png" /></ac:image></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br /><br /></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bcda613b-483e-461b-a10f-c9d7d4674aae"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">956430183</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt-include" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="203b4a95-74a3-49aa-a9cb-f102f5b0c687"><ac:parameter ac:name=""><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Case Studies for Querying the Model" /></ac:link></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="nopanel">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189143082 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00144: Case 5. Validating Elements Not Used in Diagram

- page_id: `189143082`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189143082/Case+5.+Validating+Elements+Not+Used+in+Diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:39:42.373+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Specifying criteria for querying model > Case Studies for Querying the Model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

956458043

956458056

956458046

To validate elements that are not used in a diagram

1. Create a Package and apply the «ValidationSuite» stereotype.
2. Right-click the Package to open its Specification window .
3. On the left side menu, select Constraints > Create .
4. Click three dots [IMAGE alt='' src=''] next to the **Applied Stereotype** property and set it to **«validationRule»**. Click **Apply**. If you cannot see the **Traceability** node, make sure the **Expert** or **All**mode is enabled.
5. Click three dots [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] next to the Severity , Error Message , and Abbreviation properties to specify them for the created validation rule.
6. Click three dots [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] next to the Constrained E lement property to specify the metaclasses and stereotypes whose instances are validated if not used in the diagram.
7. Click three dots [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] next to the Specification property. Specification dialog opens.
8. From the**Language**drop-down list, select **StructuredExpression**.
9. On the left side of the dialog, click **Create Operation**>****Operation from Model****>**Not.** If you cannot see the **Not**operation, make sure the **Apply Filter** box is not checked.
10. Select A > Reset .
11. Select **A** > **Operation from Model** > **IsEmpty**.
12. Select **Input** > **Operation from Model** > **UsageInDiagramsWithSymbols**.
13. Select Element > Reset .
14. Select Element > Contextual Variable . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2021-1-26_9-40-39.png']
15. Click OK .

956458042

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Case Studies for Querying the Model' space='']true

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189143086 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00145: Case 6. Requirements Without Text

- page_id: `189143086`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189143086/Case+6.+Requirements+Without+Text
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:39:42.573+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Specifying criteria for querying model > Case Studies for Querying the Model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Groovy

1476016285

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Groovy

1476016306

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Groovy

1476016295

To find Requirements without text

1. Create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating smart packages' space=''] .
2. Open its Specification window and click three dots [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] next to the Query property to edit it. The Query dialog opens.
3. Click **Create operation** > **Filter**. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. Select Input > Find . [ATTACHMENT filename='2_input_param_set_to_find.png']
5. Select Find and set the Type to Requirement . [ATTACHMENT filename='3_type_requirement.png']
6. Select **Predicate**>******Operation from Model**>****IfThenElse****. If you cannot see the **IfThenElse** operation, make sure the **Apply Filter** box is not checked. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
7. Select **Condition** > **Reset**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
8. Select Condition > **Operation from Model** > ****isEmpty**** . [ATTACHMENT filename='8_set_isEmpty.png']
9. Select Input > **Metachain Navigation** . [ATTACHMENT filename='9_set_metachain_navigation_as_input.png']
10. Click Insert .
11. Under Metaclass or Stereotype , select Requirement, and under Property , select Text . 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
12. Select Then > Boolean. Set the Value to true . [ATTACHMENT filename='12_set_value_to_true.png']
13. Select Else > **Script** . [ATTACHMENT filename='13_set_script_for_else.png']
14. Select the Contextual Variable arg1 > Reset .
15. Select the Contextual Variable arg1 > Metachain Navigation . [ATTACHMENT filename='14_set_variable_to_metachain_nav.png']
16. Set Parameter Name to "text".
17. Click Insert .
18. Under Metaclass or Stereotype , select Requirement , and under Property , select Text . 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
19. From the Language drop-down list, select Groovy .
20. Insert the following script as the **B****ody**: groovyGroovy0 ? text.iterator().next().trim().length() == 0 : false ; ]]> [IMAGE alt='' src='']
21. Click **OK**. You can view the requirements without text nested under the **Smart Package** in the Containment tree. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189143108 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00146: Case 7. Sum of Default Values of Recursively Collected Properties Redefined by Specific Property

- page_id: `189143108`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189143108/Case+7.+Sum+of+Default+Values+of+Recursively+Collected+Properties+Redefined+by+Specific+Property
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:39:42.877+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Specifying criteria for querying model > Case Studies for Querying the Model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1694275867

1694275888

1694275877

Prerequisites:

To get the sum of Default Values of Recursively Collected Properties Redefined by Specific Property

1. Create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generic table' space=''] .
2. Set the Element Type to Block and Property (e.g. Value Property, Part Property, etc.) to be displayed.
3. Set the Scope to the Block element which contains the Properties.
4. Click the Columns button and select New Custom Column . Expression dialog opens.
5. Set the Type as Real .
6. Select **Create operation** > **Operation from Model** > [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Case 7.1. Sum of Default Values of Property' space='']. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
7. Select Property > Operation from Model > [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Case 7.2. Filter Properties Redefined by Specific Property' space=''] . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2021-12-29_10-29-15.png']
8. Select Redefines and click three dots [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] next to the Element field to choose a Property that redefines the Properties that need to be collected. [ATTACHMENT filename='image2021-12-29_10-30-16.png']
9. Select Input > Operation from Model > [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Case 7.3. Recursive Structure Decomposition' space=''] . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2021-12-29_10-46-4.png']
10. Select **Element** > **Reset**.
11. Select **Element** > **Contextual Variable**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
12. Click OK .

1694275865

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Case Studies for Querying the Model' space='']true

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189143123 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00147: Case 7.1. Sum of Default Values of Property

- page_id: `189143123`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189143123/Case+7.1.+Sum+of+Default+Values+of+Property
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:39:43.310+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Specifying criteria for querying model > Case Studies for Querying the Model > Case 7. Sum of Default Values of Recursively Collected Properties Redefined by Specific Property
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Javascript Rhino

1694243026

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Javascript Rhino

1694243047

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Javascript Rhino

1694243036

To get the sum of the Default Values of a Property

1. Create an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opaque Behavior' space=''] named 'Sum of Default Values of Property'.
2. Right-click the Opaque Behavior element/symbol and open its Specification window . Do one of the following:
  1. Click the plus sign next to the **Owned Parameter** property to edit it. If you cannot see the****Owned Parameter****property, make sure the **Expert** mode is enabled.
  2. On the left side menu, select Parameters > Create .
3. In the Specification of Parameter window , set Name to property , Type to Property , Direction to in , and Multiplicity to [0..*] . Click Back .
4. In the Opaque Behavior Specification window , click three dots [IMAGE alt='' src=''] next to the **Body and Language** property to edit it.
5. In the **Body and Language** dialog, select **Language**>******StructuredExpression**.
6. Select Metachain Navigation .
7. Click Insert .
8. Under Metaclass or Stereotype , select Property , and under Property , select Default Value .
9. Click **Insert** again.
10. Under Metaclass or Stereotype , select LiteralReal , and under Property , select Value . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2021-12-29_8-40-12.png']
11. Select Metachain Navigation > Context and set Value to property .
12. Select **Create Operation** > **Operation from Model** > ****Reduce****.******** If you cannot see the **Reduce**operation, make sure the **Apply Filter** box is not checked.
13. Drag and drop the created Metachain Navigation onto Input . [IMAGE alt='' src='']
14. Select Reduce Operation > Script .
15. From the Language drop-down list, select Javascript Rhino .
16. Select Create parameter > Parameter .
17. Insert the following script as the**B****ody**for the**Script**: Javascript Rhino [IMAGE alt='' src='']
18. Click **OK**.

1694243024

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Case Studies for Querying the Model' space='']true

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189143128 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00148: Case 7.2. Filter Properties Redefined by Specific Property

- page_id: `189143128`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189143128/Case+7.2.+Filter+Properties+Redefined+by+Specific+Property
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:39:43.529+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Specifying criteria for querying model > Case Studies for Querying the Model > Case 7. Sum of Default Values of Recursively Collected Properties Redefined by Specific Property
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1694254134

1694254145

1694254135

To filter Properties Redefined by a Specific Property

1. Create an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opaque Behavior' space=''] named 'Filter Properties Redefined by Specific Property'.
2. Right-click the Opaque Behavior element/symbol and open its Specification window . Do one of the following:
  1. Click the plus sign next to the **Owned Parameter** property to edit it. If you cannot see the****Owned Parameter****property, make sure the **Expert** mode is enabled.
  2. On the left side menu, select Parameters > Create .
3. In the Specification of Parameter window , set Name to input , Type to Element , Direction to in , and Multiplicity to [1..*] . Click Back .
4. Create a second parameter.
5. Set Name to redefines , Type to Property , Direction to in , and Multiplicity to [1] . Click Close .
6. In the Opaque Behavior Specification window , click three dots [IMAGE alt='' src=''] next to the **Body and Language** property to edit it.
7. In the Body and Language dialog, select Language > StructuredExpression .
8. Click **Create Operation** > **Filter**.
9. Select Input > Contextual Variable and set the Value to input . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2021-12-28_14-47-40.png']
10. Select Predicate > Operation from Model > Contains . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2021-12-28_14-50-1.png']
11. Select **Body** > **Input** > **Metachain Navigation.**
12. Click**Insert**.
13. Under **Metaclass or Stereotype**, select **Property**, and under **Property**, select **Redefined Property**. [ATTACHMENT filename='image2021-12-28_14-49-26.png']
14. Select **Obj** > **Contextual Value** > **redefines**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
15. Click OK .

1694254133

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Case Studies for Querying the Model' space='']true

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189143134 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00149: Case 7.3. Recursive Structure Decomposition

- page_id: `189143134`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189143134/Case+7.3.+Recursive+Structure+Decomposition
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:39:43.760+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Specifying criteria for querying model > Case Studies for Querying the Model > Case 7. Sum of Default Values of Recursively Collected Properties Redefined by Specific Property
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1694265108

1694265119

1694265118

To create Recursive Structure Decomposition

1. Create an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opaque Behavior' space=''] named 'Recursive Structure Decomposition'.
2. Right-click the Opaque Behavior element/symbol and open its Specification window . Do one of the following:
  1. Click the plus sign next to the **Owned Parameter** property to edit it. If you cannot see the****Owned Parameter****property, make sure the **Expert** mode is enabled.
  2. On the left side menu, select Parameters > Create .
3. In the Specification of Parameter window , set Name to element , Type to Element , Direction to in , and Multiplicity to [1] . Click Back .
4. In the Opaque Behavior Specification window , click three dots [IMAGE alt='' src='']next to the **Body and Language** property to edit it.
5. In the Body and Language dialog, select Language > StructuredExpression .
6. Select Create operation > Implied Relation .
7. Select the Contextual Variable Element and set the Value to element . [ATTACHMENT filename='1_contextual_var_to_element.png']
8. Reset Criteria , Source , and Target parameter values. [ATTACHMENT filename='2_reset_criteria_source_target.png']
9. Select Criteria > Nested Operation . [IMAGE alt='' src='']
10. Select Criteria > Body > Contextual Variable . [ATTACHMENT filename='4_criteria_body_contextual_var.png']
11. Select Source > Operation from Model > Structure Decomposition . [ATTACHMENT filename='5_source_structure_decomposition.png']
12. Select Source > Body > Element > Reset . [ATTACHMENT filename='6_source_body_element.png']
13. Select Source > Body > Element > Contextual Variable . [ATTACHMENT filename='7_source_body_element_contextual_var.png']
14. Click OK .

1694265107

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Case Studies for Querying the Model' space='']true

## PAGE 00150: Case 8. Showing Parameter Direction and Type in Single Table Cell using StringConcat

- page_id: `189143148`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189143148/Case+8.+Showing+Parameter+Direction+and+Type+in+Single+Table+Cell+using+StringConcat
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:39:44.044+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Specifying criteria for querying model > Case Studies for Querying the Model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Javascript Rhino

1724974424

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Javascript Rhino

1724974444

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Javascript Rhino

1724974434

To display Parameter Direction and Parameter Type in a single table cell

1. Create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generic table' space=''] .
2. Set the Element Type to Parameter and Activity .
3. Set the Scope to the Activity element which contains the Parameters.
4. Click the Columns button and select New Custom Column . Expression dialog opens.
5. Set the Type as String .
6. Select the Single Value check box. [ATTACHMENT filename='1_type_string_single_value.png']
7. Select **Create operation** > **Operation from Model** > **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Case 7.1. Sum of Default Values of Property' space='']fThenElse**. If you cannot see the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Case 7.1. Sum of Default Values of Property' space='']fThenElse**operation, make sure the **Apply Filter** box is not checked. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
8. Select Condition > Reset . [ATTACHMENT filename='3_condition_reset.png']
9. Select Condition > Type Test . [ATTACHMENT filename='4_condition_type_test.png']
10. Select Condition and set the Type to Parameter . [ATTACHMENT filename='5_condition_type_parameter.png']
11. Select Then > Operation from Model > StringConcat . [ATTACHMENT filename='6_then_operation_from_model_stringconcat.png']
12. Select A > Reset .
13. Select A > Script . [ATTACHMENT filename='7_a_script.png']
14. Select A > arg1 > Remove . [ATTACHMENT filename='8_a_arg1_remove.png']
15. Select A > Create parameter > Metachain Navigation . [ATTACHMENT filename='9_a_create_parameter_metachain_nav.png']
16. Set the Parameter Name to direction .
17. Click Insert .
18. Under Metaclass or Stereotype , select Parameter, and under Property , select Direction . [ATTACHMENT filename='10_direction_parameter_direction.png']
19. Select A > Script . From the Language drop-down list, select Javascript Rhino .
20. Insert the following script as the **B****ody**: jsJavascript Rhino0 ? direction.iterator().next().name + " : " : " : " ;]]> [IMAGE alt='' src='']
21. Select B > Reset .
22. Select B > Metachain Navigation . [ATTACHMENT filename='12_b_metachain_nav.png']
23. Click Insert .
24. Under Metaclass or Stereotype , select Parameter, and under Property , select Type .
25. Click Insert again.
26. Under Metaclass or Stereotype , select Type, and under Property , select Name . [ATTACHMENT filename='13_b_paramter_type_type_name.png']
27. Click OK .

1724974422

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Case Studies for Querying the Model' space='']true

## PAGE 00151: Case 9. Requirements Derivation and Satisfaction using Table Hierarchy

- page_id: `189143164`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189143164/Case+9.+Requirements+Derivation+and+Satisfaction+using+Table+Hierarchy
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:39:44.344+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Specifying criteria for querying model > Case Studies for Querying the Model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1660006747

1660006768

1660006758

To perform Requirements Derivation and Satisfaction using the Table Hierarchy

1. Create a **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Generic Table' space='']**.
2. Set the Element Type to Call Behavior Action , Property , Requirement , State .
3. Set the Scope to the Requirements that will be displayed in the Table.
4. Open the Specification window of the Generic Table and click three dots [IMAGE alt='' src='']next to the Query property to edit it.
5. In the **Query** dialog, select **Custom**.
6. Select Simple Navigation and set:
  - DeriveReqt : Is Applied: **True** Direction: Target to Source
  - Satisfy : Is Applied: **True** Direction: Target to Source
  - Owned Element : Is Applied: **True** Direction: Source to Target
7. Select the Build Hierarchy check box to recursively execute the specified expression for building a multilevel hierarchy starting from the scope as the root. [ATTACHMENT filename='image2021-12-29_14-2-29.png']
8. Click OK .

1660006746

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Case Studies for Querying the Model' space='']true

##### CaseStudy1Case Study 1 - Modeling user interface for the Report Wizard dialog

This case study provides step-by-step instructions for modeling the **Report Wizard** dialog. If you are already familiar with this case study, please review , or

**Step #1. Create a new Project**

1. Choose New Project from the File menu.
2. Name it Report Wizard .

**Step #2. Create a new Diagram**

1. Click Create Diagram and select User Interface Modeling Diagram .
2. Name the new diagram Report Wizard .

**Step #3. Create Container components**

1. Click the Frame button under the Containers toolbar and drag and drop it on the diagram pane. A Frame component is created.
2. Name the Frame component by doing one of the following:
  - Click the Frame shape on the diagram pane and type Report Wizard.
  - Open the Frame's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] .
  1. 
    - Double-click the Frame shape on the diagram pane.
    - In the opened specification window, type Report Wizard in the Title field.
3. Clear the check box properties Maximize and Minimize in the Frame's Specification window.
4. Define an Icon for the Frame symbol:
  1. In the Frame's Specification window, select the Custom option in the Icon property. The Open dialog box opens.
  2. Select an image from your file system and click Open .
5. Under the Containers toolbar, drag and drop two Group Box elements onto the Frame symbol. Under the Other toolbar, drag and drop one Separator element onto the Frame symbol.
  1. Place one Group Box into the other Group Box. Double-click on the larger Group Box and ensure Titled is set to true . In the Title field type Select Template.
  2. Open the specification window of the smaller Group Box and clear the check box property Titled .
  3. Arrange everything appropriately.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Report Wizard Frame with two Group Boxes and one Separator

**Step #4. Create a Tree**

1. Create a new Tree component:
  1. Under the Other toolbar, drag and drop the Tree element onto the Select Template Group Box.
2. Delete old nodes from the Tree component.
3. Create new nodes in the Tree component.
  1. In the Containment tree, right-click on the Tree to invoke its shortcut menu and choose Create Element > Node .
  2. Create five new Nodes in this way and name them as shown below.
4. Add Leaves to Nodes.
  1. You can add a Leaf by right-clicking on a Node in the containment tree and then choosing Create Element > Leaf .
  2. Add at least one Leaf to every Node to indicate that the nodes have internal elements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Report Wizard Window with Added Tree

**Step #5. Add Buttons**

1. Create predefined text buttons.
  1. Under the Buttons toolbar, expand the OK Button group.
  2. Right-click the Back button to expand the text buttons group. Create Back , Next, and Cancel buttons. Guide yourself according to the figure below.
2. Create regular buttons.
  1. Under the Buttons toolbar, drag and drop the Button symbol onto the diagram pane inside the Frame, next to the Select Template Group Box.
  2. Select the created button in the diagram pane and type in New.
  3. Create the remaining buttons by repeating steps 2.a and 2.b. Refer to the figure below.
3. Double-click on the other buttons to invoke their specification window, excluding the Next > , Cancel , New, and Import buttons. Clear the check box Inactive to true.
4. It should look like the figure shown below.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Sample of the Report Wizard window

**Step #6. Using the Report Wizard window**

This User Interface model can now be exported as an image. The steps to do this are as follows:

1. Select Save As Image from the File menu.
2. In the Save As Image dialog, select Active Diagram .
3. In Image File define the location where the image should be placed.
4. Select Joint Photographic Experts Group (.jpg) (or any format you would like) in Image Format and then click Save.

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Notation Formality

982345345897

INLINE

##### CaseStudy2Case Study 2 - Slider Example

This case study contains step-by-step instructions for creating a User Interface model with Sliders. It also shows how to customize the symbol properties of User Interface components. However, it does **not** explain how to create a new project and a new diagram. Please refer to "" for those instructions. If you are already familiar with these case studies, please review

**Step #1 Create a Frame, Labels and Sliders**

1. Create a new Frame .
  1. Under the Containers toolbar, click the Frame button.
  2. Drop it on the diagram pane.
  3. Click on your newly created frame and title Symbol Properties Customization.
2. Add a new Label to the Frame.
  1. Under the Tools toolbar, click the Label button. Place the label onto the frame.
  2. Click the label and change the name to Fill Color.
  3. Create the remaining two labels and name them Text Color and Font Name .
3. Add Sliders to the Frame.
  1. Under the Other toolbar, click the Slider button. Place the slider onto the frame.
  2. Double-click on the Slider to invoke its specification window.
  3. In the opened window, set the Spacing property value to 50.
  4. Set the Knob Position property value to 0 in order to move the knob to the left position.
  5. In the Slider's specification window, select the Values property and click the plus sign button. A Values dialog window opens. If there are values already defined in the window, click the minus sign button to delete them.
  6. To add new values, click the plus sign button and then type 0 Red . 0 Red must be written in a specific way. First, type in 0 and press Enter . Type Red on the next line. Rename the other two given values to 50 Blue and 100 White using the same procedure.
  7. Draw two more sliders and repeat steps 3a - 3f until your frame looks like the one shown in the figure below.

#### NOTE: Notation Formality

Notation Formality

When setting the values of a slider it is important to note that there is no empty space between *0*. For example, *0* represents a new line. When entering a value, it should look like this:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Symbol Properties Customization Frame with Added Labels and Sliders

**Step #2 Add Text Fields**

1. Create Text Fields.
  1. Under the Text toolbar. click the Text Field button. Drop it onto the frame.
  2. Name the text field Red Background .
  3. Repeat the previous two steps for the remaining two text fields, as shown in the figure below.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Added Text Fields to the Frame

**Step #3 Edit Symbol Properties for the Text Fields**

1. Edit Symbol Properties for the first text field.
  1. Select the first text field on the Frame shape.
  2. Right-click it and select Symbol Properties from its shortcut menu.
  3. Set the Use Fill Color property to true and change the Fill Color property value to red. Change the fill color by clicking the ... to the right of the field, and in the Color dialog, click RGB and type in FF0000 in the Color Code field.
2. Edit Symbol Properties for the remaining fields.
  1. Follow steps 1a - 1b for the next steps.
  2. For the second text field, select yellow in Text Color .
  3. For the third one, select the font name Tahoma in Font , as shown in the figure below

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Finalized Slider Example

458973459054

INLINE

##### CaseStudy3Case Study 3 - User Interface Prototyping Example

This case study demonstrates how to connect several user interface models and create a browsable report. The models should represent a test application with a Login Dialog, a Test Browser and a test with several questions. Here are step-by-step instructions on how to add hyperlinks and create browsable reports. If you are already familiar with this case study, please review, or

**Step #1 Create the first Model**

1. Create a new Package for the model.
2. Create a model similar to the one shown below.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']First Model of Prototyping Example - Login Dialog

**Step #2 Create the second Model**

1. Create another new Package for this model.
2. Create a model similar to the one depicted below.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Second Model of Prototyping Example - Test Browser

**Step #3 Create remaining Models**

1. Create a separate Package for each model.
2. You can build models similar to the ones shown in the detailed samples mentioned in the beginning of this chapter, or create your own in a similar manner.

**Step #4 Add Hyperlinks**

1. Add a hyperlink.
  1. Open the model with the Login Dialog.
  2. Open the Specification window of the **OK** button.
  3. Next to the Active Hyperlink property field, click the ... button. The Edit Hyperlink dialog opens.
  4. In the Edit Hyperlink dialog, select Element/Symbol and click the "..." button. Browse to the Package Test Browser , select the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='User Interface Modeling diagram' space=''] in it and confirm two times with OK - a diagram symbol will appear next to the OK button. Double-clicking the OK button will lead to the other diagram.
2. Now connect all the buttons in the other diagrams with hyperlinks. The diagram symbol appears next to a component with a hyperlink.

**Step #5 Create a browsable Report**

1. Create a new report.
  1. Select **Report Wizard** from the **Tools** menu.
  2. Expand the **Default Template** in the tree and select **Web Publisher 2.0**.
  3. Confirm three times by clicking **Next** until the dialog allows you to add data to the report. Add all Packages that contain the created diagrams and click **Next**.
  4. Give the **Report file** a name to the output file. Clear the check box **Display in viewer after generating report**.
  5. Click **Generate**. The report is built and shown in your default browser.

433458345987534

INLINE
