Validation Rules</strong>.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bb2ebccd-661c-4b44-ab39-7224077841f1"><ac:rich-text-body>The validation will still be triggered during code generation. If you want to hide the validation results at this point, set the <strong>Show Validation Report</strong> property to <em>false</em> in the <strong>Project Options</strong> &gt; <strong>CATIA Software Producer Client</strong> (see <ac:link ac:anchor="Validation Results" />.). </ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="validation_project_options.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5adc8d88-6d27-49d7-9c2e-7a72de6cd363"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Validation is actually a SPO (Software Production Operation) launched in dry-run mode (no file generation, no credit consumption).</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="margin-left: 0.0in;"><br /></p><h2>Executing the code generator</h2><p>To execute the code generator, you can run the generation from the following locations:</p><ul><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Main menu</span></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Code generation diagram</span></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Containment tree</span></li></ul><h3><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Execution from the main menu</span></h3><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="execution_main_menu.png" /></ac:image></span></p><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">You can do one of the following:</span></p><ul><li>launch the default action <strong>Execute Code Generation</strong> that will launch the operations<ul><li>if <strong>Activate Smart Launch Mode</strong> is set to true in the environment options (<strong>Environment </strong>options dialog &gt; <strong>CATIA Software Producer Client General Properties</strong>), operations will be launched only if a change was done in the model. By default, the property is set to false.</li></ul></li></ul><ul><li>launch the <strong>Execute Clean &amp; Code Generation</strong> that will delete your previously generated local files and cloud assets then launch all the operations.<ul><li>for the deletion of the local files: if files previously generated by this configuration are found, a dialog will be prompted to the user to confirm the deletion of local files. <br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2531679e-8cff-4972-9d05-2b9de4156812"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you do not want to see this message again,  cancel the selection of the <strong>Show this message next time</strong> check box. If you want to see this message again, there is a dedicated Environment option to turn the message showing on (see <ac:link ac:anchor="Additional Information" />).</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="delete_local_folders.png" /></ac:image></li><li>for the deletion of cloud assets: it will be done without asking for the confirmation of the user. </li></ul></li></ul><h3>Exercution from a code generation diagram</h3><p>Do one of the following:</p><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">On the diagram pane, right-click a configuration element, and from the shortcut menu, choose <strong>Software Code Generation</strong> &gt; <strong>Execute Code Generation</strong>:<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="execution_from_diagram.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">On the diagram pane, select a configuration element, and in the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Execute Selected Configuration</strong>:<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="execution_from_diagram_toolbar.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></li></ul><h4>Execution from Containment tree</h4><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">In the Containment tree, right-click a configuration element, and from the shortcut menu, select <strong>Software Code Generation</strong> &gt; <strong>Execute Code Generation</strong>:<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="execute_config_containment_tree.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">In the Containment tree, right-click an input element, and from the shortcut menu, select <strong>Software Code Generation</strong> &gt; <strong>Execute with...</strong>:<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="execute_containment_tree_imput_element.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></li></ul><p>The operation logs are displayed in the<strong> Software Producer Logs</strong> window.</p><h2>Software Governance</h2><p style="text-align: left;"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">To commit elements from CATIA Magic, you need to:</span></p><ul style="text-align: left;"><li>Associate a SCM repository to the model element<ul><li>Using Connected Software Connectors</li><li>With a local configuration</li></ul></li><li>Create an SCM Configuration element</li><li>Configure an SCM branch<ul><li>Using Software Logical Items of Connected Software </li><li>With a local configuration</li></ul></li></ul><h3 style="text-align: left;">SCM Repository association</h3><h4 style="text-align: left;">Associating repository using Connected Software Connectors</h4><p style="text-align: left;">To associate the SCM repository</p><hr /><ol><li style="text-align: left;">In the Containment tree, right-click Model.</li><li style="text-align: left;">In the shortcut menu, click <strong>Software Code Generation</strong> &gt; <strong>Associate SCM Repository</strong>.</li><li style="text-align: left;">In the open <strong>Associate SCM Repository</strong> dialog, choose the needed repository,</li><li style="text-align: left;">Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p style="text-align: left;"><span class="image-wrap"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image-2024-05-07-15-31-16-554.png" /></ac:image></span><span class="image-wrap"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image-2024-05-07-16-22-46-874.png" /></ac:image></span></p><p style="text-align: left;">After you click <strong>OK</strong> in the <strong>Associate SCM Repository</strong> dialog, the repository information will be associated to the model via the «Connector» stereotype from the Embedded Software Profile:</p><ul style="text-align: left;"><li>repositoryHost: root url of the targeted Git server (ex: :<span> </span><a class="external-link" href="https://gitlab.com)/" title="Follow link">https://gitlab.com)</a></li><li>repositoryPath: relative path of the repository (ex: SJA/cubesat-control)</li><li>repositoryToken: associate the access token to the repository (example: glpat-6_y_4y8hMDNDxWyaATi4)</li></ul><p style="text-align: left;"><br /></p><p style="text-align: left;">To remove the association of the SCM repository</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Containment tree, right-click Model.</li><li>In the shortcut menu, click <strong>Software Code Generation</strong> &gt; <strong>Associate SCM Repository</strong>.</li><li>In the open <strong>Associate SCM Repository</strong> dialog, click the selected repository for which you want to remove the association.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p style="text-align: left;">When you click <strong>OK</strong>, the association will be removed and the branches will be detached from elements if there were any. </p><h4 style="text-align: left;">Local Configuration</h4><p style="text-align: left;">If Connected Software is not used, it is still possible to attach a repository to the model element.</p><p style="text-align: left;">To do so, associate the «Connector» stereotype from Embedded Software Profile to the model element and configure it as following:</p><ul style="text-align: left;"><li>repositoryHost: root url of the targeted Git server (ex: :<span> </span><a class="external-link" href="https://gitlab.com)/" title="Follow link">https://gitlab.com)</a></li><li>repositoryPath: relative path of the repository (ex: SJA/cubesat-control)</li><li>repositoryToken: associate the access token to the repository (example: glpat-6_y_4y8hMDNDxWyaATi4)</li></ul><h3 style="text-align: left;">SCM Configuration creation</h3><p style="text-align: left;">It is necessary to associate a repository before attaching a branch from the configuration dialog.</p><p style="text-align: left;">From the diagram palette, the you can create a SCM Configuration that will contain <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">a reminder of the repository path of the model: you cannot edit the repository here. If you need to change the repository, see </span><ac:link ac:anchor="SCM Repository association" /><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">.</span></p><p style="text-align: left;"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="scm_cofig_creation.png" /></ac:image></span></p><p style="text-align: left;">To use the SCM Configuration, you have to drag and drop the SCM configuration on the code configuration or application configuration that you wants to execute and commit:</p><p style="text-align: left;"><span class="image-wrap"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image-2024-05-07-16-37-27-308.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h3 style="text-align: left;">SCM Branch Configuration</h3><p style="text-align: left;">This is the branch to which you are going to commit the code.</p><p style="text-align: left;">It is necessary to associate a repository before attaching a branch from the configuration dialog.</p><h4 style="text-align: left;">With Software Logical Items of Connected Software</h4><ul><li><span>you can edit the branch in the <strong>Specification </strong>window by clicking the three dots in the branch value field:</span></li></ul><p style="text-align: left;margin-left: 40.0px;"><span> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image-2024-05-07-16-29-11-710.png" /></ac:image><br /></span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image-2024-05-07-16-33-07-936.png" /></ac:image></p><ul><li>When you click <strong>OK </strong>in the <strong>Associate Branch of Repository</strong> dialog, the branch information will be associated to the configuration via the «SoftwareLogicalItem» stereotype from Embedded Software Profile:</li></ul><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li>branchName: the branch name to target (example: “main”)</li><li>SoftwareLogicalItemId: ID of the logical Item linked to the branch (from Connected Software)</li></ul></li></ul></li></ul><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">If if you remove the repository association, the branches will also be detached from elements if there were any. See </span><ac:link ac:anchor="SCM Repository association" /><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">.</span></p><h4 style="text-align: left;">Local Configuration</h4><p style="text-align: left;">The you can edit it in the branch:</p><ul style="text-align: left;"><li>branchName: the branch name to target (example: “main”)</li></ul><h3 style="text-align: left;">Code persistency</h3><p style="text-align: left;">Code generation is executed via the regular mechanism.</p><p style="text-align: left;"><br /></p><p style="text-align: left;">The code will be committed after a successful execution of code generation.</p><p style="text-align: left;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="persistency.png" /></ac:image></p><p style="text-align: left;">At execution phase, mandatory parameters are checked, if they are not present or are invalid, the operation is canceled and a message is printed (see an example below).</p><p style="text-align: left;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="log.png" /></ac:image></p><p style="text-align: left;">The mandatory parameters are:       </p><ul style="text-align: left;"><li>the repository path: must be present</li><li>the repository token: must be present</li><li>the repository host: must be present and must reference a valid URL (syntax check only)</li><li>the commit message: must be present</li><li>the commit author: must be present</li><li>the branch name: must be present</li></ul><h2>Local workspace of the software producer</h2><p>Under the local workspace, you can find the generated files, if you have selected the download option and left the default location (as seen above) in the configurations. You can also find some other files under an “etc” folder:</p><ul><li>&lt;Local_workspace_path&gt;</li></ul><ul><ul><li>&lt;configuration1_name&gt;</li><li>&lt;configuration2_name&gt;</li><li>etc<ul><li>json<sup>3</sup></li></ul><ul><li>log<sup>4</sup></li></ul></li><ul><li>operations</li><li>backup<sup>2</sup></li><li>logs</li></ul></ul></ul><hr /><p><sup>1</sup>You can change the root of the local workspace path in the project options.</p><p><sup>2</sup>By default, a backup of your downloaded files is saved in this folder so that in case something wrong happens with the download of new files, you can still reach your previous files. You can disable this option in the <strong>Project Options </strong></p><p><sup>3</sup>SPO Report files are rotated by size and date so that you might see multiple report files here.</p><p><sup>4</sup>Log files are rotated by size and date so that you might see multiple log files here.</p><h2>RoundTrip</h2><p>The <strong>roundtrip</strong> action is a feature available for State Machines and Opaque Behaviors. Using the roundtrip action, you can add the modification you made in the code in between BEG_USER_CODE / END_USER_CODE tags (in files commonly named &lt;ComponentName&gt;_usr.c), back into the model and synchronize the file on the workspace on the cloud.</p><p><ac:image ac:height="140"><ri:attachment ri:filename="roundtrip_example.png" /></ac:image></p><p>The roundtrip is available in the configuration diagram toolbar:</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="round_trip_from_toolbar.png" /></ac:image></p><h2>Views</h2><h3>Software Producer Logs</h3><p>In the Software Producer Log panel, you can see the messages of the running SPO.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="software_producer_logs.png" /></ac:image></p><p style="text-align: left;">In the following table, the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">actions to enhance the navigation in this window are explaned:</span></p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th scope="col">Icon</th><th scope="col">Name</th><th scope="col">Description</th></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="scroll_start.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td><p>Scroll to the Start</p></td><td>Scrolls to the tip of the panel.</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="scroll_end.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Scroll to the End</td><td>Scrolls to the bottom of the panel.</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="lock_position.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Lock Position</td><td>By default, when some new logs are printed, the scrollbar goes down. If the user wants to prevent the panel to be scrolled automatically to the bottom, he can click on this button.</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="17"><ri:attachment ri:filename="clear.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Clear</td><td>Clears the panel.</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="17"><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_log_file.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Show Log File</td><td>Opens the file where the logs are written (each project has its own log file).</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="19"><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_timestamp.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Show Timestamp</td><td>Adds a timestamp as a prefix to the messages.</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="17"><ri:attachment ri:filename="additional_messages.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Show Additional Messages</td><td>Shows more messages related to the SOP client.</td></tr><tr><td><br /></td><td>Level combo box</td><td>Shows the messages of the selected level, hide the others. By default, <strong>All levels </strong>is selected to display all types of messages. Other available types are: Info, Error, Warning.</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="17"><ri:attachment ri:filename="search.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Find</td><td>Highlights the search results and navigate to them</td></tr></tbody></table><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">A log file for general Software Producer messages not related to a specific project is stored next to the modeling tool log file.</span></p><p>At the bottom right corner of the modeling tool, in the progress bar, you can see whether the generation is running. After the generation is completed, the notification is displayed in case of failure or always if the verbose mode is activated (<span><strong>Environment Options </strong>&gt; <strong>CATIA Software Producer Client General Properties</strong> &gt; <strong>Notifications</strong> section</span>).</p><h3>Validation Results</h3><p>When an operation produces a report, the plugin reads it and opens it in a <strong>Validation Result</strong> window if any element has a diagnostic associated.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="validation_results_window.png" /></ac:image></p><p>A diagnostic is associated with a rule consisting of a severity, message, and source.</p><p>You can navigate to the source in the Containment tree.  If you need to show more details concerning the rule or open the full report, right-click the appropriate lines of the table.</p><p>You can turn off the <strong>Validation Results</strong> window's opening after the operation produces a report.</p><p><br /></p><p>To turn the validation report off</p><hr /><ol><li>In the modeling tool main menu, click <strong>Options </strong>and select <strong>Project</strong>.</li><li><span>Set the <strong>Show Validation Report </strong>property to <em>false</em>.</span></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>When you execute a configuration, before calling the operation the plugin checks the UML Completeness Constraints. If a mandatory property is missing (“elements” in the example below), a validation warning and a notification will be displayed informing which tag value(s) are missing.</p><p><ac:image ac:width="1553"><ri:attachment ri:filename="validation_results_missing_properties.png" /></ac:image></p><h3>Diagrams and Containment tree</h3><p>The following commands are available from the diagrams toolbar or from the shortcut menu of the supported elements in the Containment tree:</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 101.262%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 11.9235%;" /><col style="width: 88.0765%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><p><strong>Button</strong></p></th><th><p><strong>Description</strong></p></th></tr><tr><th><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="23"><ri:attachment ri:filename="see_in_file.png" /></ac:image>Show in File Explorer</p></div></th><td><p>Open a location in your file system wherein the Software Production Operation results file is stored.</p></td></tr><tr><th><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="22"><ri:attachment ri:filename="roundtrip.png" /></ac:image>Roundtrip</p></div></th><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>The changes made in the generated code are applied in the State Machine diagram or Opaque Behavior.</p></div></td></tr></tbody></table><h2>Code Generation Result Table</h2><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">When the option &quot;storeExecutionSummaryInModel&quot; is set to true in the configuration, the results of the execution of the configuration is saved as an object under the configuration inside the model.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="store_execution_option.png" /></ac:image></span></p><p style="text-align: left;">You can have an overview of all of these execution objects in the <strong>Code Generation Result Table</strong>.</p><p style="text-align: left;"><br /></p><p style="text-align: left;">To create a table</p><hr /><ol><li style="text-align: left;">In the Containment tree, right-click a package.</li><li style="text-align: left;">From the shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Diagram</strong>.</li><li style="text-align: left;">Search for <strong>Code Generation Result Table</strong> and click it.</li></ol><p style="text-align: left;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_code_generation_result_table.png" /></ac:image></p><p style="text-align: left;"><ac:image ac:width="1559"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image-2024-08-23-12-51-12-576.png" /></ac:image></p><p style="text-align: left;">In the Containment tree on the above image, you can see under the configurations that new objects were created:</p><ul style="text-align: left;"><li>Configuration Execution: for example, &quot;fmu&quot; execution at 2024-08-23T12:47:14.008&quot;</li><li>Operation Instance: for example, &quot;FMU Middleware Generation&quot;</li></ul><p style="text-align: left;">Those objects are displayed in the <strong>Code Generation Result Table</strong>. Among other information you can see the duration of each operation and the total duration on the execution, same as for the status.</p><p style="text-align: left;">If you click the Open Logs in the Logs column,  you will see the logs of the selected operation.</p><p style="text-align: left;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="logs_of_operations.png" /></ac:image></p><p>If you click the location in the Local Result Path column, your file explorer is opened at the specified location.</p><h2>Options</h2><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">You can switch projects (or even shut down your computer, saving your work first) while waiting for the generation result as it is running on the cloud and as the widget keeps the IDs of the cloud assets in the record files located in &lt;local workspace path&gt;\etc\operations\spoRecordFile.json (you will have access to it if you need it, for example, for the debugging purpose). When you come back, another dialog will tell you that operations were running while you were away and will propose you inspect the result and download it.</span></p><h2>Additional Information</h2><p>A workspace is automatically created on our cloud servers for each of your projects using the widget. This workspace gathers the assets needed for the generation. A cloud workspace is automatically deleted after 30 days of inactivity. You can also delete it manually in the<strong> Environment Options </strong>dialog. If you delete the workspace, all the components will have to be regenerated on the cloud on your next session because you will not be able to push any files to a workspace through the plugin.</p><p><strong>New environment options:</strong> Show next time section<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="environment_options.png" /></ac:image></p><h2>Troubleshooting</h2><h4>Issue 1</h4><p>If you get the following error (see screenshot attached), it means you are using a model that was made for previous <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE versions, method signatures in the OB have evolved in SOP 1.19.1 (25xFD03) </p><p>/usr/bin/x86_64-w64-mingw32-ld: MyOutput_fmu/obj/win64/Cse_Flashlight.o:Cse_Flashlight.c:(.text+0xb7e): <strong>undefined reference to `Counter_OB_cnt_init'</strong><br />/usr/bin/x86_64-w64-mingw32-ld: MyOutput_fmu/obj/win64/Cse_Flashlight.o:Cse_Flashlight.c:(.rdata$.refptr.Counter_OB_cnt<span>[.refptr.Counter_OB_cnt]</span>+0x0): <strong>undefined reference to `Counter_OB_cnt'</strong><br /><strong>collect2: error: ld returned 1 exit status</strong><br />make: *** <span>[compilation.mk:40: MyOutput_fmu/fmu/binaries/win64/Flashlight.dll]</span> Error 1<br />End of FMU Target Build Operation v1.0.0 *****<br />Operation &quot;FMU Target Build&quot; finished<br />File(s) downloaded under: E:\Program Files\CATIAMagic\from3DSSoftware\2024xR2\samples\CATIA Software Producer\Flashlight_SOP\fmu<br /><span>[2025.05.30::17:57:34]</span> Generation of &quot;fmu&quot; by &quot;FMU Target Build&quot; ended with code 1 (FMU Builder execution failed 2)</p><p><strong>Solution</strong></p><p>In your opaque behavior, add as a prefix the name of the parent of your OB (for example, OB_cnt by &quot;Counter_&quot;). Clean and execute your operation again to get the new files.</p><p><br /></p><h4>Issue 2</h4><p style="text-align: left;">There is an evolution in the signature of the opaque behaviors in SOP 2.0.1 (3DEXP 2026xBeta2), the main one is now suffixed by &quot;_main&quot;.'</p><p style="text-align: left;"><strong>Solution</strong></p><p style="text-align: left;">FlashlightModel::Counter::ob element (sample<span> </span><strong>SysML Flashlight Tempo.mdzip</strong>) ** has to be updated as follows (same for the two other behaviors<br />WindowsApplication::Windows_IO::ob, and LinuxApplication::Linux_IO::ob):</p><p style="text-align: left;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="troubleshooting2.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189140084 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00161: CATIA Systems Synthesis

- page_id: `189140084`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189140084/CATIA+Systems+Synthesis
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:36:42.993+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > CATIA applications
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

4

An integrated MDZipX plugin helps you improve the display of diagrams on the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform by exporting them to an MDZipX file. You can use different export options for different types of projects as described below.

##### Exporting diagrams to an MDZipX file

You can export diagrams from a local project to an MDZipX file either with or without used project data.

#### NOTE: Prerequisite

Prerequisite

Publishing diagrams to an MDZipX file works only with local projects.

To export diagrams to an MDZipX file

1. Open a local project
2. In the main menu, select 3DEXPERIENCE > CATIA Systems Synthesis Analysis .
3. Select one of the following commands:
  - Export to MDZipX File - exports the .svg file for each diagram.
  - Export to MDZipX File Including Data from Used Projects - exports the .svg file for each diagram and the data from used projects. [ATTACHMENT filename='export_to_mdzipx_file.png']
4. In the open dialog, select the target directory for the exported file.
5. Click the Save button.

The exported MDZipX file is saved in the specified target directory.

##### Publishing MDZipX files to Teamwork Cloud

When working with Teamwork Cloud projects, you can publish all model diagrams to Teamwork Cloud as an MDZipX file. During publishing, each diagram from every project version is saved as an SVG file.

#### NOTE: Prerequisite

Prerequisite

Publishing MDZipX files to Teamwork Cloud works only with Teamwork Cloud projects.

To publish MDZipX to Teamwork Cloud

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opening projects from Teamwork Cloud' space=''] .
2. In the main menu, click 3DEXPERIENCE > CATIA Systems Synthesis Analysis .
3. Select Publish MDZipX File to Teamwork Cloud . [ATTACHMENT filename='publish_mdzipx_to_teamwork_cloud.png']

##### Publishing diagrams as project assets to the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform

When working with projects stored on the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform, you can export model diagrams to the SVG format and store them as auxiliary resources inside the model. The SVG files are then retrieved from the model and displayed in the CATIA Systems Traceability Application.

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

- Publishing diagrams as project assets works only with the projects stored on the 3D EXPERIENCE platform.
- Only the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Complete diagrams' space=''] are exported to the SVG format and published as project assets.

To publish diagrams as project assets to the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opening projects from 3DEXPERIENCE platform' space='']Open a project stored in the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform .
2. In the main menu, select 3DEXPERIENCE > CATIA Systems Synthesis Analysis > Publish Diagrams as Assets . A new Assets package with the SVG package inside it is created under the Model element. All diagrams marked as complete are exported to SVG and stored under the SVG package. [ATTACHMENT filename='assets_package.png']
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Committing changes to 3DEXPERIENCE platform' space='']Commit changes to the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform .

##### Environment Options

You modify the behavior of the MDZipX plugin by changing the environment options under the CATIA Systems Synthesis Analysis group. For example, you can include diagrams from used projects to exported MDZipX files or choose to publish an MDZipX file to Teamwork Cloud automatically upon every project commit.

You can specify the diagram export options, such as dimensions and building time (the latter applies only to tables and matrices), in the **Project Options** dialog. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram image export' space=''] page to learn more.

To include diagrams from used projects to an MDZipX file or project assets

1. In the main menu, select Options > Environment .
2. In the left-side panel, select the Collaboration option group.
3. Set the Publish Diagrams from Used Projects option to true .
4. Click OK .

Publish an MDZipX file to Teamwork Cloud automatically upon project commit

1. In the main menu, select Options > Environment .
2. In the left-side panel, select the Collaboration option group.
3. Set the Publish MDZipX Upon Commit option to true .
4. Click OK .

MagicDraw and NoMagic Teamwork Cloud Connectors Configuration

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bfed528a-9361-4361-a030-dd5a2bcb6491"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>An integrated MDZipX plugin helps you improve the display of diagrams on the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform by exporting them to an MDZipX file. You can use different export options for different types of projects as described below.</p><h3>Exporting diagrams to an MDZipX file</h3><p>You can export diagrams from a local project to an MDZipX file either with or without used project data.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="05af13e6-4be0-4497-a1f7-a521aad3e6d0"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Prerequisite</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Publishing diagrams to an MDZipX file works only with local projects.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To export diagrams to an MDZipX file</p><hr /><ol><li>Open a local project</li><li>In the main menu, select <strong>3DEXPERIENCE </strong>&gt; <strong>CATIA Systems Synthesis Analysis</strong>.</li><li>Select one of the following commands:<ul><li><strong>Export to MDZipX File</strong> - exports the .svg file for each diagram.</li><li><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Export to MDZipX File Including Data from Used Projects</strong> - exports the .svg file for each diagram and the data from used projects.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:height="114"><ri:attachment ri:filename="export_to_mdzipx_file.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li></ul></li><li>In the open dialog, select the target directory for the exported file.</li><li>Click the <strong>Save</strong> button.</li></ol><p><br />The exported MDZipX file is saved in the specified target directory.</p><h3>Publishing MDZipX files to Teamwork Cloud</h3><p>When working with Teamwork Cloud projects, you can publish all model diagrams to Teamwork Cloud as an MDZipX file. During publishing, each diagram from every project version is saved as an SVG file.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3d9c34ce-fd05-4082-8685-827f0e49ff5f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Prerequisite</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Publishing MDZipX files to Teamwork Cloud works only with Teamwork Cloud projects.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To publish MDZipX to Teamwork Cloud</p><hr /><ol><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Opening projects from Teamwork Cloud" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open a Teamwork Cloud project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>In the main menu, click <strong>3DEXPERIENCE </strong>&gt; <strong>CATIA Systems Synthesis Analysis</strong>.</li><li>Select <strong>Publish MDZipX File to Teamwork Cloud</strong>.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:height="114"><ri:attachment ri:filename="publish_mdzipx_to_teamwork_cloud.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><h3>Publishing diagrams as project assets to the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform</h3><p style="text-align: left;">When working with projects stored on the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform, you can export model diagrams to the SVG format and store them as auxiliary resources inside the model. The SVG files are then retrieved from the model and displayed in <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">the CATIA Systems Traceability Application.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7a6b8a47-a73e-4570-b634-fcc821f25410"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Prerequisites</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Publishing diagrams as project assets works only with the projects stored on the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform.</li><li>Only the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Complete diagrams" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[diagrams marked as complete]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> are exported to the SVG format and published as project assets.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="text-align: left;"><br /></p><p>To publish diagrams as project assets to the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform</p><hr /><ol><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Opening projects from 3DEXPERIENCE platform" /><ac:link-body>Open a project stored in the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform</ac:link-body></ac:link><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">.</span></li><li>In the main menu, select <strong>3DEXPERIENCE </strong>&gt; <strong>CATIA Systems Synthesis Analysis</strong> &gt; <strong>Publish Diagrams as Assets</strong>. A new <em>Assets</em> package with the <em>SVG</em> package inside it is created under the Model element. All diagrams marked as complete are exported to SVG and stored under the <em>SVG</em> package.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="assets_package.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Committing changes to 3DEXPERIENCE platform" /><ac:link-body>Commit changes to the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li></ol><h3><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Environment Options</span></h3><p>You modify the behavior of the MDZipX plugin by changing the environment options under the CATIA Systems Synthesis Analysis group. For example, you can include diagrams from used projects to exported MDZipX files or choose to publish an MDZipX file to Teamwork Cloud automatically upon every project commit. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0ae12f24-a4b3-4614-b014-2a939c187bc5"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can specify the diagram export options, such as dimensions and building time (the latter applies only to tables and matrices), in the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog. See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Diagram image export" /></ac:link> page to learn more.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><br /><p>To include diagrams from used projects to an MDZipX file or project assets</p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu, select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong>. </li><li>In the left-side panel, select the <strong>Collaboration</strong> option group.</li><li>Set the <strong>Publish Diagrams from Used Projects</strong> option to <em>true</em>.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br /><br /></li></ol><p>Publish an MDZipX file to Teamwork Cloud automatically upon project commit</p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu, select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong>.</li><li>In the left-side panel, select the <strong>Collaboration</strong> option group.</li><li>Set the <strong>Publish MDZipX Upon Commit</strong> option to <em>true</em>.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="671a27e2-5851-4cc8-899c-f76020a4f529"><ac:rich-text-body>For more information, please refer to <a href="https://help.3ds.com/2024x/English/DSDoc/TraUserMap/smv-c-ad-NoMagicConfiguration.htm?contextscope=onpremise">MagicDraw and NoMagic Teamwork Cloud Connectors Configuration</a>.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

## PAGE 00162: CG Properties Editor

- page_id: `189145853`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189145853/CG+Properties+Editor
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:42:03.630+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Code Engineering > C++ Code Engineering
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### File Separator Format

This property is used to indicate the format of a file separator used in code generation.

##### Default Return Value

You can set the default return value to be used for generating function body for new function. For example, if the default return value of bool is set to false and generated, the function shown below will be created in a .cpp file.

```cpp

```

| Property Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Interactive mode | If enabled, you will be questioned during the reverse process when the parser cannot decide whether the symbol is a class or namespace. For example:If you reverse the following code class A { B::X i; }; The parser does not have enough information to decide whether B is a class or namespace. Figure 122 -- Question dialog for interactive mode Default ModeIf you are using the default mode, rather than the interactive mode, the reverse module will automatically give the best guess according to the information in the code. |
| Parse error before stop | Allows you to specify the number of errors to ignore before the reverse process will be terminated. You can set the property to zero to allow CE to reverse all code before stopping. |
| Parse Includes | If set to false, the reverse module will reverse only the selected files and ignore all #include statements. |
| Show message for not found includes | Used to display messages in the message window when the reverse module cannot find the files included in the #include statement. Parse IncludesYou must set the property Parse Includes to true in order to use the property Show message for not found includes. |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189143748 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00163: Change Sets

- page_id: `189143748`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189143748/Change+Sets
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:40:21.148+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Using Teamwork Cloud > Reviewing model history
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Editing elements

1192448784

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Editing elements

1192448786

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Editing elements

1192448785

While editing your server project, you can track project modifications in the **Change Sets**panel, located right after the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='']. A change set is a related group of elements, diagrams and symbol modifications in a project that are created or modified locally and not yet committed to the server. You may have several sets for your project and commit them one by one. This feature is useful when, for example, you are working on a server project and receive a request for an immediate change. You can suspend your changes, complete the immediate task and commit the changes, after which you can resume your workon the previously suspended change set. This also applies when you are working simultaneously on different tasks that are required to be delivered as separate commits.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A set of changes made to a server project.

#### NOTE: Editing elements

Editing elements

Ensure that you [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Locking model for edit' space=''] to enable editing.

You can create as many change sets as you need. To organize and identify change sets, you may name each one. Only one change set can be active at a time. In order to create a new change set, all other change sets must be suspended. As shown in the preceding figure, colors on an element in the change set indicate whether the edit is an addition, modification or deletion. Also, you may [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Comparing projects' space=''] between the base project and a project with modifications applied.

To manage an *active* change set

- Right click an active change set and select:
  - Commit - to commit changes to the server
  - Rename - to name a change set
  - Suspend - to suspend a change set for further editions.
  - Discard - to discard the local changes in a change set. Note: all changes will be removed and this action cannot be undone.
  - Tag version with Change ID after Commit - for the Change ID to be automatically reflected in the Project History.

To manage *suspended* change sets

- Right click a suspended change set and select:
  - Resume - to resume working with a suspended change set. Ensure that the active change set is suspended before resuming work on another change set.
  - Discard - to discard the local changes in a change set. Note: all changes will be removed and this action cannot be undone.

To compare a project

1. Select the active change set.
2. On the toolbar of the Change Sets panel, click [ATTACHMENT filename='quick_diff_button.png'] . The list containing three types of comparison appears. Click Compare Projects .
3. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Analyzing comparison results' space='']**Difference Viewer**dialog opens showing changes in element property values as well as detailed information of directly related elements and new relationships.

##### Resuming change sets in Lock-Free Editing mode

In some cases, it may not be possible to resume the suspended change sets in the Lock-Free Editing mode without taking additional steps. The reason why this happens is the detection of conflicts among the suspended changes and present project version elements. In such a case, you need to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Reviewing conflicting changes' space=''] before you can resume the changes and continue working on them.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Resuming suspended change sets.

The project version with the suspended change set has to be opened to review and confirm the update result of the conflicting changes. You can simply do so by clicking the **Review Conflicts** button in the warning that appears:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Dialog asking to return to the project with the suspended change set version.

1192448783

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Analyzing comparison results' space='']

Ensure that the active change set is suspended before resuming work on another change set.</li><li><strong>Discard</strong> - to discard the local changes in a change set. In such a case, you need to <ac:link ac:anchor="Resolving conflicting changes"><ri:page ri:content-title="Reviewing conflicting changes" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[review and resolve these conflicts]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> before you can resume the changes and continue working on them. </p><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="resuming.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(94,108,132);">Resuming suspended change sets.</span></h6><p>The project version with the suspended change set has to be opened to review and confirm the update result of the conflicting changes. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141067 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00164: Changing a path style

- page_id: `189141067`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141067/Changing+a+path+style
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:37:45.680+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming > Working with paths and relationships
- labels: ['path-style', 'rectilinear', 'oblique', 'bezier']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To change a path style, do either

- Right-click a path(s) on the diagram pane and select Reset Labels Positions or Remove Break Points .
- Select a path(s) on the diagram pane and on the diagram toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_break_points.png'] , [ATTACHMENT filename='reset_labels_positions.png'] , or [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .
- Select a path(s) on the diagram pane and on the Edit menu click Paths , and then choose one of the following:
  - Path Style to select one of the path styles - rectilinear, oblique, or bezier.
  - Change Path Style (shortcut keys Ctrl+L) to change the path style to rectilinear, oblique, or bezier.
  - Reset Labels Positions to reset the changed path labels position to default.
  - Remove Break Points to remove all angles of the path.
- Right-click a path(s) on the diagram pane and select Symbol Properties . In the Symbol Properties dialog, change the Path Style property value by selecting rectilinear, oblique, or bezier.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To change a path style, do either</p><hr /><ul><li>Right-click a path(s) on the diagram pane and select <strong>Reset Labels Positions</strong> or <strong>Remove Break Points</strong>.</li><li>Select a path(s) on the diagram pane and on the diagram toolbar, click <ac:image ac:title="Remove Break Points" ac:alt="Remove Break Points"><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove_break_points.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>, <ac:image ac:title="Reset Labels Positions" ac:alt="Reset Labels Positions"><ri:attachment ri:filename="reset_labels_positions.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>, or<strong> <ac:image ac:title="Rectilinear" ac:alt="Rectilinear"><ri:attachment ri:filename="rectilinear.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong>.</li><li>Select a path(s) on the diagram pane and on the <strong>Edit</strong> menu click <strong>Paths</strong>, and then choose one of the following:<br /><ul><li><strong>Path Style</strong> to select one of the path styles - rectilinear, oblique, or bezier.</li><li><strong>Change Path Style</strong> (shortcut keys Ctrl+L) to change the path style to rectilinear, oblique, or bezier.</li><li><strong>Reset Labels Positions </strong>to reset the changed path labels position to default.</li><li><strong>Remove Break Points</strong> to remove all angles of the path.</li></ul></li><li>Right-click a path(s) on the diagram pane and select <strong>Symbol Properties</strong>. In the <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog, change the <strong>Path Style</strong> property value by selecting rectilinear, oblique, or bezier.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141053 space=MD2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00165: Changing diagram type

- page_id: `189141053`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141053/Changing+diagram+type
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-06T19:45:21.357+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The diagram type can be changed to another type of diagram if both diagram types are compatible.

#### NOTE: Diagram Elements

Diagram Elements

Diagram elements are not converted.

###### **Uses for changing the diagram type:**

- To migrate with the existing project to a diagram type that was not available until then. For example, migrating a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space=''] to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Block Definition Diagram' space='SYSMLP2024xR2'] .
- To migrate with the existing project from the diagram type, which will be erased from your project. For example, if the user has decided to erase a plugin, they would need to convert that plugin's specific diagram to standard diagrams.

###### **Diagram conversion scenarios:**

- Any static diagram can be converted to another static diagram.
- Any dynamic diagram can be converted to another diagram if and only if both diagrams are based on the same diagram type and both diagrams are compatible.

To change the diagram type

1. Select one or more of the same type diagrams in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space=''] (Containment, Structure, or Diagram tree).
2. From the shortcut menu, choose Refactor > Change Type To . In the open dialog, select the desired diagram type. [ATTACHMENT filename='Screen Shot 2017-06-06 at 5.02.07 PM.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target">The diagram type can be changed to another type of diagram if both diagram types are compatible.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d10c128c-e709-43cf-b861-e3c7eba7699c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Diagram Elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Diagram elements are not converted.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h4><strong>Uses for changing the diagram type:</strong></h4><ul><li>To migrate with the existing project to a diagram type that was not available until then. For example, migrating a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Class diagram" /></ac:link> to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" ri:content-title="SysML Block Definition Diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SysML Block Definition diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>To migrate with the existing project from the diagram type, which will be erased from your project. For example, if the user has decided to erase a plugin, they would need to convert that plugin's specific diagram to standard diagrams. </li></ul><p><br /></p><h4><strong>Diagram conversion scenarios:</strong></h4><ul><li>Any static diagram can be converted to another static diagram. </li><li>Any dynamic diagram can be converted to another diagram if and only if both diagrams are based on the same diagram type and both diagrams are compatible.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To change the diagram type</p><hr /><ol><li>Select one or more of the same type diagrams in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Model Browser" /></ac:link> (Containment, Structure, or Diagram tree).</li><li>From the shortcut menu, choose <strong>Refactor </strong>&gt;<strong> Change Type To</strong>. In the open dialog, select the desired diagram type.<br /><ac:image ac:height="400"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Screen Shot 2017-06-06 at 5.02.07 PM.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189139659 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00166: Changing interface style

- page_id: `189139659`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189139659/Changing+interface+style
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:36:15.303+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Getting started > Understanding the user interface
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Look and Feel Themes

1426180851

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Look and Feel Themes

1426180854

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Look and Feel Themes

1426180852

#### EXCERPT: Look and Feel Themes

INLINE

You can change the appearance of your modeling tool windows, dialogs, and menus. The **Look and Feel** menu allows you to personalize the user interface using a number of predefined styles and themes. You can also create a custom theme with your favorite colors and fonts.

To change the interface style

1. In the main menu of a modeling tool, select Options > Look and Feel .
2. Select the desired style from the list of available styles.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Look and Feel menu options.

#### NOTE: Important

Important

- The selected style may not look exactly like the style in related operating systems.
- Depending on your operating system, some style options may be unavailable.

##### Creating a custom theme

Define your own interface style by creating a custom theme. The **Custom** theme option is located in the **Look and Feel Themes** submenu which also offers 8 predefined themes. The **Custom** theme allows you to specify text fonts and sizes of the desired interface elements as well as their colors.

To create a custom theme

1. In the main menu of a modeling tool, select Options > Look and Feel > Metal .
2. In the same menu, select Options > Look and Feel > Look and Feel Themes > Custom .
3. In the open dialog, change the values of the desired style properties.
4. Click OK .

#### NOTE: Look and Feel Themes

Look and Feel Themes

The themes listed in the **Look and Feel Themes** submenu are only available if you select the **Metal** style.

1426180850

## PAGE 00167: Changing state to composite/submachine/orthogonal state

- page_id: `189141764`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141764/Changing+state+to+composite+submachine+orthogonal+state
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:38:29.923+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > State Machine > State
- labels: ['changing-states']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can change your current State to a simple State, composite State, orthogonal State, and submachine State. 
 
 #simpletocompositeTo change a State to a composite State

- Drag a vertex on a simple State symbol. A region is created and the simple State is converted to a composite State.

To change a State to a orthogonal State

- Add two regions to the State. Read more about [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State' space=''].

To change a State to a submachine State

1. On the diagram, right-click the State, then on the shortcut menu, click **Submachine**.
2. In the dialog, click to select a submachine that you wish to apply on the State or click the **Create** button, to create the submachine.

To change a State to a submachine State, the State must contain no regions.

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space='']

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189140376 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00168: Changing the context

- page_id: `189140376`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189140376/Changing+the+context
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:36:55.103+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Analysis diagrams > Relation Map
- labels: ['relation-map-change-context', 'relation-map-context']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Context is the element, from which a relation map is drawn and the analysis is started. You can set any element as the context.

To set an element as the context of the relation map

- Drag the selected element from the Model Browser to the relation map.
- In the Criteria area, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='select_context_element_button.png'] button next to the Context box and then select the element in the Select Context Element dialog.
- If the **Make Element as Context on Selection** option is turned on, click any node to make it the new context of the structure. To turn this option on, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the toolbar and select **Make Element as Context on Selection**.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Context is the element, from which a relation map is drawn and the analysis is started. You can set any element as the context.</p><p>To set an element as the context of the relation map</p><hr /><ul><li>Drag the selected element from the Model Browser to the relation map.</li><li>In the Criteria area, click the <ac:image ac:title="Select Context Element" ac:alt="Select Context Element"><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_context_element_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button next to the Context box and then select the element in the <strong>Select Context Element</strong><span> </span>dialog.<span> </span></li><li><p>If the <strong>Make Element as Context on Selection</strong> option is turned on, click any node to make it the new context of the structure. To turn this option on, click <ac:image ac:title="Options" ac:alt="Options"><ri:attachment ri:filename="options_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the toolbar and select <strong>Make Element as Context on Selection</strong>.</p></li></ul><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189142306 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00169: Changing the tooltips style

- page_id: `189142306`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189142306/Changing+the+tooltips+style
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:39:09.242+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can specify the tooltips display mode of the symbols on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space=''] by selecting to hide it, to show only the element name, or its documentation text. The element tooltip in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space=''] displays only documentation text, if it is specified for an element. Otherwise, the tooltip is not shown in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space=''].

#### INFO: Writing documentation for an element

Writing documentation for an element

You can write a documentation for an element by using the:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Documentation tab' space=''] .
- Element [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] > Documentation/Hyperlinks property group.
- Element [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] > Documentation property.

To change the tooltips style of the symbol on the diagram pane

1. On the main menu, select Options > Project .
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project options' space='']**Project Options** dialog find the Tooltips Style option.
3. Select one of the following values:
  - Do Not Show (default) - the tooltip is not shown when hovering a mouse on any element.
  - Show Element Name - The tooltip displays the name of the element when hovering a mouse on it.
  - Show Element Documentation - The tooltip displays the documentation text of the element when hovering a mouse on it.
4. Click **OK**. 
The symbol tooltips display mode is changed in the entire project.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can specify the tooltips display mode of the symbols on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Understanding the user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[diagram pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> by selecting to hide it, to show only the element name, or its documentation text. The element tooltip in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Model Browser" /></ac:link> displays only documentation text, if it is specified for an element. Otherwise, the tooltip is not shown in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Model Browser" /></ac:link>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="71741dd5-c1fa-46c6-9794-ac42b18dacf7"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Writing documentation for an element</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can write a documentation for an element by using the:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Documentation tab" /></ac:link>.</li><li>Element <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link> &gt; <strong>Documentation/Hyperlinks</strong> property group.</li><li>Element <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link> &gt; <strong>Documentation</strong> property.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To change the tooltips style of the symbol on the diagram pane</p><hr /><ol><li>On the main menu, select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project</strong>.</li><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Project options" /><ac:link-body><strong>Project Options</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> find the <strong>Tooltips Style</strong> option.</li><li>Select one of the following values:<br /><ul><li><strong>Do Not Show</strong> (default) -  the tooltip is not shown when hovering a mouse on any element.</li><li><strong>Show Element Name</strong> - The tooltip displays the name of the element when hovering a mouse on it.</li><li><strong>Show Element Documentation </strong>- The tooltip displays the documentation text of the element when hovering a mouse on it.</li></ul></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br />The symbol tooltips display mode is changed in the entire project.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189143776 space=MD2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00170: Changing the used project version

- page_id: `189143776`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189143776/Changing+the+used+project+version
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-06T19:45:57.332+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Using Teamwork Cloud > Managing project usages
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Shortcut menu

762721493

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Shortcut menu

762721495

762721492

**On this page**

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Shortcut menu

762721494

#### WARNING: Permissions

Permissions

To change the used project version, you must have:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Permissions' space='TWCloud2024xR2'], [Edit Resources](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/TWCloud2024xR2/Permissions#Permissions-EditResourcespermission), [Edit Resource Properties](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/TWCloud2024xR2/Permissions#Permissions-EditResourcePropertiespermission), and Administer Resources permissions on the main project and
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Permissions' space='TWCloud2024xR2'] permission on the used project whose version you want to change.

You can set an option to receive a notification when a new used project version appears. You may then choose to update the used project version to the latest version or keep working with the current one.

##### Setting notifications about version changes

By default, when you open your server project you will get a notification if there are used project versions later than the used one in your project that meet notification criteria. When this notification appears, click the **Update used projects** link and select the projects you want to update in the open **Update Used Projects** dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can change the default notification settings to receive notifications when a server project is opened and when updates are done, as well as disable them if they are not necessary.

To set when to show notifications about newer used project versions

1. On the main menu, click **Options** > **Environment** to open the **Environment Options**dialog.
2. In the options group list, select **Collaboration**, then select the **Notify About Newer Project Usages**option, and specify the commands to be notified:
  1. 
    - on project opening and updating;
    - on project opening; or
    - never.

##### Notifications for any new version

If you do not need to specify a used project version with tags, you can choose to get notifications for any new version.

To get notifications for any new version

1. From the main menu, select **Collaborate** > **Project Usages** > **Project Usages**.
2. Select a used project and click to select the check box next to **Notify on**.
3. From the drop down list select **Notify for Any New Version**.
4. Click **OK** to close the dialog.

##### Notifications for new tagged versions

When you want to get notifications about updated used projects during your modeling process, you must define the used projects for routine version checking.Youare allowed to specify certain commit tags for every used project. The modeling tool will show a notification when a new version of a used project with a specified commit tag is available.

To get notifications about changes to a certain tagged used project version

1. From the main menu, select **Collaborate** > **Project Usages** > **Project Usages**.
2. Select a used project and click to select the check box next to **Notify on**.
3. From the drop down list select**Notify on New Tagged Version**.
4. Click **Specify Tags**, near the drop-down list.
5. In the **Specify Tags**dialog, double click the first row. To add or delete rows, do one of the following:
  1. To add a row, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''], or press Alt + Enter.
  2. To delete a row, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''], or press Alt + Backspace.
6. Specify the tag, e.g., *2018Q*(tags are case insensitive), and then click **OK** to close the dialog. If you do not specify tags, the modeling tool will show a warning icon.It is recommended that tags specification be done in patterns, because the modeling tool searches for partial matches. See the example below for more information.ExampleAfter a new version of the used project is committed to the server with a tag that fully or partially matches the specified tag, you will be notified about updates of these tagged projects.Let’s say you specify these tags: 
*2018Q* 
*2019Q* 
*2020Q* 
*Major*New versions and notification status:New version without tags - no notificationNew version with the tag [ Major ] – notification about a new versionNew version with the tag [ 2018q1 ] – notification about a new versionNew version with tags [Release][Major] – notification about a new versionNew version with the tag [Release] – no notificationNew version with the tag [Q_2018Q2T] - notification about a new version
7. Click **OK** to close the dialog.

##### Updating versions of used projects

If a used project has tags specified for notification, only the version meeting the used projects notifications criteria (tags) will be shown in this dialog.

To update the used project version

1. From the main menu, select Collaborate > Project Usages > Project Usages .
2. Click the Update Used Projects button.
3. In the open Update **Used Projects** dialog, select the project you want to update. [ATTACHMENT filename='Update Used Projects_target version.png']
4. Wait while the version changes to the latest and click OK to close the dialog. The used project is automatically locked. The lock will be released after the project commit.

##### Changing versions of used projects

To change the used project version

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Locking model for edit' space=''] .
2. From the main menu, select Collaborate > Project Usages > Project Usages .
3. Select a used project.
4. Click the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button to change the version of a used project. Shortcut menuThis step can be done via shortcut menu. Right-click project and then select **Change version.** [IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. In the open dialog, select the used project version to switch to and click OK .
6. Wait while the version changes to the selected one and click OK to close the dialog.

Versions are displayed in the following format:*#<used version>/<latest version>.*

The term #1/3 denotes that 1 is the used version and 3 is the latest version.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=209257488 space=MD2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00171: Changing used project iterations

- page_id: `209257488`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/209257488/Changing+used+project+iterations
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-02-04T14:54:10.547+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform > Managing project usages via 3DEXPERIENCE platform
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When a new used project iteration becomes available on the server, you may want to update the used project to the latest iteration. In addition, you can set up notifications about new used project iterations committed to the server.

##### Changing used project iterations

To change the used project iteration

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Locking model for edit' space='MD2022x'] .
2. From the main menu, select Collaborate > Project Usages > Project Usages .
3. In the Project Usages dialog, do one of the following:
  - Select a used project and click [ATTACHMENT filename='change_version_button.png'] .
  - Right-click a used project and select Change Iteration . [ATTACHMENT filename='changing_used_project_iteration.png']
4. In the Edit Branches dialog, select the project iteration you want to use and click OK .
5. When the iteration is changed, click OK to close the Project Usages dialog.

In the **Project Usages** dialog, used project iterations are shown in this format <*used iteration>/<latest iteration>*. For example, 2/3 means that project iteration 2[https://docs.nomagic.com/pages/resumedraft.action?draftId=111218408&draftShareId=1efcb639-bb31-4680-9fb3-f980b62c2a5c&](https://docs.nomagic.com/pages/resumedraft.action?draftId=111218408&draftShareId=1efcb639-bb31-4680-9fb3-f980b62c2a5c&)is used in your project, but the latest usage iteration on the server is 3.

##### Updating used projects to the latest iteration

To update used projects to the latest iteration

1. In the main menu, select Collaborate > Project Usages > Project Usages .
2. In the Project Usages dialog, click [ATTACHMENT filename='Update_used_projects.PNG'] .
3. In the Update column of the open dialog, select the check boxes for the projects you want to update and click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='updating_used_projects.png']
4. Click OK to close the Project Usages dialog.

##### Setting notifications about new used project iterations

By default, you will get a notification aboutnew used project iterations every time you open the main project. You can change the setting for these notifications in the**Environment Options**dialog.

To change the notification settings for used projects

1. In the main menu, select**Options** > **Environment**.
2. In the**Environment Options**dialog, select the**Collaboration**option group.
3. Change the value of the **Notify About Newer Project Usages**option, to one of the following:
  - Open - to show notifications about new used project versions every time you open the main project.
  - Open, Update - to show notifications about new used project versions every time you open or update the main project.
  - **Never**- to disable notifications about new used project versions.
4. Click**OK**to close the**Environment Options**dialog.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

In addition, you can set up notifications about new used project iterations committed to the server.</span></p><h3>Changing used project iterations</h3><p>To change the used project iteration</p><hr /><ol><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2022x" ri:content-title="Locking model for edit" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Lock the used project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>From the main menu, select <strong>Collaborate</strong> &gt; <strong>Project Usages</strong> &gt; <strong>Project Usages</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Project Usages</strong> dialog, do one of the following:<ul><li>Select a used project and click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="16"><ri:attachment ri:filename="change_version_button.png" /></ac:image>.</li><li>Right-click a used project and select <strong>Change Iteration</strong>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="changing_used_project_iteration.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li></ul></li><li>In the <strong>Edit Branches</strong> dialog, select the project iteration you want to use and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li>When the iteration is changed, click <strong>OK</strong> to close the <strong>Project Usages</strong> dialog.</li></ol><p><br />In the <strong>Project Usages</strong> dialog, used project iterations are shown in this format &lt;<em>used iteration&gt;/&lt;latest iteration&gt;</em>. For example, 2/3 means that project iteration 2<a class="toolbar-trigger aui-button aui-button-subtle" data-control-id="linkbrowserButton" href="https://docs.nomagic.com/pages/resumedraft.action?draftId=111218408&amp;draftShareId=1efcb639-bb31-4680-9fb3-f980b62c2a5c&amp;"> </a>is used in your project, but the latest usage iteration on the server is 3.</p><h3>Updating used projects to the latest iteration</h3><p>To update used projects to the latest iteration</p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu, select <strong>Collaborate</strong> &gt; <strong>Project Usages</strong> &gt; <strong>Project Usages</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Project Usages</strong> dialog, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Update_used_projects.PNG" /></ac:image>.</li><li>In the <strong>Update</strong> column of the open dialog, select the check boxes for the projects you want to update and click <strong>OK</strong>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="updating_used_projects.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> to close the <strong>Project Usages</strong> dialog.</li></ol><h3>Setting notifications about new used project iterations</h3><p style="">By default, you will get a notification about<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span> </span>new used project iterations every time you open the main project. You can change the setting for these notifications in the<span> </span><strong>Environment Options</strong><span> </span>dialog.</span></p><p style=""><br /></p><p style="">To change the notification settings for used projects</p><hr style="" /><ol style=""><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">In the main menu, select<span> </span><strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">In the<span> </span><strong>Environment Options</strong><span> </span>dialog, select the<strong><span> </span>Collaboration</strong><span> </span>option group.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Change the value of the <strong>Notify About Newer Project Usages </strong>option, to one of the following:</span><ul><li><strong>Open</strong><span> </span>- to show notifications about new used project versions every time you open the main project.</li><li><strong>Open, Update</strong><span> </span>- to show notifications about new used project versions every time you open or update the main project.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Never</strong><span> </span>- to disable notifications about new used project versions.</span></li></ul></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Click<span> </span><strong>OK</strong><span> </span>to close the<span> </span><strong>Environment Options</strong><span> </span>dialog.</span></li></ol>
````
