## PAGE 00172: Chart tool

- page_id: `189145021`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189145021/Chart+tool
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-06T19:46:07.920+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1348176924

1348176935

1348176934

The Chart tool provides functions to prepare tag values from models in matrix tabular form to be mapped into a variety of chart types and styles. This tool is supported in the DOCX template. Like other Custom tools, the Chart tool (chart.JAR) must be installed in the **extensions** folder of the Report Wizard plugin. For further information about Custom tool and installation, see [Custom tool](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Custom+tool).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Chart tool using tag values from models in matrix tabular form to be mapped into chart types and styles.

To use the Chart tool in the modeling tool

1. Place the JAR file in the extensions folder of the Report Wizard plugin folder.
2. Start the modeling tool.
3. Use the following line of code to import the Chart tool to your report: text

1348176923

**Related page**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="02799f1f-fc4f-4d86-ad13-20b2332911db"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1348176924</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="74d59865-b058-43ea-ac55-d7a7f7853f60"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1348176935</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e4997ef5-05e7-4968-9af0-1832dba8af1c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1348176934</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The Chart tool provides functions to prepare tag values from models in matrix tabular form to be mapped into a variety of chart types and styles. This tool is supported in the DOCX template. Like other Custom tools, the Chart tool (chart.JAR) must be installed in the <strong>extensions</strong> folder of the Report Wizard plugin. For further information about Custom tool and installation, see <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Custom+tool">Custom tool</a>.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Chart Tool Intro.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Chart tool using tag values from models in matrix tabular form to be mapped into chart types and styles.</h6><p>To use the Chart tool in the modeling tool</p><hr /><ol><li>Place the JAR file in the <strong>extensions</strong> folder of the Report Wizard plugin folder.</li><li>Start the modeling tool.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Use the following line of code to import the Chart tool to your report:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="38fa6769-1074-4a6c-9402-be5f148457b4"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#import('chart', 'com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.ChartTool')]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="59592cd5-3e80-4e65-affc-110621bb5c70"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1348176923</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="14e8f005-5ffc-4c29-9ad1-7c194941921c" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141750 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00173: Choice

- page_id: `189141750`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141750/Choice
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:38:28.817+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > State Machine > Pseudo States
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The choice vertices, when reached, result in the dynamic evaluation of the guards or the triggers of its outgoing transitions. This realizes a dynamic conditional branch. It allows splitting of transitions into multiple outgoing paths such that the decision on which path to take may be a function of the results of prior actions performed in the same run-to-completion step.

#### PANEL: Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141318 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00174: Class

- page_id: `189141318`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141318/Class
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:38:06.023+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1408904160

1408904163

1408904162

A Class is drawn as a solid-outline rectangle with three compartments separated by horizontal lines. The top name compartment holds the Class name and other general properties of the Class (including [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='']); the middle list compartment holds a list of properties; the bottom list compartment holds a list of operations. The property and operation compartments are optional, and you can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying and suppressing compartments on shapes' space=''] them.

A Class is a descriptor for a set of objects with similar structures, behaviors, and relationships. The model is concerned with describing the intention of the Class, which are the rules that define it. The run-time execution provides its extension that are its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instance Specification' space=''].

A Class represents a concept within the system being modeled. It has a data structure, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Behavior' space=''], and relationships to other elements. The name of a Class has a scope within the package in which it is declared, and the name must be unique, among Class names, within its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package' space=''].

Classes are declared in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space=''] and are used in most of other diagrams. UML provides a graphical notation for declaring and using these Classes as well as a textual notation for referencing Classes within the descriptions of other model elements.

1408904159

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189140187 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00175: Class diagram

- page_id: `189140187`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189140187/Class+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:36:44.986+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > UML diagrams
- labels: ['class', 'diagram', 'uml']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

306054507

306054509

306054508

**Overview**

A Class diagram falls under the structural diagramming family. It is a graphic representation of the static structural model. It shows [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interface' space=''], along with their internal structure and relationships. The classes represent types of objects that are handled in a system. The instances of those types (objects) are instantiated only on the runtime and are represented by an object and the interaction diagrams. A Class diagram does not show temporal information, it describes only the classification.

Classes can be related to each other in a number of ways: associated (connected to each other), dependent (one class depends/uses another class), specialized (one class is a subtype of another class), or packaged (grouped together as a unit – package). A Class diagram does not express anything specific about the relationships of a given object, but it does abstractly describe the potential relationships of one object with other objects.

A system typically has a number of Class diagrams – not all the classes are inserted into a single Class diagram. A class can have multiple levels of meaning and participate in several Class diagrams. A Class diagram is the logical map of an existing or future source code.

**Purpose**

A class diagram describes the types of objects (e.g., people, materials, information, and behaviors) in the system and various kinds of static relationships that exist among them. Class diagrams also show the attributes and operations of a class and the constraints that apply to the way objects are connected.

**Usage**

A class diagram can be used to:

- illustrate both simple and complex models for information systems
- understand the general overview of the schematics of an application
- visually express the specific needs of a system
- highlight the specific code that needs to be programmed and implemented to the structure
- provide an implementation-independent description of types used in a system
- share relevant information with stakeholders
- detect errors early in the lifecycle.

**Summary:**

Class diagrams are valuable because they:

- define the essential resources of the system
- define relationships between resources
- generate code
- model code (reverse engineering)
- provide a focus for all the other diagrams.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of a Class diagram

306054506

The classes represent types of objects that are handled in a system. The instances of those types (objects) are instantiated only on the runtime and are represented by an object and the interaction diagrams. A Class diagram does not show temporal information, it describes only the classification. </p><p>Classes can be related to each other in a number of ways: associated (connected to each other), dependent (one class depends/uses another class), specialized (one class is a subtype of another class), or packaged (grouped together as a unit – package). A Class diagram does not express anything specific about the relationships of a given object, but it does abstractly describe the potential relationships of one object with other objects.</p><p>A system typically has a number of Class diagrams – not all the classes are inserted into a single Class diagram. A Class diagram is the logical map of an existing or future source code.</p><p><strong>Purpose</strong></p><p>A class diagram describes the types of objects (e.g., people, materials, information, and behaviors) in the system and various kinds of static relationships that exist among them. Class diagrams also show the attributes and operations of a class and the constraints that apply to the way objects are connected. </p><p><strong>Usage</strong></p><p>A class diagram can be used to:</p><ul><li>illustrate both simple and complex models for information systems </li><li>understand the general overview of the schematics of an application</li><li>visually express the specific needs of a system</li><li>highlight the specific code that needs to be programmed and implemented to the structure</li><li>provide an implementation-independent description of types used in a system</li><li>share relevant information with stakeholders </li><li>detect errors early in the lifecycle. </li></ul><p><strong>Summary:</strong></p><p>Class diagrams are valuable because they:</p><ul><li>define the essential resources of the system</li><li>define relationships between resources </li><li>generate code</li><li>model code (reverse engineering)</li><li>provide a focus for all the other diagrams. </li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="class_diagram.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Example of a Class diagram</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bdbc5c26-9baa-4aab-8ebe-f1760f1a8623"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">306054506</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating diagrams" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Class" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189143413 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00176: Class Diagram Wizard

- page_id: `189143413`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189143413/Class+Diagram+Wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:40:12.610+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Model Visualizer
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

561698190

561698194

561698193

This functionality is available in Standard, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions only.

The **Class Diagram Wizard** helps you create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space=''] when all classes and their relationships are already created and specified. You can select which classes, packages, and relationships will be included in a new [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space=''], as well as the details of the class representation to be configured (attributes, operations, accessibility). The **Class Diagram Wizard** then guides you through several steps and collects information along the way. It will automatically create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space=''] with your selected elements, freeing you from creating the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space=''] manually.

To create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space=''] using the **Class Diagram Wizard**

1. Open the Class Diagram Wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the Diagrams menu, select Diagram Wizards > Class Diagram Wizard .
  - From the Analyze menu, select Model Visualizer . In the open Model Visualizer dialog, select to open the Class Diagram Wizard and click Start.
2. Follow the steps as directed by the wizard.

561698189

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='']

| Old Value | Translation |
|  |  |

| struct | Apply the «C++Struct» stereotype. |
| union | Apply the «C++Union» stereotype. |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189140666 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00178: Clearing values from rows

- page_id: `189140666`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189140666/Clearing+values+from+rows
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:37:07.745+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Tables > Basic tasks in tables > Working with rows
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To clear values from an entire row

1. Right-click the number cell to select the entire row.
2. From the shortcut menu, click Clear . Alternatively, you can press Delete on your keyboard after selecting the entire row.

#### WARNING: Data types

Data types

Only String, Boolean, Number, or Enumeration data types can be cleared from a cell.

To clear a value from a cell

1. Right-click the specific cell of the row.
2. From the shortcut menu, click Clear . Alternatively, you can press Delete on your keyboard after selecting the cell.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target">To clear values from an entire row</p><hr /><ol><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="f5205aa9-b573-4983-984b-8ec9e03d6c2c">Right-click the number cell to select the entire row. </ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li>From the shortcut menu, click <strong>Clear</strong>.<br />Alternatively, you can press<strong> Delete</strong> on your keyboard after selecting the entire row. </li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="63035fe7-faf5-41da-b952-aaf332340a76"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Data types</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Only String, Boolean, Number, or Enumeration data types can be cleared from a cell.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p>To clear a value from a cell</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the specific cell of the row.</li><li>From the shortcut menu, click<strong> Clear</strong>. <br />Alternatively, you can press <strong>Delete</strong> on your keyboard after selecting the cell. </li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189143494 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00179: Cloning projects

- page_id: `189143494`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189143494/Cloning+projects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:40:14.516+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Using Teamwork Cloud > Managing server projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

54100852

54100855

54100853

You can clone the latest version of your server project as a separate project or the latest version of a project branch as a separate branch. The cloned project can be used as a "template" to create a new project or as an independent base for new development. IDs of a cloned project and elements are reset automatically. Project permissions are reset as well; the project is available only for the user who has cloned the project. All references to used projects (if there are any) are maintained.

- You can only clone either the latest version of a project or the latest version of a project branch.
- You need at least double the amount of memory to clone a project in comparison to opening a project.

To clone a project or a branch

1. On the main menu, click **Collaborate**> **Projects**. The **Manage Projects** dialog opens.
2. To clone a project, select the project you want to clone. You can use the search bar to filter used projects fasterby name.
3. (Optional) To clone a branch of a chosen project, select the project and do one of the following: [ATTACHMENT filename='1_select_branch.png']
  - Click the Select Branch [ATTACHMENT filename='select_branches_button.png'] button.
  - Click the three dots [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] button in the Branch section.
  - Right-click the project and select Select Branch . Select the branch you want to clone in the Select Branch dialog and click OK .
4. To clone the selected project or branch, do one of the following: [ATTACHMENT filename='2_clone_server_project.png']
  - Click the Clone button.
  - Right-click the project and select Clone Server Project .
5. The Clone Server Project dialog opens, wherein you need to type the name of the cloned project, select a category to save the project and/or add a comment. Click More , if you want to add a tag for the project.
6. (Optional) To clone with used projects, click**More**, select the**Used Projects**tab and check the**Clone Used Projects**check box. You can change the names of the cloned used projects in the**New Name**section once the**Clone Used Projects**check box is selected. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Cloned used projects do not include package permissions, hidden packages, and code engineering sets.
7. Click Clone when you are done.
8. Close the Manage Projects dialog.

Before using the cloned project with Teamwork Cloud web applications, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Committing changes to Teamwork Cloud' space=''].

54100851

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding projects to Teamwork Cloud' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189142194 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00180: Cloning selected element

- page_id: `189142194`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189142194/Cloning+selected+element
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:39:00.773+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Selecting elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

402269317

402269319

402269318

In the element selection dialog, you can clone an existing element. It means you can create a new element on the basis of the selected element having the same data as the selected element.

To clone a selected element

1. In the Tree tab, click the Creation Mode button.
2. In the model tree, select the element which you want to clone and do one of the following:
  - Click the Clone button.
  - From the selected element's shortcut menu, select Clone .
3. The Specification window of a new element opens with all properties derived from the cloned element. By default, the number is added to the element name.
4. Change or define desired properties and click Close when you are done. The new element is included to the model tree.

402269316

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with model elements' space='']

## PAGE 00181: Code Engineering

- page_id: `189145567`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189145567/Code+Engineering
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:41:48.107+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Functionality Availability

807320373

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Functionality Availability

100.00002%

807320375

820270240

**Demos**

- Code Generation
- Code Reverse

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Functionality Availability

807320374

##### Abbreviations

- UML Unified Modeling Language
- GUI Graphical User Interface
- CE Code Engineering
- CES Code Engineering Set
- AST Abstract Syntax Tree
- JLS Java Language Specification
- JVM Java Virtual Machine
- RT Round-Trip forward and backward code engineering without code loss CLR Common Language Runtime
- DSL Domain-Specific Language

#### INFO: Functionality Availability

Functionality Availability

Code Engineering is available in Professional and Enterprise editions only.

The code generation from behavior is not supported.

Code engineering provides a simple and intuitive graphical interface for merging code and UML models, as well as preparing both code skeletons out of UML models and models from code.

There are several cases where code engineering may be very useful:

- You already have code that needs to be reversed to a model.
- You wish to implement the created model.
- You need to merge your models and code.

The tool may generate code from models or create models out of code. Changes in the existing code can be reflected in the model, and you can see model changes in your code. You can also merge independent changes to a model and code without destroying data in the code or model.

Code engineering supports Java, C++, CORBA IDL, DDL, and XML Schema languages. Your models can be converted to any of those languages, or you can create UML models from the source code written in those languages. Additionally, the tool supports reversing from Java Bytecode.

807320372

**Related pages:**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189145568 space=MD2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00182: Code Engineering Sets

- page_id: `189145568`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189145568/Code+Engineering+Sets
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-06T19:46:18.028+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Code Engineering
- labels: ['code-engineering-manager', 'round-trip-set']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

418339696

418339698

418339697

You can manage code engineering through the**Code Engineering Sets**in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space=''].The **Code Engineering Sets** tree contains the list of all sets created in the project as well as instruments for managing those sets.

To add a new set

1. Choose New from the Code Engineering Sets shortcut menu.
2. Choose the language you want:

- 
  - Java
  - Java Bytecode
  - C++
  - CORBA IDL
  - DDL (Cloudscape, DB2, Microsoft Access, Microsoft SQL Server, MySQL, Oracle, Pervasive, Pointbase, PostgreSQL, Sybase). For DDL, the is required
  - XML Schema, the is required [ATTACHMENT filename='CE_language_options.png']

Edit sets in the **Round Trip Set**dialog.

To open the **Round Trip Set**dialog

- Right-click the selected set and choose Edit from the set shortcut menu.
- Select the set and press Enter.

The **Round Trip Set** dialog allows you to manage entities to be added/removed to your set.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Round Trip Set dialog. Add files tab

Specify the **Working Directory** for displaying source files. This option indicates files and required sub-directories where a code generation output goes. Type a path manually or browse in the directory tree by clicking the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button.

The **Working Package** box allows you to define any package for reverse output or code generation. A model is reversed or code generated from this specified package.

You can only select or change the working package prior to the addition of files from the working directory to the code engineering set.

The **Round Trip Set** dialog has two tabs: **Add Files** and **Add Data from Model**.

The **Add Files tab** helps you manage the source code files involved in your code engineering set.

| Element name | Function |
| --- | --- |
| All files | Lists directories with the source files for the set. |
| Files of type | Contains possible file name extensions for the chosen language. |

The **Add Data from Model** tab helps you manage elements located in the UML model.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Round Trip Set dialog. Add data from the model tab.

The **All Data** list contains the hierarchy of UML model packages with model elements (or other packages) inside. Your code engineering set can be combined out of model and code elements. The following buttons are available in the **Round Trip Set** dialog:

| Add | The selected file in the All Files or All Data list is added to the set. |
| --- | --- |
| Add All | All files in the opened or selected directory are added to the set. |
| Add Recursively | All files in the selected directory and its subdirectories are added to the set. |
| Remove | Removes the selected entity from the set. |
| Remove All | Removes all entities from the set. |

418339695

**Related Pages**:

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Code Engineering' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b9e9ecf7-fdc5-4096-97d9-4c2b72203ae1"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">418339696</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d44f8015-721b-4a87-8d59-0b80e5a3fb16"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">418339698</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3f3bd8fe-9f49-45b0-b551-42bd19913773"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">418339697</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>You can manage code engineering through the </span><strong>Code Engineering Sets </strong><span>in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Model Browser" /></ac:link>. </span>The <strong>Code Engineering Sets</strong> tree contains the list of all sets created in the project as well as instruments for managing those sets.</p><p><br /></p><p>To add a new set</p><hr /><ol><li>Choose <strong>New</strong> from the <strong>Code Engineering Sets</strong> shortcut menu.</li><li>Choose the language you want:</li></ol><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li>Java</li><li>Java Bytecode</li><li>C++</li><li>CORBA IDL</li><li>DDL (Cloudscape, DB2, Microsoft Access, Microsoft SQL Server, MySQL, Oracle, Pervasive, Pointbase, PostgreSQL, Sybase). For DDL, the <ac:link><ri:space ri:space-key="CDMP2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Cameo Data Modeler plugin]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> is required</li><li>XML Schema, the <ac:link><ri:space ri:space-key="CDMP2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Cameo Data Modeler plugin]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> is required<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="CE_language_options.png" /></ac:image></li></ul></li></ul><p style="text-align: left;"><br /></p><p style="text-align: left;">Edit sets in the <strong style="color: rgb(51,51,51);font-size: 14.0px;">Round Trip Set</strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> dialog.</span></p><p><br /></p><p>To open the <strong>Round Trip Set </strong>dialog</p><hr /><ul><li>Right-click the selected set and choose <strong>Edit</strong> from the set shortcut menu.</li><li>Select the set and press Enter.</li></ul><p>The <strong>Round Trip Set</strong> dialog allows you to manage entities to be added/removed to your set.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="round_trip_set.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Round Trip Set dialog. Add files tab</h6><p>Specify the <strong>Working Directory</strong> for displaying source files. This option indicates files and required sub-directories where a code generation output goes. Type a path manually or browse in the directory tree by clicking the <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="22"><ri:attachment ri:filename="three-dot-btn1.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Instance table" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button.</p><p>The <strong>Working Package</strong> box allows you to define any package for reverse output or code generation. A model is reversed or code generated from this specified package.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bb6e05be-c43f-4be0-87a0-8e4a7cfdab30"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can only select or change the working package prior to the addition of files from the working directory to the code engineering set.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The <strong>Round Trip Set</strong> dialog has two tabs: <strong>Add Files</strong> and <strong>Add Data from Model</strong>.</p><p>The <strong>Add Files tab</strong> helps you manage the source code files involved in your code engineering set.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Element name</th><th>Function</th></tr><tr><td>All files</td><td>Lists directories with the source files for the set.</td></tr><tr><td>Files of type</td><td><span>Contains possible file name extensions for the chosen language.</span></td></tr></tbody></table><p>The <strong>Add Data from Model</strong> tab helps you manage elements located in the UML model.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="round_trip_set_add_data_from_model.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Round Trip Set dialog. Add data from the model tab.</h6><p>The <strong>All Data</strong> list contains the hierarchy of UML model packages with model elements (or other packages) inside. Your code engineering set can be combined out of model and code elements. The following buttons are available in the <strong>Round Trip Set</strong> dialog:</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><strong>Add</strong></td><td>The selected file in the All Files or All Data list is added to the set. </td></tr><tr><td><strong>Add All</strong></td><td>All files in the opened or selected directory are added to the set.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Add Recursively</strong></td><td>All files in the selected directory and its subdirectories are added to the set.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Remove</strong></td><td>Removes the selected entity from the set.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Remove All</strong></td><td>Removes all entities from the set.</td></tr></tbody></table></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bc8a10f1-0c4d-45f4-875f-4fb871dec7c6"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">418339695</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related Pages</strong>:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="b45341f0-9cb0-47ea-bdd9-91f7d635de68"><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Code Engineering" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189144992 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00183: Code example for Group Tool

- space_key: `MD2024xR2`

- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Model validation tool
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The code in this section demonstrates some scenarios with Group Tool.

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The code in this section demonstrates some scenarios with Group Tool.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c459681d-485c-4c4c-b3e0-38222922fdec"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[##---------------------------------
## Execute $group.init() once in the script
#set( $void = $group.init())
## Add new entries to the group tool
#foreach($e in $elements)
#if($e.elementType == "class")
#set( $void = $group.put("class", $e) )
#elseif($e.elementType == "package")
#set( $void = $group.put("package", $e) )
#elseif($e.elementType == "interface")
#set( $void = $group.put("interface", $e) )
#end
#end
## Iterate over the groupTool instance
#foreach( $groupName in $group.groupNames() )
Grupp with name $groupName contains the following elements
#foreach( $e in $group.get($groupName) )
    Element: $e.name
#end
#end
---------------------------------
##set( $void = $group.init()) ## not use init group 
## Add new entries to the group tool
#foreach($e in $elements)
#if($e.elementType == "usecase")
#set( $void = $group.put("usecase", $e) )
#elseif($e.elementType == "stereotype")
#set( $void = $group.put("stereotype", $e) )
#end
#end
## Iterate over the groupTool instance
#foreach( $groupName in $group.groupNames() )
Grupp with name $groupName contains the following elements
#foreach( $e in $group.get($groupName) )
    Element: $e.name
#end
#end
---------------------------------
#set( $void = $group.init()) ## use init group
## Add new entries to the group tool
#foreach($e in $elements)
#if($e.elementType == "usecase")
#set( $void = $group.put("usecase", $e) )
#elseif($e.elementType == "stereotype")
#set( $void = $group.put("stereotype", $e) )
#end
#end
## Iterate over the groupTool instance
#foreach( $groupName in $group.groupNames() )
Grupp with name $groupName contains the following elements
#foreach( $e in $group.get($groupName) )
    Element: $e.name
#end
#end
##---------------------------------
## Create new group tool
#set( $newGroupTool = $group.create())
##set( $void = $newGroupTool.init()) ## no need to init at first time
## Add new entries to the group tool
#foreach($e in $elements)
#if($e.elementType == "class")
#set( $void = $newGroupTool.put("class", $e) )
#elseif($e.elementType == "interface")
#set( $void = $newGroupTool.put("interface", $e) )
#end
#end
==============================================================================
New Group
## Iterate over the groupTool instance
#foreach( $groupName in $newGroupTool.groupNames() )
Grupp with name $groupName contains the following elements
#foreach( $e in $newGroupTool.get($groupName) )
    Element: $e.name
#end
#end
OLD Group
## Iterate over the groupTool instance
#foreach( $groupName in $group.groupNames() )
Grupp with name $groupName contains the following elements
#foreach( $e in $group.get($groupName) )
    Element: $e.name
#end
#end
##---------------------------------]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189145005 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00184: Code examples for generic table tool

- page_id: `189145005`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189145005/Code+examples+for+generic+table+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:41:23.339+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Generic table tool
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The code in this section demonstrates some scenarios with a Generic Table tool. The code may not be executable or may not return the correct result. This is only the idea of how to use the Generic Table tool and how it works.

The code shown below prints the row, column, and its value in the form of an HTML table.

```text
#set($table = $generic.getTable($diagram))
	            #
	        #foreach($colName in $table.columnNames)
		            $colName
	  #end
	  
	  #foreach($row in $table.rows)
	        
		            $foreach.count
	        #foreach($col in $table.columnIds)
		            $table.getValueAsString($row, $col)
	        #end
	        
	    #end
	    
	#end]]>
```

###### How to learn more about the GenericTableTool

1. From the main menu choose Tools > Report Wizard .
2. In the Select Template pane, choose Tutorial > Tutorial 27 - Generic Table Export .
3. Click Next , and choose Built-in for Select Report Data.
4. Click Next again, Select Element Scope, and then click Next again. Select output options and click Generate .

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
The Generic Table Export dialog.

You can retrieve other diagram table types by using the complete code shown below for getting values from the Generic Table:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The complete code for getting values from the Generic Table.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

You can customize line of code, e.g., by adding diagram table types to get the Metric Table or Instance Table, as shown below:

```text

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The code in this section demonstrates some scenarios with a Generic Table tool. The code may not be executable or may not return the correct result. This is only the idea of how to use the Generic Table tool and how it works.</p><p>The code shown below prints the row, column, and its value in the form of an HTML table.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="62eece43-0081-481a-9595-f5e7bbb87043"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($diagram in $project.getDiagrams("Generic Table"))
	  <table>
	  <tr>
	        #set($table = $generic.getTable($diagram))
	            <td>#</td>
	        #foreach($colName in $table.columnNames)
		            <td>$colName</td>
	  #end
	  </tr>
	  #foreach($row in $table.rows)
	        <tr>
		            <td>$foreach.count</td>
	        #foreach($col in $table.columnIds)
		            <td>$table.getValueAsString($row, $col)</td>
	        #end
	        </tr>
	    #end
	    </table>
	#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7bd4a1d6-16e2-4902-9a2f-2a4f8c66590c"><ac:rich-text-body><h4>How to learn more about the GenericTableTool</h4><hr /><ol><li>From the main menu choose <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Report Wizard</strong>.</li><li>In the Select Template pane, choose <strong>Tutorial</strong> &gt; <strong>Tutorial 27 - Generic Table Export</strong>.</li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong>, and choose <strong>Built-in </strong>for Select Report Data.</li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong> again, Select Element Scope, and then click <strong>Next</strong> again. Select output options and click <strong>Generate</strong>.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:title="Generic Table Export dialog" ac:alt="Generic Table Export dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="generic-table-export-a.png" /></ac:image><br />The Generic Table Export dialog.</h6><p>You can retrieve other diagram table types by using the complete code shown below for getting values from the Generic Table:</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:height="250"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Complete Code Tutorial.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The complete code for getting values from the Generic Table.</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a9055601-868c-46be-9f51-d9404ed2eb7b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can customize line of code, e.g., by adding diagram table types to get the Metric Table or Instance Table, as shown below:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0847f94e-cf82-430c-b1f1-bd0279d2002c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#if($diagram.diagramType == “Metric Table” || $diagram.diagramType == “Instance Table”)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

## PAGE 00185: Code examples for model validation tool

- page_id: `189144991`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189144991/Code+examples+for+model+validation+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:41:22.986+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Model validation tool
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The code in this section demonstrates some scenarios with Model Validation tool.

To print all validation results from a project by using the “UML completeness constraints” suite, for example, type the following code:

```text

```

To print only the validation results from the Class by using the “UML Correctness” suite and the "warning" severity, for example, type the following code:

```text

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The code in this section demonstrates some scenarios with Model Validation tool.</p><p>To print all validation results from a project by using the “UML completeness constraints” suite, for example, type the following code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="999f5217-ae6d-4e1d-a6b2-1e073d9b5597"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#import('validator', 'com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.ModelValidationTool')
#set ($results = $validator.validate("UML completeness constraints"))
#foreach ($result in $results)
Element: $result.element.name
Severity: $result.severity
Abbreviation: $result.abbreviation
Message: $result.message
IsIgnored: $result.isIgnored
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To print only the validation results from the Class by using the “UML Correctness” suite and the &quot;warning&quot; severity, for example, type the following code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3d92650c-baee-4090-8255-82ece5480352"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#import('validator', 'com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.ModelValidationTool')
#set ($results = $validator.validate("UML Correctness", $Class, "warning"))
#foreach ($result in $results)
Element: $result.element.name
Abbreviation: $result.abbreviation
Message: $result.message
IsIgnored: $result.isIgnored
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141359 space=MD2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00186: Collaboration

- page_id: `189141359`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141359/Collaboration
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-06T19:45:24.814+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: More Information

1357439945

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: More Information

1357439958

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: More Information

1357439947

A Collaboration describes the structure of elements that implement a certain behavior. In the Collaboration, the elements are the properties connected with the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Connector' space=''].

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Example of a collaboration

In this example, you can see the*Loan* Collaboration. The Collaboration contains two properties - *CatcherInTheRye* and *Adele*. These are the properties (attributes) and the*LibraryItem*and*AudioRecord* arethe types of each property. The *CatcherInTheRye*and *Adele* are connected with the connector relationship.

#### TIP: More Information

More Information

For more information on how to assign a behavior to a Collaboration Use, read [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Assigning Behavior diagrams automatically' space=''].

To create the property and connector in the collaboration

1. Create a Collaboration symbol [IMAGE alt='' src='']in a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space=''], you may name it *Loan*, for example.
2. On the C lass diagram , select the Collaboration, right-click on it to open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing Element Shortcut Menu' space=''] . Select the **Symbol Properties** command.
3. In the **Symbol Properties** dialog, c lick to clear the **Suppress Structure** check box. Click **Close**.
4. On the diagram pane, or in the**Model Browser**,select the elementand drag-and-drop it to the Collaboration symbol on the diagram pane.
5. The new property is created. The type of the property is the element that was dragged and dropped.
6. To create aconnectorbetween properties, expand the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Composite Structure diagram' space='']Composite Structurediagrampallet and click the button.
7. Draw the connector relationship betweenproperties.
8. Now you have the structure represented in the Collaboration.

##### Collaboration properties

You can format collaboration symbol properties in the **Symbol Properties** dialog.

#### TIP: More Information

More Information

For more information about symbol representation properties, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space=''].

You can specify collaboration properties in the collaboration Specification window. In the same window, you can find the description of each property. Descriptions are presented in the description area of the Specification window.

#### TIP: More Information

More Information

For more information about the Specification window usage, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''].

For more information about specifying property values, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing property values' space=''].

1357439941

**Related Diagrams**

- Class Diagram
- Composite Structure Diagram
- Activity
- Sequence
- State Machine
- Protocol State Machine
- Model Elements

**Related Procedures**

- Formatting Symbols
- Customizing Environment Options

**Related Refrences**

- Specification Window
- Editing property values

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c98224c3-001d-49b1-a73a-26c8897036da"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1357439945</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="85a9b7d6-aaa6-41f2-8b85-1bd80a7f5586"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1357439958</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="368bfc73-a5de-4d65-b6d4-5fe3886003a3"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1357439947</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">A Collaboration describes the structure of elements that implement a certain behavior. In the Collaboration, the elements are the properties connected with the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Connector" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[connectors]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="531"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Screen Shot 2017-06-21 at 7.05.45 PM.png" /></ac:image><span style="color: rgb(112,112,112);font-size: 12.0px;">Example of a collaboration</span></h6><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">In this example, you can see the </span><em>Loan</em> C<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">ollaboration. The Collaboration contains two properties - <em>CatcherInTheRye</em></span> <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">and <em>Adele</em></span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">. These are the properties (</span>attributes<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">) and the </span><em>LibraryItem</em><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> and </span><em>AudioRecord</em> <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">are</span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> the types of each property. The <em>CatcherInTheRye</em> </span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">and <em>Adele</em></span> <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">are connected with the connector relationship.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3359feb2-3037-4f74-839d-290db29e3ebe"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">More ri:content-title="Assigning Behavior diagrams automatically" /></ac:link>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">To create the property and connector in the collaboration</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Create a Collaboration symbol <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="100"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Screen Shot 2017-06-12 at 2.27.21 PM.png" /></ac:image>in a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Class diagram" /></ac:link>, you may name it <em>Loan</em>, for example.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">On the C</span>lass diagram<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">, select the Collaboration, right-click on it to open its </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Customizing Element Shortcut Menu" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[shortcut menu]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">.</span> <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Select the <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> command. </span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">In the <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog, c</span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">lick to clear the <strong>Suppress Structure</strong> check box.</span> <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Click <strong>Close</strong>. </span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="ded5bcb3-a7df-4b35-8b3c-a09301b35a9c">On the diagram pane, or in the </ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="ded5bcb3-a7df-4b35-8b3c-a09301b35a9c"><strong>Model Browser</strong>,</ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="ded5bcb3-a7df-4b35-8b3c-a09301b35a9c"> </ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="239b2eea-5529-4b88-9674-7d30b5bda466"><ac:inline-comment-marker <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189143535 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00187: Collaboration between disconnected teams

- page_id: `189143535`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189143535/Collaboration+between+disconnected+teams
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:40:15.561+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Using Teamwork Cloud
- labels: ['disconnected-collaboration']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1177149143

1177149146

1177149144

Teamwork Cloud server allows for collaborating on projects, supporting multiple contractors who cannot work on the same Teamwork Cloud installation. There are two ways to collaborate on projects – using either the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using server project file format' space='']server project file format *.szip***or the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using local project file format' space='']local project file format *.mdzip**.*

Read more about different ways to collaborate on projects in the following sections:

1177149142

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Saving a local copy of Teamwork Cloud project' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Branching projects' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Merge' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ca41bd61-e500-4e3f-b9ae-a24f0d7c5a53"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1177149143</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e940427f-a8d9-4081-9e36-78aae150d698"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1177149146</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2941fd7a-1c8e-4e05-a3a2-55429caa4184"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1177149144</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>Teamwork Cloud server allows for collaborating on projects, supporting multiple contractors who cannot work on the same Teamwork Cloud installation</span>. There are two ways to collaborate on projects – using either the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Using server project file format" /><ac:link-body>server project file format <em>.szip</em></ac:link-body></ac:link><em> </em>or the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Using local project file format" /><ac:link-body>local project file format <em>.mdzip</em></ac:link-body></ac:link><em>.</em></p><p>Read more about different ways to collaborate on projects in the following sections:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="614d739d-186d-44cd-a020-9d85dfe18fa8" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="442bff62-59ea-48bb-b0a8-00397156cc11"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1177149142</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Saving a local copy of Teamwork Cloud project" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Branching projects" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Model Merge" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=209257382 space=MD2024xR2 version=3 -->
## PAGE 00188: Collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform

- page_id: `209257382`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/209257382/Collaboration+powered+by+3DEXPERIENCE+platform
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-02-04T15:09:48.686+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

CATIA Magic and No Magic modeling tools support collaboration on UML/SysML/UAF models in the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform (the feature also known as Power'By). You can use a modeling tool to create and store your projects in the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform. Moreover, different team members can work on the same project simultaneously, commit new project iterations to the platform, and update the project when new iterations become available.

There are two ways you can start a collaboration session in the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform:

- Open a modeling tool and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Logging in to 3DEXPERIENCE platform' space='']log in to the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform .
- Use the Design with CATIA Magic app in the 3D EXPERIENCE platform to open a modeling tool installed on your computer.

600400

To learn how to collaborate on projects in the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform, see:

- If collaboration powered by the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform is enabled, you cannot work with Teamwork Cloud.
- Unsupported plugins:
  - Model Obfuscator.
  - Cameo Concept Modeller. Contact [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Support' space='NMDOC'] for the workaround.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>CATIA Magic and No Magic modeling tools support collaboration on UML/SysML/UAF models in the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">(the feature also known as Power'By)</span>. You can use a modeling tool to create and store your projects in the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform. Moreover, different team members can work on the same project simultaneously, commit new project iterations to the platform, and update the project when new iterations become available.</p><p>There are two ways you can start a collaboration session in the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform:</p><ul><li>Open a modeling tool and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Logging in to 3DEXPERIENCE platform" /><ac:link-body>log in to the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Use the <a href="https://help.3ds.com/2023x/english/dsdoc/CATMagcUserMap/CATMagc-c-ov.htm?contextscope=cloud">Design with CATIA Magic app</a> in the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform to open a modeling tool installed on your computer.</li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="05e9fe92-b3a8-4879-a7b6-76cce3f26998"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">600</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="http://youtube.com/watch?v=52ANuF6gOH0" /></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="height">400</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><p><br />To learn how to collaborate on projects in the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform, see:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="4b769c12-280d-4052-b68e-48138a1c9b91" /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="29d89163-9955-4e7c-a561-e0ce0574f2b5"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">If collaboration powered by the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform is enabled, you cannot work with Teamwork Cloud.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Unsupported plugins:</span><br /><ul><li>Model Obfuscator.</li><li>Cameo Concept Modeller. Contact <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="NMDOC" ri:content-title="Support" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Customer Support]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for the workaround.</li></ul></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141362 space=MD2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00189: Collaboration Use

- page_id: `189141362`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141362/Collaboration+Use
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-06T19:45:25.010+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Collaboration
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Specification Window and Property Values

1353557643

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Specification Window and Property Values

1353557654

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Specification Window and Property Values

1353557644

A Collaboration Use represents the implementation of a pattern described by a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Collaboration' space=''] in a specific situation. In this context, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency' space=''] means that the roles in the Collaboration are bound by concrete elements. A Collaboration can be used many times in different Collaboration Uses.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of multiple Collaboration Uses along with Parts

#### TIP: Model Tip

Model Tip

These instructions will work best if you have a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Composite Structure diagram' space=''] created already.

To assign a Collaboration to a Collaboration Use

Before assigning a Collaboration to a Collaboration Use, you **must** have a Collaboration created in your model.

#### INFO: Collaboration

Collaboration

Read [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Collaboration' space=''] on how to create a Collaboration in your model.

After creating a Collaboration, do one of the following:

- On the Composite Structure diagram palette, click the Collaboration Use button [ATTACHMENT filename='Screen Shot 2017-06-12 at 3.24.55 PM.png'] , and then in the opened dialog, select an existing Collaboration or click the Creation Mode button [ATTACHMENT filename='Screen Shot 2017-06-12 at 3.35.47 PM.png'] to create a new Collaboration. Click OK when you are done.
- On the Composite Structure diagram pane, create a Collaboration Use, then click the Collaboration Use shape, and then on the smart manipulator toolbar, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='Screen Shot 2017-06-13 at 6.14.09 PM.png'] symbol. Select a Collaboration. Press Enter or click anywhere on the diagram pane. The Collaboration is now created and assigned.
- On the Composite Structure diagram pane, create a Collaboration Use, then click the Collaboration Use shape, and then press Ctrl+T . Type the name of the new Collaboration and press Enter , or click anywhere on the diagram pane. The Collaboration is now created and assigned.
- On the Composite Structure diagram pane, create a Collaboration Use, then click the Collaboration Use shape, after that, click the name area. Type the “:” colon and then type the Collaboration name. Press Enter , or click anywhere on the diagram pane. The Collaboration is now created and assigned.
- In the Containment tree , select the Collaboration and drag and drop it on the diagram pane. A Collaboration Use with the assigned Collaboration is created.
- On the Composite Structure diagram pane, open the Specification window of the Collaboration Use and click the cell of the Type property value. In the opened dialog, click [ATTACHMENT filename='Screen Shot 2017-06-12 at 2.59.14 PM.png'] , and select the Collaboration. Click OK when you are done.

##### Collaboration use properties

You can format Collaboration Use symbol properties in the **Symbol Properties** dialog.

#### TIP: Formatting Symbols

Formatting Symbols

For more information about symbol representation properties, see[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space=''].

You can specify Collaboration Use properties in the Collaboration Use's **Specification window**. In the same window, you can find the description of each property. Descriptions are presented in the description area of the Specification window.

#### TIP: Specification Window and Property Values

Specification Window and Property Values

For more information about the Specification window usage, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''].

For more information about specifying property values, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing property values' space=''].

1353557641

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating a relationship' space='']

**Related Diagrams**

- [Composite Structure Diagram](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Composite+Structure+diagram)
- Activity
- Sequence
- State Machine
- Protocol State Machine
- Collaboration
- Model Elements

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="25d3f9ed-5376-4284-864c-707c30c78176"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1353557643</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b8b57cf0-0bae-481d-9cb4-47f13498674b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1353557654</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5b922cc4-221a-4021-8bf4-246d655357a9"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1353557644</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>A Collaboration Use represents the implementation of a pattern described by a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Collaboration" /></ac:link> in a specific situation. Click <strong>OK </strong>when you are done.</li><li>On the Composite Structure diagram pane, create a Collaboration Use, then click the Collaboration Use shape, and then on the smart manipulator toolbar, click the <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="16"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Screen Shot 2017-06-13 at 6.14.09 PM.png" /></ac:image>symbol. Select a Collaboration. Press <strong>Enter</strong> or click anywhere on the diagram pane. The Collaboration is now created and assigned.</li><li>On the Composite Structure diagram pane, create a Collaboration Use, then click the Collaboration Use shape, and then press <strong>Ctrl+T</strong>. Type the name of the new Collaboration and press <strong>Enter</strong>, or click anywhere on the diagram pane. The Collaboration is now created and assigned.</li><li>On the Composite Structure diagram pane, create a Collaboration Use, then click the Collaboration Use shape, after that, click the name area. Type the <strong>“:”</strong> colon and then type the Collaboration name. Press <strong>Enter</strong>, or click anywhere on the diagram pane. The Collaboration is now created and assigned.</li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="46612b47-3b62-4ad6-9ee0-b20bee82e29f">In the </ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="46612b47-3b62-4ad6-9ee0-b20bee82e29f">Containment tree</ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="46612b47-3b62-4ad6-9ee0-b20bee82e29f">, select the Collaboration and drag and drop it on the diagram pane. A Collaboration Use with the assigned Collaboration is created.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li>On the Composite Structure diagram pane, open the Specification window of the Collaboration Use and click the cell of the <strong>Type</strong> property value. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189143453 space=MD2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00190: Collaborative modeling

- page_id: `189143453`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189143453/Collaborative+modeling
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-06T19:45:52.621+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

418781380

418781391

418781378

**On this page**

418781381

INLINE

Team members can individually develop separate parts of a single model and then merge them together to form a complete model. In comparison to individual modeling, collaborative modeling is more effective and produces better results because each member employs his/her best skills and experience.

We offer you a server for your team's collaboration – [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Teamwork Cloud' space=''], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform' space='']collaboration capabilities powered by **3D**EXPERIENCE platform.

Both servers provide a repository for storing projects and users. They also support the management of users' permissions as well as sharing and versioning projects.

Projects stored in the server repository can be accessed through the network from multiple clients who have a modeling tool installed. Different users, depending on their role in a team or enterprise, can have different permissions to the projects.

The same model or even the same diagram can be accessed and modified in parallel. Every user may instantly obtain the newest version of the model as well as commit his/her own changes.

##### Basic concepts

For better understanding further material, get acquainted with basic concepts of collaborative modeling.

| Concept | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Repository | A place for storing projects and users managed by Teamwork Cloud. |
| User | A collaboration team member, who has credentials to log into Teamwork Cloud and holds permissions to access one or more server projects. |
| Teamwork Cloud project | A project stored in Teamwork Cloud repository. |
| Server project | A common name for Teamwork Cloud projects. |
| Version | A snapshot of a project at a particular point in time. A new version of a project is created after successful commit of changes made in this project. All versions of the same project have unique numbers. |
| Comment | An optional description about changes in a project version. |
| Tag | Information about the status of a project version (for example, approved or initially tested) or other. |
| Commit | A process of sending the changes made in the project to the server. Each commit creates a new project version. |
| Author | A user who has committed a particular project version. |
| Update | A process of getting from the server the latest version of a project you are working with. |
| Category | A concept for grouping projects in a Teamwork Cloud repository. |
| Locked item | A part of the model that can be edited by the user, who has locked it. Other users cannot edit locked items. The locks can be released during the commit of a new version. |
| Server project usage | Another server project used in the project you are working with. It can be a library, profile or regular project. |
| Branch | A duplicate of a server project version. Branching allows the users to work in parallel on the same project version. |
| Trunk | A permanent branch of a server project that is a base for all other branches. It cannot be deleted. |

##### Scenario to get started

Independent of what server your team or enterprise uses, we offer you to follow these steps to get started with collaborative modelling:

1. Obtain user's credentials. Creating users and deciding the access rights of the users against projects is an administrative task. For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='User Guide' space='TWCloud2024xR2'].
2. Log into a server.
3. Add a project to the server and/or open it.
4. Lock the elements you want to edit (to prevent other users could not change the elements you are working with) and edit them. You can skip this step if you are working in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Lock-Free Editing mode' space=''].
5. Edit these elements and/or create new ones.
6. Commit the changes to the server so that other users could see these modifications.

419214738

**Related pages**

****

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189142116 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00191: Collecting symbol styles from used projects

- page_id: `189142116`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189142116/Collecting+symbol+styles+from+used+projects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:38:52.026+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Formatting symbols > Stereotype properties
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

As of MagicDraw version 17.0.3, a used project in which the symbol style is defined is displayed next to the symbol style in the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting project options' space='']** and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='']**Symbol Properties**dialogs. This functionality also allows for copying a symbol style from a used project to the current project symbol style.

You can see the particular used project from which the symbol style is used and copy the symbol style from the used project.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

In the example, you can see that in the project there are two Subsystem symbol styles - one style from the *BPMN Profile.mdzip* used project and the other from the *UML_Standard_Profile.mdzip* used project. The Subsystem symbol style from the *BPMN Profile.mdzip* used project is used in the current project.

The symbol style from used project is not editable because the used project by itself is read-only. By copying the symbol style from the used project to the project you can create a new symbol style and then edit it.

Note that there are no rules for the symbol style usage from used projects - symbols styles are used according to used projectsloading order.

To copy a symbol style to the project style by using the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting project options' space='']** dialog

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting project options' space=''] dialog, select Symbol styles > Default > Stereotypes options group, choose a symbol style that you want to copy. [ATTACHMENT filename='styles_from_modules3.png']
2. Click the Copy to Project Style button. The new symbol style is created in the project. This symbol style is now used in the project and this is the editable symbol style. [ATTACHMENT filename='styles_from_modules4.png']

To copy a symbol style from a used project in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='']**Symbol Properties** dialog

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='']**Symbol Properties** dialog , click the Apply Style button and from the drop-down list, select a style from a used project that you want to copy.
2. Click the Make Default button.
3. Edit the symbol style.
4. Click OK to close the dialog.
5. Reopen the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='']**Symbol Properties** dialog . In the expanded Apply Style drop-down list, you can see the newly created symbol style. This is the copy of the selected style from the used project.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The Subsystem symbol style from the <em>BPMN Profile.mdzip</em> used project is used in the current project.</p><p>The symbol style from used project is not editable because the used project by itself is read-only. By copying the symbol style from the used project to the project you can create a new symbol style and then edit it.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="df5cd90c-d7b3-4c46-a538-203c80a67c22"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Note that there are no rules for the symbol style usage from used projects - symbols styles are used according to <span>used projects </span>loading order.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p>To copy a symbol style to the project style by using the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Setting project options" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Project Options]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> dialog</p><hr /><ol><li>In the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Setting project options" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Project Options]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> dialog, select <strong>Symbol</strong> <strong>styles</strong> &gt; <strong>Default</strong> &gt; <strong>Stereotypes</strong> options group, choose a symbol style that you want to copy.<br /><ac:image ac:title="Example of copying symbol style from module" ac:alt="Example of copying symbol style from module"><ri:attachment ri:filename="styles_from_modules3.png" /></ac:image></li><li>Click the <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Copy to Project Style</strong> button. The new symbol style is created in the project. This symbol style is now used in the project and this is the editable symbol style.<br /><ac:image ac:title=" Example of copied symbol style" ac:alt=" Example of copied symbol style"><ri:attachment ri:filename="styles_from_modules4.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><p> </p><p>To copy a symbol style from a used project in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Symbol Properties dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link></p><hr /><ol><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Symbol Properties dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>, click the Apply Style button and from the drop-down list, select a style from a used project that you want to copy.</li><li>Click the <strong>Make Default</strong> button.</li><li>Edit the symbol style.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> to close the dialog.</li><li>Reopen the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Symbol Properties dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>. In the expanded Apply Style drop-down list, you can see the newly created symbol style. This is the copy of the selected style from the used project.</li></ol><p><ac:image ac:title="Example of copied symbol style that is listed in Symbol Properties dialog" ac:alt="Example of copied symbol style that is listed in Symbol Properties dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="styles_from_modules7.png" /></ac:image></p><p> </p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141458 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00192: Combined Fragment

- page_id: `189141458`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141458/Combined+Fragment
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:38:15.443+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Fragment
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

876860645

876860648

876860646

Our modeling tool represents twelve kinds of fragments: Alternatives, Loop, Option, Parallel, Break, Negative, Critical Region, Consider, Ignore, Weak Sequencing, Strict Sequencing, and Assertion.

The UML Combined Fragment element allows the expressions of interaction fragments to be defined in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='']. The Combined Fragments provide a means to define particular conditions and subprocesses for any sections of lifelines in the Sequence diagram by specifying an area where the conditions or subprocesses apply. Using the Combined Fragment, a fragment of a Sequence diagram can be separated.

To draw a Combined Fragment

1. In the Sequence diagram toolbar, select the Alternatives element to draw.
2. From the Combined Fragment shortcut menu, select the Covered Lifelines command and in the Covered Lifelines dialog, select the lifelines to display.
3. In the Sequence diagram toolbar, in the Options group select the Parallel Combined Fragment to draw.
4. Draw the Critical Region Combined Fragment from the Sequence diagram toolbar, in the Option button group.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

876860644

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Alternative Fragment' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Fragment' space='']

The Combined Fragments provide a means to define particular conditions and subprocesses for any sections of lifelines in the Sequence diagram by specifying an area where the conditions or subprocesses apply. Using the Combined Fragment, a fragment of a Sequence diagram can be separated. </p><p><br /></p><p>To draw a Combined Fragment</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Sequence diagram toolbar, select the Alternatives element to draw.</li><li>From the Combined Fragment shortcut menu, select the <strong>Covered Lifelines</strong> command and in the Covered Lifelines dialog, select the lifelines to display.</li><li>In the Sequence diagram toolbar, in the <strong>Options</strong> group select the <strong>Parallel</strong> Combined Fragment to draw. </li><li>Draw the <strong>Critical Region</strong> Combined Fragment from the Sequence diagram toolbar, in the <strong>Option</strong> button group. </li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Screen Shot 2017-06-21 at 6.08.00 PM.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cd976d2d-6bed-4b89-8be6-f75277a7b1be"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">876860644</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Formatting symbols" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Sequence diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Alternative Fragment" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Fragment" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
