<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189139836 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00209: Composite Structure diagram elements

- page_id: `189139836`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189139836/Composite+Structure+diagram+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:36:26.850+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Importing data from other UML tools and formats > Enterprise Architect Import > Special Transformation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

559286067

559286070

559286066

**On this page**

3

559286069

This section presents additional EA specific Composite Structure diagram information.

##### Import elements

EA differs from MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM in the Composite Structure diagram's content elements design. The table below shows the differences by focusing on the EA’s elements and how they will be transformed or converted into a format MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM can correctly load and display. The element names shown in the table are the same in both EA and MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM GUI. The contents in the brackets ([…]) are the XMI element type references.

| Enterprise Architect | MagicDraw, CEA, CSM |
| --- | --- |
| Interaction |  |
| Class [uml:Class] | Class [uml:Class] |
| Interface [uml:Interface] | Interface [uml:Interface] |
| Part [uml:Class]Part [uml:Property] | Part [uml:Property] |
| Port [uml:Port] | Port [uml:Port] |
| Collaboration [uml:Collaboration] | Collaboration Use [uml:Collaboration] |
| Expose Interface [not exist] |  |
| Connector [element type is not exist] | Connector [uml:Connector] |
| Assembly [element type is not exist] |  |
| Delegate [element type is not exist] | Connector [uml:Connector] |
| Role Binding [uml:Dependency] | Role Binding [uml:Dependency] |
| Represents [uml:Dependency] | Dependency [uml:Dependency] |
| Occurrence [uml:Dependency] | Dependency [uml:Dependency] |

##### Conversion details

###### Dummy class

In MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Composite Structure diagram' space=''] needs a Context element to contain itself. However, in EA there is no Context element. After conversion, a dummy [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='']element will be created to represent the Context element of Composite Structure diagram.

A dummy Class will be named after the closest owner package of the Composite Structure diagram. The Composite Structure diagram and all of the Composite Structure elements will be placed inside the dummy Class element.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Dummy class.

###### Class

The Class element data will be normally copied even if the Class element is drawn in a Composite Structure diagram. A Property element will be created to represent the Class element. The Property element will be named after the Class element.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Property Representing a Class Element.

###### Class with Attributes and Operations

The Class view in EA will be converted to a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part' space='']in MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Attribute' space='']and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operation' space='']will not be shown in the Composite Structure diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Part Element after Conversion.

###### Interface

The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interface' space='']element data will be normally copied even if the Interface element is drawn in a Composite Structure diagram. A Property element will be created to represent the Interface element. The Property element will be named after the Interface element.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Property Representing Interface Element.

###### Part

There are two types of [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part' space='']:

- Part with a port; and
- Part that sets a type to other elements.

###### **Part with Port**

If you assign the type of a Part element to a Class, Component, or Node, the Part element can have a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Port' space='']. If a Part is not nested to any element, the Part element will be exported from EA as a Class. Therefore, this Part element can have a Port.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Part that does not Nest Element(s) with Ports.

If a Part is nested to an element and the type is not set, then the Part type will be used to set type to a dummy Class. This particular Part can have a Port. A dummy Class will be created at the same level of the Part element that sets type to it. The dummy Class will be named after the Part concatenating with *_type*.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Part that Nests Element(s) with Post.

###### Part that sets type to other elements

The type of a Part can be set to another element (such as a Class, Actor, Component, or Usecase) by right-clicking the Part element and selecting **Advanced**> **Set Property Type**> **Select Property Type.**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Setting Part Type.

If the Part type is set to a type that cannot be the owner of a port, the port will be removed.

In MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM, the Property element data cannot have any elements. However, in EA, it can have elements. Therefore, if you draw a Part element and it has an element inside it, that particular element will be relocated to an element, which is the Part type.

If a Part is not nested to any element, the Part element will be exported from EA as a Class. The element inside the Part will not be relocated. If that particular Part assigns a type to other elements, its type will always be set to Class.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Part that Sets Type to Other Elements.

If a Nested Part whose type is set to another element (such as a Class, Actor, or Usecase) and this Part contains Nested elements, all of the Nested elements of the Part will be relocated to the element that is the type of this Part.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Part that Sets Type to Other Elements has Nested Element Inside Itself.

###### portPort

In MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM, a Property element that can have a Port is the Property that sets type to a Class, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Component' space=''], or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Node' space=''], and other elements cannot have a Port. However, in EA, most of the elements can have a Port.

If a Port is created with an element that is not a Class, Component or Node, the Port will be removed from the diagram view. However, its data will be preserved.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Only Class, Component, and Node Can Have a Port.

The following transformation message will open:

Removed view <xmi:id>: Invalid Port. Port can be added to Part that its type is set to Class, Component and Node only.

###### Port whose type is set to other elements

You can set the type of Port element to another element (a Class, Actor, Component, or Usecase) by right-clicking the Port element and selecting **Advanced**> **Set Property Type**> **Select Property Type.**

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

###### Setting Port Type.

###### Port whose type is set to another element and containing Expose Interfaces

If an Expose Interface is created on a Port and the Port type is set to another element, the Expose Interface data will be relocated to the Port's type element. For example, if the Port1 type is set to Actor, an Interface Realization (the Expose Interface data) will be placed inside the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actor' space='']after conversion.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Port that Sets Type to Other Element Contains Expose Interface.****

###### Collaboration

The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Collaboration' space='']element data and its nested data will normally be copied. However, if the Collaboration element is drawn in a Composite Structure diagram, a Collaboration use element will be created to represent the Collaboration element. The Collaboration use element will be named after the Collaboration element.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Collaboration use.

**Collaboration containing other elements**

A Collaboration can contain only Property, Activity, State Machine, and Interaction. Elements other than these will be removed. If any element is drawn inside the Collaboration, it will be removed from the diagram view.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Collaboration Containing Other Elements.

The following transformation messages will open:

Removed element <xmi:id>: Invalid Element. Collaboration can contain Property, Activity, State Machine and Interaction only.Removed view <xmi:id>: Invalid Element view. Collaboration cannot be contained in any element view.

###### Expose Interface

Expose Interfaces are the Provided and Required interfaces in MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM. You can draw a Provided Interface or Required Interface in a Port only and set the type of the Port to another element (an Actor, Use Case, or Class). You can create that particular Port on a Part element whose type is set to a Class, Component, or Node only.

###### Expose Interface with Port

If you draw an Expose Interface in a Port and the Port does not assign a type to any elements, that particular Port will be used to set type to a dummy Class. The dummy Class will be created and named after the Port concatenating with *_type*. The Interface element will be moved to the same level of the Port.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Expose Interface with Port.

If an Expose Interface is created on a Port and the type of the Port is set to another element, the Expose Interface data will be relocated to the Port's type element (see whose type is set to another element and containing Expose Interfaces for more information).

In MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM, some elements can be used as a Port type. These elements can have either a Provided Interface or Required Interface, or both.

The following is a list of the Port's type elements with specific conditions:

- Interfaces can only have a Provided Interface.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Artifact' space=''] cannot have a Provided Interface.
- Information Items cannot have a Provided Interface.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Signal Event' space=''] cannot have a Provided Interface.
- Components always have both Provided and Required Interfaces.
- The other elements can have both Provided and Required Interfaces.
- After conversion, if the type of a Port is set to an Interface and the Port has an Expose Interface, only the Provided Interface will be shown and the Interface Realization data will be removed.
- Artifacts, Information Items, and Signals cannot hold Interface Realization. If the type of a Port is set to one of them, the Provided Interface will be removed. However, this does not affect the Required Interface.
- If the type of a Port is set to a Component and the Port has an Expose Interface, it will always show both the Provided and Required Interfaces.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Port’s Type Element with Specific Conditions.

The following transformation message will open:

Updated element <xmi:id>: Provided Interface conflicts with Port type. The Port type is updated to an owner of Interface Realization.

If an Expose Interface is created with an element that is not a Port, the element will be removed both from the Containment tree and diagram pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Expose Interface with Element that is not Port.

The following transformation message will open:

Removed element <xmi:id>: Invalid Element. Expose Interface can be added to Port only.

###### Expose Interface with relationship

In MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM, the Provided and Required Interfaces cannot be connected with any relationship. If there is any relationship connecting to the Expose Interface, it will be removed from both the Containment tree and diagram pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Expose Interface with Lines.

The following transformation message will open:

Removed element <xmi:id>: Invalid Element. Expose Interface cannot be connected with the element.

###### Package

In MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM, a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package' space='']cannot be drawn in a Composite Structure diagram. If it is drawn in EA, it will be removed from the diagram once it has been converted to MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Package.

###### Assembly

In MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM, there is no Assembly line. It will be updated to a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Connector' space=''].

The following transformation message will open:

Updated element <xmi:id>: Assembly updated to Connector.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Updating the Assembly Line to the Connector Line.

###### Dependency

There are many relationships that EA exporter exports to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency' space=''] as the following elements:

- Delegate
- Role Binding
- Represents
- Occurrence
- Nest
- Derive
- Import
- Instantiate
- Usage
- Realize
- Trace

The above relationships will be shown in the diagram view as Dependencies with a stereotype.

###### Removed relationships

If an Association, Direct Association, Aggregation, Composition, Generalization, or Specialization is created in a Composite Structure diagram, it will be removed from the diagram view, but its data will be preserved.

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

###### Removing lines.

The following transformation messages will open:

Removed view <xmi:id>: Association cannot be shown in Composite Structure diagram.Removed view <xmi:id>: Direct Association cannot be shown in Composite Structure diagram.Removed view <xmi:id>: Aggregation cannot be shown in Composite Structure diagram.Removed view <xmi:id>: Composition cannot be shown in Composite Structure diagram.Removed view <xmi:id>: Generalization cannot be shown in Composite Structure diagram.

##### Transformation report

A report containing the conflicts solved during transformation, along with other transformation information (such as special mapping and removal of some irrelevant data), is required to be provided to the users.

The following is a list of the transformation messages:

Removed view <xmi:id>: Invalid Port. Port can be added to Part that set type to Class, Component, and Node only.Removed element <xmi:id>: Invalid Element. Collaboration can contain Property, Activity, State Machine, and Interaction only.Removed view <xmi:id>: Collaboration cannot be contained in any element view.Removed view <xmi:id>: UseCase cannot be contained in any element view.Removed element <xmi:id>: Invalid Element. Expose Interface can be added to Port only.Removed element <xmi:id>: Invalid Element. Expose Interface cannot be connected with the element.Updated element <xmi:id>: Assembly updated to Connector.Updated element <xmi:id>: Provided Interface conflicts with Port type. The Port type is updated to an owner of Interface Realization.Removed view <xmi:id>: Association cannot be shown in CompositeStructure diagram.Removed view <xmi:id>: Direct Association cannot be shown in Composite Structure diagram.Removed view <xmi:id>: Aggregation cannot be shown in Composite Structure diagram.Removed view <xmi:id>: Composition cannot be shown in Composite Structure diagram.Removed view <xmi:id>: Generalization cannot be shown in Composite Structure diagram.

559293207

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Composite Structure diagram' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="158f7b73-1490-48a9-a999-4df2fe58b187"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">559286067</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="62ac6b24-c1ed-4e94-9c0d-fe56da72572b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">559286070</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e56e68c8-3d58-444e-98f7-591b87e1679c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">559286066</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4913ad71-84d1-4193-be83-4f29b776f185"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9578db90-5035-4e33-a77a-fdc90bb7e706"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">559286069</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><br /></p><p>This section presents additional EA specific Composite Structure diagram information.</p><h3>Import elements</h3><p>EA differs from MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM in the Composite Structure diagram's content elements design. The table below shows the differences by focusing on the EA’s elements and how they will be transformed or converted into a format MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM can correctly load and display. The element names shown in the table are the same in both EA and MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM GUI. The contents in the brackets ([…]) are the XMI element type references.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Enterprise Architect</th><th>MagicDraw, CEA, CSM</th></tr><tr><td>Interaction</td><td><br /></td></tr><tr><td><span>Class [uml:Class]</span></td><td><span>Class [uml:Class] </span></td></tr><tr><td><span>Interface [uml:Interface]</span></td><td><p>Interface [uml:Interface]</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Part [uml:Class]</p><p>Part [uml:Property]</p></td><td><p>Part [uml:Property]</p></td></tr><tr><td><span>Port<span> </span>[uml:Port]</span></td><td><span>Port<span> </span>[uml:Port]</span></td></tr><tr><td><span>Collaboration [uml:Collaboration]</span></td><td><span>Collaboration Use [uml:Collaboration] </span></td></tr><tr><td><span>Expose Interface [not<span> </span>exist]</span></td><td><br /></td></tr><tr><td><span>Connector [element type is not exist]</span></td><td><span>Connector [uml:Connector] </span></td></tr><tr><td><span>Assembly [element type is not exist]</span></td><td><br /></td></tr><tr><td><span>Delegate [element type is not exist]</span></td><td><span>Connector [uml:Connector]</span></td></tr><tr><td><span>Role Binding [uml:Dependency]</span></td><td><span>Role Binding [uml:Dependency] </span></td></tr><tr><td><span>Represents [uml:Dependency]</span></td><td><span>Dependency [uml:Dependency] </span></td></tr><tr><td><span>Occurrence [uml:Dependency]            <span> </span></span></td><td><span>Dependency [uml:Dependency]</span></td></tr></tbody></table><h3>Conversion details</h3><h4>Dummy class</h4><p>In MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM, the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Composite Structure diagram" /></ac:link> needs a Context element to contain itself. However, in EA there is no Context element. After conversion, a dummy <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Class" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Class ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>element will be created to represent the Context element of Composite Structure diagram.</p><p>A dummy Class will be named after the closest owner package of the Composite Structure diagram. The Composite Structure diagram and all of the Composite Structure elements will be placed inside the dummy Class element.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="dummyClass.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Dummy class.</h6><h4>Class</h4><p>The Class element data will be normally copied even if the Class element is drawn in a Composite Structure diagram. A Property element will be created  to represent the Class element. The Property element will be named after the Class element.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="propertyrepresent_class.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Property Representing a Class Element.</h6><h5>Class with Attributes and Operations</h5><p>The Class view in EA will be converted to a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Part" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Part ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>in MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Attribute" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Attributes ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Operations ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>will not be shown in the Composite Structure diagram.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="partElement_converted.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Part Element after Conversion.</h6><h4>Interface</h4><p>The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Interface ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>element data will be normally copied even if the Interface element is drawn in a Composite Structure diagram. A Property element will be created to represent the Interface element. The Property element will be named after the Interface element.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="propertyrepresent_interface.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Property Representing Interface Element.</h6><h4>Part</h4><p>There are two types of <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Part" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Parts]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>:</p><ul><li>Part with a port; and </li><li>Part that sets a type to other elements.</li></ul><h5><strong>Part with Port</strong></h5><p>If you assign the type of a Part element to a Class, Component, or Node, the Part element can have a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Port" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Port ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. If a Part is not nested to any element, the Part element will be exported from EA as a Class. Therefore, this Part element can have a Port.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="part_withoutNestElement.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Part that does not Nest Element(s) with Ports.</h6><p>If a Part is nested to an element and the type is not set, then the Part type will be used to set type to a dummy Class. This particular Part can have a Port. A dummy Class will be created at the same level of the Part element that sets type to it. The dummy Class will be named after the Part concatenating with <em>_type</em>.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="part_withNestElement.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Part that Nests Element(s) with Post.</h6><h5>Part that sets type to other elements</h5><p>The type of a Part can be set to another element (such as  a Class, Actor, Component, or Usecase) by right-clicking the Part element and selecting  <strong>Advanced </strong>&gt; <strong>Set Property Type </strong>&gt; <strong>Select Property Type.</strong></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="partType.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Setting Part Type.</h6><p>If the Part type is set to a type that cannot be the owner of a port, the port will be removed.</p><p>In MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM, the Property element data cannot have any elements. However, in EA, it can have elements. Therefore, if you draw a Part element and it has an element inside it, that particular element will be relocated to an element, which is the Part type.</p><p>If a Part is not nested to any element, the Part element will be exported from EA as a Class. The element inside the Part will not be relocated. If that particular Part assigns a type to other elements, its type will always be set to Class.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="part_setType_otherElement.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Part that Sets Type to Other Elements.</h6><p>If a Nested Part whose type is set to another element (such as a Class, Actor, or Usecase) and this Part contains Nested elements, all of the Nested elements of the Part will be relocated to the element that is the type of this Part.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="part_setType_otherElementwithNest.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Part that Sets Type to Other Elements has Nested Element Inside Itself.</h6><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bfb33356-5197-4c86-acec-96a4507efd85"><ac:parameter ac:name="">port</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Port</h4><p>In MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM, a Property element that can have a Port is the Property that sets type to a Class, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Component" /></ac:link>, or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Node" /></ac:link>, and other elements cannot have a Port. However, in EA, most of the elements can have a Port.</p><p>If a Port is created with an element that is not a Class, Component or Node, the Port will be removed from the diagram view. However, its data will be preserved.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="class_component_nodewithport.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Only Class, Component, and Node Can Have a Port.</h6><p>The following transformation message will open:</p><blockquote><pre>Removed view &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Invalid Port. Port can be added to Part that its type is set to Class, Component and Node only.</pre></blockquote><h5>Port whose type is set to other elements</h5><p>You can set the type of Port element to another element (a Class, Actor, Component, or Usecase) by right-clicking the Port element and selecting <strong>Advanced </strong>&gt; <strong>Set Property Type </strong>&gt; <strong>Select Property Type.</strong></p><p><strong><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="portType.png" /></ac:image><br /></strong></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Setting Port Type.</h6><h5>Port whose type is set to another element and containing Expose Interfaces</h5><p>If an Expose Interface is created on a Port and the Port type is set to another element, the Expose Interface data will be relocated to the Port's type element. For example, if the Port1 type is set to Actor, an Interface Realization (the Expose Interface data) will be placed inside the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Actor" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Actor ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>after conversion.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="port_setTypetoElement.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Port that Sets Type to Other Element Contains Expose Interface.<strong><br /></strong></h6><h4>Collaboration</h4><p>The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Collaboration" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Collaboration ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>element data and its nested data will normally be copied. However, if the Collaboration element is drawn in a Composite Structure diagram, a Collaboration use element will be created to represent the Collaboration element. The Collaboration use element will be named after the Collaboration element.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="collaborationUse.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Collaboration use.</h6><p><strong>Collaboration containing other elements</strong></p><p>A Collaboration can contain only Property, Activity, State Machine, and Interaction. Elements other than these will be removed. If any element is drawn inside the Collaboration, it will be removed from the diagram view.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="collaboration_containElement.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Collaboration Containing Other Elements.</h6><p>The following transformation messages will open:</p><blockquote><ul><li><pre>Removed element &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Invalid Element. Collaboration can contain Property, Activity, State Machine and Interaction only.</pre></li><li><pre>Removed view &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Invalid Element view. Collaboration cannot be contained in any element view.</pre></li></ul></blockquote><h4>Expose Interface</h4><p>Expose Interfaces are the Provided and Required interfaces in MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM. You can draw a Provided Interface or Required Interface in a Port only and set the type of the Port to another element (an Actor, Use Case, or Class). You can create that particular Port on a Part element whose type is set to a Class, Component, or Node only.</p><h5>Expose Interface with Port</h5><p>If you draw an Expose Interface in a Port and the Port does not assign a type to any elements, that particular Port will be used to set type to a dummy Class. The dummy Class will be created and named after the Port concatenating with <em>_type</em>. The Interface element will be moved to the same level of the Port.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="exposeInterface_withPort.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: right;">Expose Interface with Port.</h6><p>If an Expose Interface is created on a Port and the type of the Port is set to another element, the Expose Interface data will be relocated to the Port's type element (see <ac:link ac:anchor="port"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Port ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>whose type is set to another element and containing Expose Interfaces for more information).</p><p>In MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM, some elements can be used as a Port type. These elements can have either a Provided Interface or Required Interface, or both.</p><p>The following is a list of the Port's type elements with specific conditions:</p><ul><li>Interfaces can only have a Provided Interface.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Artifact" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Artifacts ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>cannot have a Provided Interface.</li><li>Information Items cannot have a Provided Interface.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Signal Event" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Signals ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>cannot have a Provided Interface.</li><li>Components always have both Provided and Required Interfaces.</li><li>The other elements can have both Provided and Required Interfaces.</li><li>After conversion, if the type of a Port is set to an Interface and the Port has an Expose Interface, only the Provided Interface will be shown and the Interface Realization data will be removed.</li><li>Artifacts, Information Items, and Signals cannot hold Interface Realization. If the type of a Port is set to one of them, the Provided Interface will be removed. However, this does not affect the Required Interface.</li><li>If the type of a Port is set to a Component and the Port has an Expose Interface, it will always show both the Provided and Required Interfaces.</li></ul><h6><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="port_withTypeElement.png" /></ac:image></h6><h6 style="text-align: center;">Port’s Type Element with Specific Conditions.</h6><p>The following transformation message will open:</p><blockquote><pre>Updated element &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Provided Interface conflicts with Port type. The Port type is updated to an owner of Interface Realization.</pre></blockquote><p>If an Expose Interface is created with an element that is not a Port, the element will be removed both from the Containment tree and diagram pane.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="exposeInterface_withElement.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Expose Interface with Element that is not Port.</h6><p>The following transformation message will open:</p><blockquote><pre>Removed element &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Invalid Element. Expose Interface can be added to Port only.</pre></blockquote><h5>Expose Interface with relationship</h5><p>In MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM, the Provided and Required Interfaces cannot be connected with any relationship. If there is any relationship connecting to the Expose Interface, it will be removed from both the Containment tree and diagram pane.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="exposeInterface_withLines.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Expose Interface with Lines.</h6><p>The following transformation message will open:</p><blockquote><pre>Removed element &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Invalid Element. Expose Interface cannot be connected with the element.</pre></blockquote><h4>Package</h4><p>In MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM, a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Package" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Package ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>cannot be drawn in a Composite Structure diagram. If it is drawn in EA, it will be removed from the diagram once it has been converted to MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="package.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Package.</h6><h4>Assembly</h4><p>In MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM, there is no Assembly line. It will be updated to a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Connector" /></ac:link>.</p><p>The following transformation message will open:</p><blockquote><pre>Updated element &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Assembly updated to Connector.</pre></blockquote><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="assemblyLine_toConnectorLine.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Updating the Assembly Line to the Connector Line.</h6><h4>Dependency</h4><p>There are many relationships that EA exporter exports to <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Dependency" /></ac:link> as the following elements:</p><ul><li>Delegate</li><li>Role Binding</li><li>Represents</li><li>Occurrence</li><li>Nest</li><li>Derive</li><li>Import</li><li>Instantiate</li><li>Usage</li><li>Realize</li><li>Trace</li></ul><p>The above relationships will be shown in the diagram view as Dependencies with a stereotype.</p><h4>Removed relationships</h4><p>If an Association, Direct Association, Aggregation, Composition, Generalization, or Specialization is created in a Composite Structure diagram, it will be removed from the diagram view, but its data will be preserved.</p><p><strong><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="removeLines.png" /></ac:image><br /></strong></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Removing lines.</h6><p>The following transformation messages will open:</p><blockquote><ul><li><pre>Removed view &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Association cannot be shown in Composite Structure diagram.</pre></li><li><pre>Removed view &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Direct Association cannot be shown in Composite Structure diagram.</pre></li><li><pre>Removed view &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Aggregation cannot be shown in Composite Structure diagram.</pre></li><li><pre>Removed view &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Composition cannot be shown in Composite Structure diagram.</pre></li><li><pre>Removed view &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Generalization cannot be shown in Composite Structure diagram.</pre></li></ul></blockquote><h3>Transformation report</h3><p>A report containing the conflicts solved during transformation, along with other transformation information (such as special mapping and removal of some irrelevant data), is required to be provided to the users.</p><p>The following is a list of the transformation messages:</p><blockquote><ul><li><pre>Removed view &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Invalid Port. Port can be added to Part that set type to Class, Component, and Node only.</pre></li><li><pre>Removed element &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Invalid Element. Collaboration can contain Property, Activity, State Machine, and Interaction only.</pre></li><li><pre>Removed view &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Collaboration cannot be contained in any element view.</pre></li><li><pre>Removed view &lt;xmi:id&gt;: UseCase cannot be contained in any element view.</pre></li><li><pre>Removed element &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Invalid Element. Expose Interface can be added to Port only.</pre></li><li><pre>Removed element &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Invalid Element. Expose Interface cannot be connected with the element.</pre></li><li><pre>Updated element &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Assembly updated to Connector.</pre></li><li><pre>Updated element &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Provided Interface conflicts with Port type. The Port type is updated to an owner of Interface Realization.</pre></li><li><pre>Removed view &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Association cannot be shown in CompositeStructure diagram.</pre></li><li><pre>Removed view &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Direct Association cannot be shown in Composite Structure diagram.</pre></li><li><pre>Removed view &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Aggregation cannot be shown in Composite Structure diagram.</pre></li><li><pre>Removed view &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Composition cannot be shown in Composite Structure diagram.</pre></li><li><pre>Removed view &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Generalization cannot be shown in Composite Structure diagram.</pre></li></ul></blockquote></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9f3bce0e-d6b4-4f89-8bf3-c27fcd3116b7"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">559293207</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Composite Structure diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189144587 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00210: Concepts

- page_id: `189144587`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189144587/Concepts
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:41:02.858+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Generating Reports from Report Wizard
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Report Wizard includes the following concepts:

- Template specifies the document layout, format, corresponding model elements, and custom defined fields.
- Report Data specifies a set of custom fields and data in the selected template. A template can have a number of different custom defined fields for a project or different projects organized in a report.
- Report a generated user document with data from the project displayed in the selected template style.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Report Wizard includes the following concepts:</p><ul><li><strong>Template<br /></strong>specifies the document layout, format, corresponding model elements, and custom defined fields.</li><li><strong>Report Data</strong> <br />specifies a set of custom fields and data in the selected template. A template can have a number of different custom <br />defined fields for a project or different projects organized in a report.</li><li><strong>Report</strong> <br />a generated user document with data from the project displayed in the selected template style.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189139682 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00211: Configuration files

- page_id: `189139682`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189139682/Configuration+files
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:36:17.147+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Getting started
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Sample property value

1228358741

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Sample property value

1228358744

1228387255

**On this page**

4

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Sample property value

1228358743

#### EXCERPT: Sample property value

INLINE

A configuration file is where the initial parameters and settings of a program are defined. All default parameter values come with a program installation but you can edit configuration files and define your own values, for example, define the Java home directory. This page describes the locations where you can find the configuration files of your modeling tool, and how you can modify them and change their default storage location. Configuration files can be used to:

- Define application arguments
- Set the Java Home
- Change Java parameters (such as memory allocation)
- Update default Java classpath (advanced: for plugin developers)

The configuration files can be opened and modified using any standard text or code editor. Once modified, changing the default location of configuration files can allow you to store multiple configuration files for different versions of the tool.

This page covers all modeling tools:

- MagicDraw
- Cameo Enterprise Architecture
- Cameo Systems Modeler
- Magic Software Architect
- Magic Cyber Systems Engineer
- Magic Systems of Systems Architect

By default the configuration and auxiliary files of a modeling tool are stored in the following location:

| OS | Configuration files storage |
| --- | --- |
| WindowsVista/7/8/10 | C:\Users\<USERNAME>\AppData\Local\.<modeling tool name>\<modeling tool version number> |
| Other OS | <user.home>/.<modeling tool name>/< modeling tool version number> |

#### INFO: Modeling tool file naming

Modeling tool file naming

In a file system, modeling tools' folders are named as follows:

| Modeling tool | File name |
| --- | --- |
| MagicDraw | magicdraw |
| Cameo Enterprise Architecture | cameoea |
| Cameo Systems Modeler | cameo.systems.modeler |
| Magic Software Architect | magic.software.architect |
| Magic Cyber Systems Engineer | magic.cyber.systems.engineer |
| Magic Systems of Systems Architect | magic.systems.of.systems.architect |

##### Getting the path to configuration files

If you do not know the exact path to your modeling tool configuration files, follow the steps below to get it.

To get the path to the configuration files

1. From the Help menu, select About <modeling tool name>. The About dialog opens.
2. Click the Environment tab.
3. Click the hyperlink next to Configuration Files . The folder containing modeling tool configuration files opens.

You can also save configuration files in any of the following locations:

- <modeling tool installation directory>
- Common Application Data location (on Windows OS)
- Another directory

##### Storing configuration files in the installation directory or Common Application Data location on Windows

#### NOTE: Note

Note

The Common Application Data location on Windows Vista/7/8/10 is *C:\ProgramData*.

To store configuration files in the modeling tool installation directory or Common Application Data location on Windows

1. Open the <modeling tool name>.properties file, which is located in <modeling tool installation directory>\bin .
2. In the JAVA_ARGS line, change the -DLOCALCONFIG property value according to the following table:

| -DLOCALCONFIG | =true | =false |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Windows OSx | Common Application Data | Modeling tool installation directory |
| Other | <user.home>/.<modeling tool name>/< modeling tool version number> | Modeling tool installation directory |

#### WARNING: Important!

Important!

It is important to add the **-DWINCONFIG=false** parameter on Window OS, otherwise, the configuration files will not be stored in the modeling tool installation directory.

You may also store configuration files in your custom location.

##### Storing configuration files in a custom location

#### NOTE: Note

Note

If you have defined to store files in the modeling tool installation directory, files will not be stored in the defined location.

To store modeling tool configuration files in a custom location

Do either:

1. Open the <modeling tool name>.properties file, which is located in <modeling tool installation directory>\bin .
2. In the JAVA_ARGS line, add the following property: -Dlocalconfig.location=<absolute path to a custom location> Sample property value-Dlocalconfig.location\=E\:\\temp\\innerPack

Or:

1. Go to the folder, where .
2. Create a new file named *magicdrawredirect*.
3. In the file, type the absolute path to the folder, wherein modeling configuration files will be saved, e.g., C:/<directory name>.

1228358739

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing environment options' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing and Selecting Perspective' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189143798 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00212: Configure used projects

- page_id: `189143798`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189143798/Configure+used+projects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:40:24.344+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Using Teamwork Cloud > Managing project usages
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can see all used projects and their versions In the **Advanced Project Usages Configuration** dialog.

To open the **Advanced Project Usages Configuration** dialog

1. Login to the Teamwork Cloud server.
2. Open your server project.
3. On the main menu, click Collaborate > Project Usages > Project Usages . The Project Usages dialog opens.
4. Click the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The ****Advanced Project Usages Configuration**** dialog opens. Search barYou can use the search bar to faster filter used projects by name. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

In this dialog you can make various modifications for one or more projects at a time. Select one or more used projects and:

- Click the Notify on New Version check-box to get notifications when a new version of a particular project is available on the server.
- Use the commands in the toolbar or shortcut menu to:
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Start using a project in your project' space=''] to the server project you are working with
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stop using the project in your project' space=''] from the server project you are working with.
  - Start using an indirectly used project directly.
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Changing the used project version' space=''] of a selected used project.
  - Review the information of a selected used project.
  - Reset changes to a selected used project. You must do this before applying selected actions!

In the **Action**column, you can see the action to be performed on a particular project after the **Apply** button is clicked.

To turn the action on, click one of the following:

- Apply - to apply changes and keep the dialog open in order to see what changes are applied. Please note, the applied changes cannot be undone.
- OK - to apply changes and close the dialog.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can see all used projects and their versions In the <strong>Advanced Project Usages Configuration</strong> dialog.</p><p><br /></p><p>To open the <strong>Advanced Project Usages Configuration</strong> dialog</p><hr /><ol><li>Login to the Teamwork Cloud server.</li><li>Open your server project.</li><li>On the main menu, click <strong>Collaborate </strong>&gt; <strong>Project Usages</strong> &gt; <strong>Project Usages</strong>. The <strong>Project Usages</strong> dialog opens.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click the <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="17"><ri:attachment ri:filename="usages_configuration.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button. The <strong><strong>Advanced Project Usages Configuration</strong></strong> dialog opens.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d2f9b9a6-313b-47ff-b4ef-12b29d090646"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Search bar</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">You can use the search bar to faster filter used projects by name.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="advanced_project_usages_configuration_dialog.png" /></ac:image></p></li></ol><p>In this dialog you can make various modifications for one or more projects at a time. Select one or more used projects and:</p><ul><li>Click the <strong>Notify on New Version </strong>check-box to get notifications when a new version of a particular project is available on the server.</li><li>Use the commands in the toolbar or shortcut menu to:<ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Start using a project in your project" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Add a new project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to the server project you are working with</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Stop using the project in your project" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Remove a used project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> from the server project you are working with.</li></ul><ul><li>Start using an indirectly used project directly.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Changing the used project version" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Change the version]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of a selected used project.</li><li>Review the information of a selected used project.</li><li>Reset changes to a selected used project. You must do this before applying selected actions!</li></ul></li></ul><p>In the <strong>Action </strong>column, you can see the action to be performed on a particular project after the <strong>Apply</strong> button is clicked.</p><p>To turn the action on, click one of the following:</p><ul><li><strong>Apply </strong>- to apply changes and keep the dialog open in order to see what changes are applied. Please note, the applied changes cannot be undone.</li><li><strong>OK </strong>- to apply changes and close the dialog.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189144856 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00213: Configuring report engine properties

- page_id: `189144856`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189144856/Configuring+report+engine+properties
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:41:14.112+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Configuring Report Wizard > Report Wizard Environment options
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can configure the report properties of Report Wizard in the **Environment Options** dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Configure report properties pane.

#### TIP: Tip

Tip

You can select a report property in the **Configure report properties** pane, and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to see its description.

To edit, add, or delete a report property

1. Click **Options** > **Environment** to open the **Environment Options** dialog.
2. Select **Report Wizard** from the tree view to open the **Configure report properties** pane.
3. Click: [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to edit a selected property name and value in the **Report Properties**dialog, and click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to create a property name and value in the **Report Properties** dialog, and click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to delete a selected property name and value from the **Configure report properties** pane.

The following table lists the fields of the **Report Properties**dialog:

| Field Name | Function | Default Value | Possible Value | Required |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Name | The property name | Blank | Text | Yes |
| Value | The property value | Blank | Text | No |
| Description | The property description |  | Text | No |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can configure the report properties of Report Wizard in the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="ReportProperties.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Configure report properties pane.</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7547a99f-e620-4ba6-927d-5689986605fd"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Tip</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can select a report property in the <strong>Configure report properties</strong> pane, and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="EditProperties_button.png" /></ac:image> to see its description.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p>To edit, add, or delete a report property</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Click <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong> to open the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog.</p></li><li><p>Select <strong>Report Wizard</strong> from the tree view to open the <strong>Configure report properties</strong> pane.</p></li><li><p>Click:</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="EditProperties_button.png" /></ac:image> to edit a selected property name and value in the <strong>Report Properties </strong>dialog, and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png" /></ac:image>.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="AddProperties_button.png" /></ac:image> to create a property name and value in the <strong>Report Properties</strong> dialog, and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png" /></ac:image>.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="RemoveProperties_button.png" /></ac:image> to delete a selected property name and value from the <strong>Configure report properties</strong> pane.</p></li></ol><p> </p><p>The following table lists the fields of the <strong>Report Properties </strong>dialog:</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Field Name </th><th>Function </th><th>Default Value </th><th>Possible Value </th><th>Required </th></tr><tr><td> Name</td><td>The property name</td><td> Blank </td><td>Text </td><td>Yes</td></tr><tr><td> Value</td><td>The property value </td><td> Blank </td><td>Text </td><td>No </td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Description</td><td colspan="1">The property description</td><td colspan="1"> </td><td colspan="1">Text</td><td colspan="1">No</td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189144870 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00215: Configuring template mappings

- page_id: `189144870`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189144870/Configuring+template+mappings
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:41:14.652+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Configuring Report Wizard > Report Wizard Environment options
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can use the **Environment Options** dialog to configure the template mappings of Report Wizard.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Configure template mappings pane.

To edit, create, or delete a template mapping

1. Click **Options** > **Environment**. The **Environment Options** dialog will open.
2. Select **Report Wizard** from the tree view. The **Configure template mappings** pane will open.
3. Click: [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to edit a selected filename extension and template type in the **T****emplate mappings**dialog, and click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to create a filename extension and template type in the **T****emplate mappings** dialog, and click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to delete a selected filename extension and template type from the **Configure template mappings**pane.

The following table lists the fields of the **T****emplate mappings** dialog:

| Field Name | Function | Default Value | Possible Value | Required |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| File extension | The filename extension (not case-sensitive). | Blank | Text | Yes |
| Template type | The template type. You can select any of the following: html, odp, docbook, dbk, odt, ods, docx. | Blank | Text | Yes |

#### WARNING: Warning

Warning

Deleting a template mapping from the**Environment Options**dialog will cause the file extension mapping to be removed from the modeling tool. To restore the default setting, restart the modeling tool.

The <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog will open.</p></li><li><p>Select <strong>Report Wizard</strong> from the tree view. The <strong>Configure template mappings</strong> pane will open.</p></li><li><p>Click:</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="EditProperties_button.png" /></ac:image> to edit a selected filename extension and template type in the <strong>T</strong><strong>emplate mappings </strong>dialog, and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png" /></ac:image>.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="AddProperties_button.png" /></ac:image> to create a filename extension and template type in the <strong>T</strong><strong>emplate mappings</strong> dialog, and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png" /></ac:image>.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="RemoveProperties_button.png" /></ac:image> to delete a selected filename extension and template type from the <strong>Configure template mappings </strong>pane.</p><p><br /></p></li></ol><p>The following table lists the fields of the <strong>T</strong><strong>emplate mappings</strong> dialog:</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Field Name </th><th>Function </th><th>Default Value </th><th>Possible Value </th><th>Required</th></tr><tr><td>File extension</td><td>The filename extension (not case-sensitive).</td><td>Blank</td><td>Text </td><td>Yes </td></tr><tr><td>Template type </td><td><p>The template type. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=209257507 space=MD2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00216: Configuring used projects in 3DEXPERIENCE platform

- page_id: `209257507`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/209257507/Configuring+used+projects+in+3DEXPERIENCE+platform
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-02-04T14:54:10.211+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform > Managing project usages via 3DEXPERIENCE platform
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can see all used projects and their versions in the **Advanced Project Usages Configuration** dialog.

To open the **Advanced Project Usages Configuration** dialog

1. In the main menu, click Collaborate > Project Usages > Project Usages .
2. In the Project Usages dialog, click [ATTACHMENT filename='usages_configuration.png'] . [ATTACHMENT filename='advanced_project_usages_configuration.png']

In the **Advanced Project Usages Configuration** dialog, you can modify one or several used projects at a time. Select one or more used projects and use the commands in the toolbar or shortcut menu to:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using a server project in 3DEXPERIENCE platform' space=''] to the server project you are working with
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Removing server project usages in 3DEXPERIENCE platform' space=''] from the server project you are working with.
- Start using an indirectly used project directly.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Changing used project iterations' space=''] of a selected used project.
- Review the information of a selected used project.
- Reset changes to a selected used project. NOTE: You must do this before applying the selected actions!

In the **Action**column, you can see the action to be performed on a particular project after the **Apply** button is clicked.

To turn the action on, click one of the following:

- Apply - to apply changes and keep the dialog open in order to see what changes are applied. Please note that the applied changes cannot be undone.
- OK - to apply changes and close the dialog.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can see all used projects and their versions in the <strong>Advanced Project Usages Configuration</strong> dialog.</p><p><br /></p><p>To open the <strong>Advanced Project Usages Configuration</strong> dialog</p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu, click <strong>Collaborate </strong>&gt; <strong>Project Usages</strong> &gt; <strong>Project Usages</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Project Usages</strong> dialog, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="usages_configuration.png" /></ac:image>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="advanced_project_usages_configuration.png" /></ac:image><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p><br />In the <strong>Advanced Project Usages Configuration</strong> dialog, you can modify one or several used projects at a time. Select one or more used projects and u<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="ea8e0aae-4b61-415d-bd5c-322e1a1fa7e6">se the commands in the toolbar</ac:inline-comment-marker> or shortcut menu to:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Using a server project in 3DEXPERIENCE platform" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Add a new project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to the server project you are working with</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Removing server project usages in 3DEXPERIENCE platform" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Remove a used project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> from the server project you are working with.</li><li>Start using an indirectly used project directly.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Changing used project iterations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Change the version]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of a selected used project.</li><li>Review the information of a selected used project.</li><li>Reset changes to a selected used project. NOTE: You must do this before applying the selected actions!</li></ul><p>In the <strong>Action </strong>column, you can see the action to be performed on a particular project after the <strong>Apply</strong> button is clicked.</p><p>To turn the action on, click one of the following:</p><ul><li><strong>Apply </strong>- to apply changes and keep the dialog open in order to see what changes are applied. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141765 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
````
## PAGE 00217: Connection Point Reference

- page_id: `189141765`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141765/Connection+Point+Reference
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:38:30.061+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > State Machine > State
- labels: ['connection-point-reference']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Connection Point Reference represents an entry to or an exit from the submachine State. It can be used as the source or target of a transition.

The following procedures show you how to work with Connection Point Reference:

##### Drawing the Connection Point Reference

To draw the Connection Point Reference on the submachine State

1. On the State diagram palette, click the **Connection Point Reference** button.
2. Click the shape of the submachine State. The **Select Entry/Exit Point** dialog opens.
3. Select *entry point* to define the entry into the submachine State, or *exit point* to define the exit from the submachine State. The Connection Point Reference is drawn on the submachine State with a defined entry or exit point.

##### Checking the assigned entry/exit point to Connection Point Reference

To see the assigned entry/exit point

- Open the **Connection Point Reference** dialog. The **Entry** or **Exit** properties display the defined entries.

##### Assigning the entry/exit points to the Connection Point Reference

To assign the entry/exit points to the Connection Point Reference

- Right-click the Connection Point Reference shape, and, on the shortcut menu ,click **Select****Entry/Exit Point**.

In the **Select** **Entry/Exit** **Point** dialog, only the entry and exit points that are created at the same State Machine as the submachine State are listed. If there are no entry/exit points at the same State machine, the **Select Entry/Exit Point** dialog is not opened when drawing the Connection Point Reference.

##### Displaying the Connection Point References

To display the Connection Point References on the submachine State shape

1. Select the submachine State shape of the Connection Point References you want to display.
2. Open the **Select****Points**dialog by performing one of the following steps: **[IMAGE alt='' src='']** ****
  - From the shortcut menu, select **Display** > **Display Points**.
  - On the diagram toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select **Display Points**.
3. In the dialog, select the Connection Point References that you want to display, and click **OK**. [ATTACHMENT filename='select_points_dialog.png'] Selected Connection Point References are displayed on the submachine State shape. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space='']

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141380 space=MD2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00218: Connector

- page_id: `189141380`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141380/Connector
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-06T19:45:25.212+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1896492679

1896492681

1896492680

A Connector specifies a link that enables communication between two or more instances. This link can be an instance of an association, or it can represent the possibility of the instances being able to communicate because their identities are known by virtue of being passed on as parameters, held in variables or slots, or because the communicating instances are the same instance.

The link can be realized by something as simple as a pointer or by something as complex as a network connection. In contrast to the associations, which specify the links between any instance of the associated classifiers, the connectors specify the links between instances playing the connected parts only.

Each connector can be attached to two or more connectable elements, each representing a set of instances.

You can specify connector and connector end properties in the connector and connector end Specification window. In the same window, you can find the description of each property. Descriptions are presented in the description area of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''].

##### Drawing a connector end

A Connector has at least two ends, each representing the participation of instances of the classifiers typing the connectable elements attached to the end. The set of Connector ends is ordered. The connector end information can be displayed on the diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Selecting a Port automatically when drawing a Connector

To select a Port automatically when drawing a Connector from a Port to a Part

1. Select a Port on the diagram pane.
2. On the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select Part shape. The **Select Port** menu appears. The menu appears if a new Port can be created on the Part or the Part has hidden Ports.
3. Select one of the following command:

- None - if you want to connect connector straight to the part.
- On a port in the list. Hidden ports of the part are listed. The selected port will be represented on the diagram and the connector will be connected to it.
- New Port - to create a new port with the same name, type and multiplicity as the port from which the connector is drawn.
- New Nested Port - to create a new nested port. Note that the command exists only if the connector is drawn from the nested port.

You can draw a connector to a Port too and the menu appears. As a result, the nested Port is created.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Hiding Connector type

You can hide the type name of the represented Connector on the diagram pane.

To hide the type name of Connector

1. Select a single or any number of Connectors on the diagram pane.
2. Right-click to open the shortcut menu.
3. From the shortcut menu, select Symbol Properties.
4. In the Symbol Properties dialog, set the Show Type option value to false. The name of the Connector type is hidden and no longer visible in the Connector label.

1896492678

**On this page**

- [Drawing a connector end](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Connector#Connector-Drawingaconnectorend)
- [Selecting a Port automatically when drawing a Connector](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Connector#Connector-SelectingaPortautomaticallywhendrawingaConnector)
- [Hiding Connector type](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Connector#Connector-HidingConnectortype)

**Related pages**

- Editing property values
- [Setting an Information Flow](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Information+Flow)
- Reversing Information Flow direction
- Model Elements

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="662ab2b7-c6ee-4cda-892d-8a8ab849926e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1896492679</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a015aa7a-9214-4600-ab83-4edb71aea6cc"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1896492681</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7a6ef9ad-afb8-4432-8eb3-3cbd219f3316"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1896492680</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>A Connector specifies a link that enables communication between two or more instances. This link can be an instance of an association, or it can represent the possibility of the instances being able to communicate because their identities are known by virtue of being passed on as parameters, held in variables or slots, or because the communicating instances are the same instance.</p><p>The link can be realized by something as simple as a pointer or by something as complex as a network connection. In contrast to the associations, which specify the links between any instance of the associated classifiers, the connectors specify the links between instances playing the connected parts only.</p><p>Each connector can be attached to two or more connectable elements, each representing a set of instances.</p><p>You can specify connector and connector end properties in the connector and connector end Specification window. In the same window, you can find the description of each property. Descriptions are presented in the description area of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3>Drawing a connector end</h3><p>A Connector has at least two ends, each representing the participation of instances of the classifiers typing the connectable elements attached to the end. The set of Connector ends is ordered. The connector end information can be displayed on the diagram.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="connector_end.png" /></ac:image></p><h3>Selecting a Port automatically when drawing a Connector</h3><p>To select a Port automatically when drawing a Connector from a Port to a Part</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a Port on the diagram pane.</li><li><p>On the smart manipulator toolbar, click <ac:image ac:title="Connector" ac:alt="Connector"><ri:attachment ri:filename="connector_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and select Part shape. The <strong>Select Port</strong> menu appears.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c44ae08c-f167-45be-a4b5-6fc39c3a2351"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The menu appears if a new Port can be created on the Part or the Part has hidden Ports.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Select one of the following command:</li></ol><ul><li><strong>None</strong> - if you want to connect connector straight to the part.</li><li>On a port in the list. Hidden ports of the part are listed. The selected port will be represented on the diagram and the connector will be connected to it.</li><li><strong>New Port</strong> - to create a new port with the same name, type and multiplicity as the port from which the connector is drawn.</li><li><strong>New Nested Port</strong> - to create a new nested port. Note that the command exists only if the connector is drawn from the nested port.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d1b8c24d-f20e-4fdd-98c9-8de199f52d66"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can draw a connector to a Port too and the menu appears. As a result, the nested Port is created.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image ac:title="Drawing Connector to Part" ac:alt="Drawing Connector to Part"><ri:attachment ri:filename="draw_connector_to_part.png" /></ac:image></p><h3>Hiding Connector type</h3><p>You can hide the type name of the represented Connector on the diagram pane.</p><p>To hide the type name of Connector</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a single or any number of Connectors on the diagram pane.</li><li>Right-click to open the shortcut menu.</li><li>From the shortcut menu, select Symbol Properties.</li><li>In the <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog, set the <strong>Show Type</strong> option value to <em>false.</em><br />The name of the Connector type is hidden and no longer visible in the Connector label.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3aba7f54-29fc-44da-b1f3-932a2d42b191"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1896492678</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><ul><li><span class="toc-item-body"><a class="toc-link" href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Connector#Connector-Drawingaconnectorend">Drawing a connector end</a></span></li><li><span class="toc-item-body"><a class="toc-link" href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Connector#Connector-SelectingaPortautomaticallywhendrawingaConnector">Selecting a Port automatically when drawing a Connector</a></span></li><li><span class="toc-item-body"><a class="toc-link" href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Connector#Connector-HidingConnectortype">Hiding Connector type</a></span></li></ul><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Editing+property+values">Editing property values</a></li><li><span class="toc-item-body"><span class="toc-item-body"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Information+Flow">Setting an Information Flow</a></span></span></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Reversing+Information+Flow+direction">Reversing Information Flow direction</a><span class="toc-item-body"><span class="toc-item-body"><br /></span></span></li><li class="ancestor-link parent-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/UML+elements">Model Elements</a></li></ul><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141384 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00219: Constraint

- page_id: `189141384`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141384/Constraint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:38:09.086+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Constraint represents additional semantic information attached to the constrained elements. It is an assertion that indicates a restriction that must be satisfied by a correct design of the system. The constrained elements are those elements required to evaluate the constraint specification. In addition, the context of the Constraint can be accessed, and can be used as the namespace for interpreting names used in the specification.

A Constraint is represented as a linguistic, enclosed in braces ({}), statement in some formal (OCL, C++, and other), or a natural language. There are 14 standard constraints in UML such as association, global, and parameter. You can also define your own constraints.

You can create the following constraints:

- Time Constraint. It specifies the combination of min and max timing interval values.
- Duration Constraint. It defines a value specification that indicates the temporal distance between two time instants.
- Constraint. It represents an additional semantic information attached to the constrained elements.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Link' space=''] . It is a graphical representation of a constraint element.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### NOTE: Constraints for validation

Constraints for validation

Constraints can also be used to define [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation' space=''] for your model.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='']

There are 14 standard constraints in UML such as association, global, and parameter. You can also define your own constraints.</p><p>You can create the following constraints:</p><ul><li>Time Constraint. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141387 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00220: Constraint Link

- page_id: `189141387`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141387/Constraint+Link
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:38:09.581+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Constraint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Constraint Link is a graphical representation of a constraint element. The Constraint Link is shown on the diagram pane as a dashed arrow from one shape to another. You can assign a particular constraint to the Constraint Link. The constraint assigned to the Constraint Link is labeled in braces: {} . See an example of the constraint representation in the following figure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

On the Constraint Link, you can display the constraint direction arrow. The direction of the arrow represents a relevant information within the constraint. The client (the tail of the arrow) is mapped to the first position and the supplier (arrowhead) is mapped to the second position in the constraint.

You can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='']the constraint symbol properties in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space=''].

After applying the constraint to the Constraint Link, you can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing property values' space='']the constraint in the Constraint specification window. In the same window, you can find the description of all constraint properties. Descriptions are presented in the description area of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''].

To draw a Constraint Link

1. In the Class, Use Case, Sequence, or Activity [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space=''] , under the Common category, expand the Constraint command and select the Constraint Link.
2. Draw a Constraint Link between desired shapes.

To add a constraint expression to the Constraint Link

- From the Constraint Link shortcut menu, choose the Select Constraint and do one of the following:
  - Select Create Constraint to create a new constraint.
  - Choose a constraint from the list.

To display a Constraint Link direction arrow

1. Select the Constraint Link and from the shortcut menu, select **Show Arrow**. 
The direction of the Constraint Link will be displayed. See an example in the following figure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Actions' space='']
