## PAGE 00258: Creating a template for an OpenDocument spreadsheet or Microsoft Excel spreadsheet

- page_id: `189145081`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189145081/Creating+a+template+for+an+OpenDocument+spreadsheet+or+Microsoft+Excel+spreadsheet
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:41:27.240+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Template Creation Tutorial > Linking report templates to the modeling tool > Supported file types
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a template for an OpenDocument spreadsheet or Microsoft Excel spreadsheet

1. Open OpenOffice.org Calc or Microsoft Excel.
2. Add the code to the template file.
3. Save the template file.
4. Add the template to Report Wizard.

All multiline directives such as **#if, #else,****#elseif**, **#foreach**, and **#macro** must be used under the following conditions:

1. The beginning and end of a statement must be declared within a single cell. A #macro statement must be declared in a single cell. [ATTACHMENT filename='worddav195c3297d20439835819f6152c9fb7fe.png']
2. You can use #foreach in a single cell record only. To create data for multiple rows, use the #forrow directive instead, see figure below. [ATTACHMENT filename='worddav6f99f5e91aea399e24eac62e0953d947.png']
3. You can use #forcol to create data for multiple columns, see figure below. You can use the statement in conjunction with #forrow . [ATTACHMENT filename='worddav55c194a898ab2d279c85ddb229cdf770.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a template for an OpenDocument spreadsheet or Microsoft Excel spreadsheet</p><hr /><ol><li>Open OpenOffice.org Calc or Microsoft Excel.</li><li>Add the code to the template file.</li><li>Save the template file.</li><li>Add the template to Report Wizard.</li></ol><p>All multiline directives such as <strong>#if, #else, </strong><strong>#elseif</strong>, <strong>#foreach</strong>, and <strong>#macro</strong> must be used under the following conditions:</p><ol><li>The beginning and end of a statement must be declared within a single cell. A <strong>#macro</strong> statement must be declared in a single cell.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="worddav195c3297d20439835819f6152c9fb7fe.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>You can use <strong>#foreach</strong> in a single cell record only. To create data for multiple rows, use the <strong>#forrow</strong> directive instead, see figure below.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="worddav6f99f5e91aea399e24eac62e0953d947.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>You can use <strong>#forcol</strong> to create data for multiple columns, see figure below. You can use the statement in conjunction with <strong>#forrow</strong>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="worddav55c194a898ab2d279c85ddb229cdf770.png" /></ac:image></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189142437 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00259: Creating a validation dashboard

- page_id: `189142437`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189142437/Creating+a+validation+dashboard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:39:13.731+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Tools > Metric Suites > Building validation-based metric definitions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

If you have several Metric tables where you calculate metrics using Metric Suites with validation-based metric definitions, you can display the validation results from these tables in a validation dashboard.

800450

To create a validation dashboard follow these steps:

1. .
2. .

##### Creating Metric Suites and Metric Tables to track in a validation dashboard

You can calculate model validation results in Metric Tables based on Metric Suites with validation-based metric definitions. Metrics calculated in these Metric Tables can later be displayed in a validation dashboard to track all validation results in one place. Follow the steps below to create Metric Suites and Metric Tables for a validation dashboard.

To create Metric Suites for a validation dashboard

1. Create a Package to store your Metric Suites.
2. Create a Profile Diagram and name it (e.g., *Metric Suites*).
3. In the Profile diagram, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding a new Metric Suite' space='']Create a Metric Suite using the *BaseMetricSuite* for each validation rule you want to track.
4. Create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Building validation-based metric definitions' space=''] for the Metric Suites created in the previous step. Example of a Metric Suite that tracks a validation ruleFor example, if you want to track the Unused Constraint Parameter validation rule, you can create a Metric Suite named *Unused Constrained Parameter* with the following validation-based metric definitions:Validation-based metric definition named*****Constrained Parameter* with the **Type** property set to **Integer**, **Validation Rule** property set to **Unused Constrained Parameter**, and the **Metric Type** property set to **Element Quantity**.Validation-based metric definition named *Unused Constrained Parameter* with the **Type** property set to **Integer**, **Validation Rule** property set to **Unused Constrained Parameter**, and the **Metric Type** property set to **Failed Element Quantity**.Validation-based metric definition named *Unused Constrained Parameter Percentage* with the **Type** property set to **Real**, **Validation Rule** property set to **Unused Constrained Parameter**, and the **Metric Type** property set to **Failed Element Percentage**.
5. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating a Metric Table' space=''] for each Metric Suite created in step 3 and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Calculating metrics in the Metric Table' space=''] .

##### Creating a validation dashboard

A validation dashboard is a convenient way to track model validation results in one place. It displays metric values from multiple Metric Tables created from validation-based Metric Suites.

To create a validation dashboard

1. Open the Profile Diagram in which you created Metric suites with validation based metric definitions (see step 2 in ).
2. In the Profile diagram, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding a new Metric Suite' space='']Create a Metric Suite using the *BaseMetricSuite* and name it (e.g., Validation Dashboard ). [ATTACHMENT filename='creating_validation_dashboard_metric_suite.png']
3. Open the Specification window of the newly created Metric Suite (e.g., Validation Dashboard Metric Suite, as displayed above) and set the Is Abstract property to true .
4. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Building validation-based metric definitions' space=''] for the Metric Suite to define the structure of the dashboard. Each validation-based metric definition represents a column in the validation dashboard. Example of validation-based metric definitionsFor example, if you want to create a validation dashboard with the *Elements*, *Failed Element Quantity*, and *Failed Element Percentage* columns, create the following validation-based metric definitions:Validation-based metric definition named*****Elements* with the **Type** property set to **Integer** and the **Metric Type** property set to **Element Quantity**.Validation-based metric definition named *Failed Element Quantity* with the **Type** property set to **Integer** and the **Metric Type** property set to **Failed Element Quantity**.Validation-based metric definition named *Failed Element Percentage* with the **Type** property set to **Real** and the **Metric Type** property set to **Failed Element Percentage**.
5. In the same Profile diagram, create a Generalization relationship from each Metric Suite that you created to track a validation rule (step 3 in ) to the abstract Metric Suite created in step 2. [ATTACHMENT filename='creating_metric_suites_to_track_validation_rules.png']
6. Link each validation-based metric definition that calculates the metric you want to display in the validation dashboard to the metric definition representing a specific validation dashboard column (see step 4), as described below:
  1. Open the Specification window of the validation-based metric definition that calculates the metric you want to display in the validation dashboard.
  2. Select the specification cell of the Redefined Property property and click [ATTACHMENT filename='3dot_short.png'] .
  3. On the left side of the Select Property window, find the abstract Metric Suite created in step 2 and select its metric definition representing the validation dashboard column in which you want to display the metric.
  4. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to add the metric definition to the **Selected elements** area. Click **OK**. Example of linking a validation-based metric definitionLet's say you have the *Unused Constraint Parameter* Metric Suite with the *Constraint Parameter* validation-based metric definition that calculates the number of constraint parameters. If you want to display the number of constraint parametersin the *Elements* column of the validation dashboard, you must open the Specification window of the the*Constraint Parameter*validation-based metric definition, and select the *Elements* validation-based metric definition of the abstract Metric Suite (created in step 2) as the value of the **Redefined Property** property.
  5. Repeat steps a through d for each validation-based metric definition calculating the metric you want to display in the validation dashboard.
7. Build the validation dashboard:
  1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating a Metric Table' space=''] that will be your validation dashboard.
  2. In the Metric table, click [ATTACHMENT filename='3dot_long.png'] next to the Metric Suite box.
  3. In the Select Metric Suite dialog, select the abstract Metric Suite created in step 2. Click OK .
  4. Open the Specification window of the Metric table.
  5. Select the specification cell of the Scope property and click [ATTACHMENT filename='3dot_short.png'] .
  6. On the left side of the **Select Elements** dialog, select the Metric Tables you want to track in the validation dashboard. Selecting multiple elementsIf you cannot select multiple elements, click the **Multiple Selection** button on the bottom of the **Select Elements** dialog.
  7. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to add the selected Metric tables to the **Selected elements** area. Click **OK**.
  8. In the Specification widow of the Metric Table, select the specification cell of the Query property and click [ATTACHMENT filename='3dot_short.png'] .
  9. On the left side of the **Query** dialog, select **Create operation** and click the **Operation from Model** operation on the right side of the dialog. Enabling the Expert modeThe **Operation from Model** operation is only available in the Expert mode. To enable the Expert mode, click the **Expert** button on the bottom left corner of the **Query** dialog.
  10. In the **Select Opaque Behavior** dialog, select the *LatestMetricInstances* Opaque Behavior. Click **OK**.
  11. On the left side of the Query dialog, select Element = unspecified located under the LatestMetricInstances1 operation and click the Reset button. [ATTACHMENT filename='removing_argument_of_parameter.png']
  12. On the right side of the Query dialog, select the Contextual Variable operation in the Other operation group and click OK .
  13. Close the Specification window of the Metric Table.

The latest instances from the specified Metric Tables are now collected and displayed in your validation dashboard, as shown below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### This sample validation dashboard tracks the latest metrics from three Metric Tables.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>If you have several Metric tables where you calculate metrics using Metric Suites with validation-based metric definitions, you can display the validation results from these tables in a validation dashboard.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d6843c2c-827d-4099-ab8c-34bafd181fb8"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">800</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="http://youtube.com/watch?v=5QJnTyUXvF0" /></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="height">450</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><p style="text-align: center;"><br /></p><p>To create a validation dashboard follow these steps:</p><ol><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Creating Metric Suites and Metric Tables for a validation dashboard"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Create Metric Suites for validation rules you want to track and create Metric Tables to calculate validation results]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Creating a validation dashboard"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Create a validation dashboard that displays metric values from the Metric Tables created in the previous step]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li></ol><h3><br />Creating Metric Suites and Metric Tables to track in a validation dashboard</h3><p>You can calculate model validation results in Metric Tables based on Metric Suites with validation-based metric definitions. Metrics calculated in these Metric Tables can later be displayed in a validation dashboard to track all validation results in one place. Follow the steps below to create Metric Suites and Metric Tables for a validation dashboard.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create Metric Suites for a validation dashboard</p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Create a Package to store your Metric Suites.</p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Create a Profile Diagram and name it (e.g., <em>Metric Suites</em>).</p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="0e0a2fe7-5c38-4e6c-95b1-ea291c49f2e1">In the Profile diagram</ac:inline-comment-marker>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Adding a new Metric Suite" /><ac:link-body>Create a Metric Suite using the <em>BaseMetricSuite</em></ac:link-body></ac:link> for each validation rule you want to track.</p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Create <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Building validation-based metric definitions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[validation-based metric definitions]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for the Metric Suites created in the previous step.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9b0541b0-3db5-4461-978b-dab84afeab2b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example of a Metric Suite that tracks a validation rule</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>For example, if you want to track the Unused Constraint Parameter validation rule, you can create a Metric Suite named <em>Unused Constrained Parameter</em> with the following validation-based metric definitions:</p><ul><li>Validation-based metric definition named<strong> </strong><em>Constrained Parameter</em> with the <strong>Type</strong> property set to <strong>Integer</strong>, <strong>Validation Rule</strong> property set to <strong>Unused Constrained Parameter</strong>, and the <strong>Metric Type</strong> property set to <strong>Element Quantity</strong>.</li><li>Validation-based metric definition named <em>Unused Constrained Parameter</em> with the <strong>Type</strong> property set to <strong>Integer</strong>, <strong>Validation Rule</strong> property set to <strong>Unused Constrained Parameter</strong>, and the <strong>Metric Type</strong> property set to <strong>Failed Element Quantity</strong>.</li><li>Validation-based metric definition named <em>Unused Constrained Parameter Percentage</em> with the <strong>Type</strong> property set to <strong>Real</strong>, <strong>Validation Rule</strong> property set to <strong>Unused Constrained Parameter</strong>, and the <strong>Metric Type</strong> property set to <strong>Failed Element Percentage</strong>.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating a Metric Table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Create a Metric Table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for each Metric Suite created in step 3 and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Calculating metrics in the Metric Table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[calculate metrics]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li></ol><h3><br />Creating a validation dashboard</h3><p>A validation dashboard is a convenient way to track model validation results in one place. It displays metric values from multiple Metric Tables created from validation-based Metric Suites.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a validation dashboard</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the Profile Diagram in which you created Metric suites with validation based metric definitions (see step 2 in <ac:link ac:anchor="Creating Metric Suites and Metric Tables for a validation dashboard" />).</li><li>In the Profile diagram, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Adding a new Metric Suite" /><ac:link-body>Create a Metric Suite using the <em>BaseMetricSuite</em></ac:link-body></ac:link> and name it (e.g., <em>Validation Dashboard</em>).<br /><br /><ac:image ac:height="204"><ri:attachment ri:filename="creating_validation_dashboard_metric_suite.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Open the Specification window of the newly created Metric Suite (e.g., <em>Validation Dashboard</em> Metric Suite, as displayed above) and set the <strong>Is Abstract</strong> property to <em>true</em>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Building validation-based metric definitions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Build validation-based metric definitions]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for the Metric Suite to define the structure of the dashboard. Each validation-based metric definition represents a column in the validation dashboard.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="880adc39-9e49-4229-a38f-2f77f7d8bb9f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example of validation-based metric definitions</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>For example, if you want to create a validation dashboard with the <em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a23eecf2-6c61-4402-9008-ba8633f4bc6f">Elements</ac:inline-comment-marker></em>, <em>Failed Element Quantity</em>, and <em>Failed Element Percentage</em> columns, create the following validation-based metric definitions:</p><ul><li>Validation-based metric definition named<strong> </strong><em>Elements</em> with the <strong>Type</strong> property set to <strong>Integer</strong> and the <strong>Metric Type</strong> property set to <strong>Element Quantity</strong>.</li><li>Validation-based metric definition named <em>Failed Element Quantity</em> with the <strong>Type</strong> property set to <strong>Integer</strong> and the <strong>Metric Type</strong> property set to <strong>Failed Element Quantity</strong>.</li><li>Validation-based metric definition named <em>Failed Element Percentage</em> with the <strong>Type</strong> property set to <strong>Real</strong> and the <strong>Metric Type</strong> property set to <strong>Failed Element Percentage</strong>.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>In the same Profile diagram, create a Generalization relationship from each Metric Suite that you created to track a validation rule (step 3 in <ac:link ac:anchor="Creating Metric Suites and Metric Tables to track in a validation dashboard" />) to the abstract Metric Suite created in step 2.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="creating_metric_suites_to_track_validation_rules.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Link each validation-based metric definition that calculates the metric you want to display in the validation dashboard to the metric definition representing a specific validation dashboard column (see step 4), as described below:<ol><li>Open the Specification window of the validation-based metric definition that calculates the metric you want to display in the validation dashboard.</li><li>Select the specification cell of the <strong>Redefined Property</strong> property and click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="15"><ri:attachment ri:filename="3dot_short.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li>On the left side of the <strong>Select Property</strong> window, find the abstract Metric Suite created in step 2 and select its metric definition representing the validation dashboard column in which you want to display the metric.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="23"><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_button_long.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to add the metric definition to the <strong>Selected elements</strong> area. Click <strong>OK</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6b84c8b8-b4cb-4528-84ab-0ad56e89d188"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example of linking a validation-based metric definition</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Let's say you have the <em>Unused Constraint Parameter</em> Metric Suite with the <em>Constraint Parameter</em> validation-based metric definition that calculates the number of constraint parameters. If you want to display the number of constraint parameters<span> in the <em>Elements</em> column of the validation dashboard, you must open the Specification window of the <span>the </span><em>Constraint Parameter</em><span> validation-based metric definition, and</span> select the <em>Elements</em> validation-based metric definition of the abstract Metric Suite (created in step 2) as the value of the <strong>Redefined Property</strong> property.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Repeat steps a through d for each validation-based metric definition calculating the metric you want to display in the validation dashboard.</li></ol></li><li>Build the validation dashboard:<ol><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating a Metric Table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Create a Metric Table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> that will be your validation dashboard.</li><li>In the Metric table, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="16"><ri:attachment ri:filename="3dot_long.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> next to the <strong>Metric Suite</strong> box.</li><li>In the <strong>Select Metric Suite</strong> dialog, select the abstract Metric Suite created in step 2. Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li>Open the Specification window of the Metric table.</li><li>Select the specification cell of the <strong>Scope</strong> property and click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="15"><ri:attachment ri:filename="3dot_short.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">On the left side of the <strong>Select Elements</strong> dialog, select the Metric Tables you want to track in the validation dashboard.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="50a2baa7-ce3c-4612-b681-f33ede79ebcf"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Selecting multiple elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you cannot select multiple elements, click the <strong>Multiple Selection</strong> button on the bottom of the <strong>Select Elements</strong> dialog.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="23"><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_button_long.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to add the selected Metric tables to the <strong>Selected elements</strong> area. Click <strong>OK</strong>.</p></li><li>In the Specification widow of the Metric Table, select the specification cell of the <strong>Query</strong> property and click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="15"><ri:attachment ri:filename="3dot_short.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">On the left side of the <strong>Query</strong> dialog, select <strong>Create operation</strong> and click the <strong>Operation from Model</strong> operation on the right side of the dialog.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="af9ac358-c815-40b2-9303-be41418596b0"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Enabling the Expert mode</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <strong>Operation from Model</strong> operation is only available in the Expert mode. To enable the Expert mode, click the <strong>Expert</strong> button on the bottom left corner of the <strong>Query</strong> dialog.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <strong>Select Opaque Behavior</strong> dialog, select the <em>LatestMetricInstances</em> Opaque Behavior. Click <strong>OK</strong>.</p></li><li>On the left side of the <strong>Query</strong> dialog, select <strong>Element = unspecified</strong> located under the <em>LatestMetricInstances1</em> operation and click the <strong>Reset</strong> button.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="removing_argument_of_parameter.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>On the right side of the <strong>Query</strong> dialog, select the <strong>Contextual Variable</strong> operation in the <strong>Other</strong> operation group and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li>Close the Specification window of the Metric Table.</li></ol></li></ol><p><br />The latest instances from the specified Metric Tables are now collected and displayed in your validation dashboard, as shown below.<br /><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="sample_validation_dashboard.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">This sample validation dashboard tracks the latest metrics from three Metric Tables.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141852 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00260: Creating an alternative flow

- page_id: `189141852`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141852/Creating+an+alternative+flow
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:38:37.357+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Use Case > Use Case Scenario sketch
- labels: ['alternative-condition', 'alternative-flow-step']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

879536389

879536391

879536390

To create an alternative flow

1. Open the Use Case Specification window and click the Use Case Scenario Sketch property group.
2. Select the basic flow step you want to create an alternative flow for.
3. In the Alternative Flow tab, click the Add new button [ATTACHMENT filename='add_new_value_button.png'] . The shortcut menu opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='adding_alternative_flow.png']
4. Do one of the following:

- 
  - Select the **Add New Alternative Condition**command or press ALT+R to add a new alternative condition. A new alternative condition is created together with the alternative flow step. Type the name of the alternative condition. The alternative condition for the basic flow is created.
  - Select the Add New Alternative Flow Step command. Press ALT+ENTER to add a new alternative flow step. If there was an alternative condition created in the alternative flow already, a new alternative flow step is created for the selected alternative condition. If in the alternative flow there was no alternative condition, a new alternative flow step is created together with a new alternative condition. Now you can type the name of the new alternative flow step.

Conditions and steps of the alternative flow are numbered automatically. Use the Up [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and Down [IMAGE alt='' src=''] buttons to change the order of conditions in the alternative flow or the order of steps in a particular condition. In the Activity diagram, steps are represented according to their order in the Use Case scenario.

879536388

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Transition' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case diagram' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="44221497-c88b-4d71-8a14-23a3b72c8a14"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">879536389</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2085b824-f483-43fe-968a-b9126b87f541"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">879536391</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="429a62ec-ece0-40c2-821a-bb6f56086b60"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">879536390</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>To create an alternative flow</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the Use Case Specification window and click the <strong>Use Case Scenario Sketch </strong>property group.</li><li>Select the basic flow step you want to create an alternative flow for.</li><li>In the <strong>Alternative Flow </strong>tab, click the Add new button <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_new_value_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. The shortcut menu opens.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="adding_alternative_flow.png" /></ac:image></li><li><p>Do one of the following:</p></li></ol><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li><p>Select the <strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Add New Alternative Condition </strong>command or press ALT+R to add a new alternative condition. A new alternative condition is created together with the alternative flow step. Type the name of the alternative condition. The alternative condition for the basic flow is created.</p></li><li>Select the <strong>Add New Alternative Flow Step </strong>command. Press ALT+ENTER to add a new alternative flow step. If there was an alternative condition created in the alternative flow already, a new alternative flow step is created for the selected alternative condition. If in the alternative flow there was no alternative condition, a new alternative flow step is created together with a new alternative condition. Now you can type the name of the new alternative flow step.</li></ul></li></ul><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Conditions and steps of the alternative flow are numbered automatically. Use the Up <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="move_up_selected_row_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and Down <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="move_down_selected_row.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> buttons to change the order of conditions in the alternative flow or the order of steps in a particular condition. In the Activity diagram, steps are represented according to their order in the Use Case scenario.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="08d48bca-7a36-46cb-a6dc-8a7cef6561fb"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">879536388</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Transition" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="State Machine diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Activity diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Sequence diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Use Case diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

## PAGE 00261: Creating an aspect diagram

- page_id: `189141201`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141201/Creating+an+aspect+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-06T19:45:22.829+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming > Diagram aspects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

581406002

581406004

581406003

Before creating an aspect diagram, the following conditions must be met:

- The custom aspects are created in the project. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating custom aspects' space='']>]]> If you have [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Plugin Documentation' space='SYSMLP2024xR2'] installed, the predefined aspect diagrams already exist in your project. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Finding predefined diagram aspects' space='']Predefined diagram aspects >>
- The Use Aspects option is enabled. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Enabling or disabling diagram aspects option' space='']How to enable the diagram aspects option >>

Creating a new aspect diagram is similar to creating any other diagram. Every way to create a diagram is described here: [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating diagrams' space='']. The difference is that aspect [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Composite Structure diagram' space=''] are automatically collected under the separate **Aspect Diagrams** group of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating diagrams' space='']**Create Diagram** dialog. If you have [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Plugin Documentation' space='SYSMLP2024xR2'] installed, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Internal Block Diagram' space='SYSMLP2024xR2'] with custom and predefined aspects are collected under the same **Aspect Diagrams** group.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Aspect Diagram group, highlighted in orange, with predefined and custom UML and SysML aspect diagrams. The illustration displays concepts from SysML Plugin.

581406001

**Sample model**

The model used in the figures of this page is **Diagram aspects**. To open this sample:

- Download [ATTACHMENT filename='diagram aspects.mdzip'].
- Find in the modeling tool: *<modeling tool installation directory>\samples\SysML\diagram aspects.mdzip.*

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2eea682d-6132-4919-b097-b1fe2b788eb7"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">581406002</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="832b5f0b-f69e-4fab-9331-ad0af76897d8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">581406004</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="df5481be-0c5c-48fc-8a29-80df9d841825"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">581406003</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Before creating an aspect diagram, the following conditions must be met:</p><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">The custom aspects are created in the project. <span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating custom aspects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to create a custom aspect >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="76645e7d-a3c6-4b1f-997f-63a00eea5366"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you have <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" ri:content-title="SysML Plugin Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SysML Plugin]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> installed, the predefined aspect diagrams already exist in your project. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Finding predefined diagram aspects" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">Predefined diagram aspects &gt;</span>&gt;</ac:link-body></ac:link></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>The <strong>Use Aspects </strong>option is enabled. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Enabling or disabling diagram aspects option" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">H</span><span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link">ow to enable the diagram aspects option &gt;</span>&gt;</span></ac:link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p><br /></p><p>Creating a new aspect diagram is similar to creating any other diagram. Every way to create a diagram is described here: <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating diagrams" /></ac:link>. The difference is that aspect <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Composite Structure diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Composite Structure diagrams]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> are automatically collected under the separate <strong>Aspect Diagrams</strong> group of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating diagrams" /><ac:link-body><strong>Create Diagram</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>. If you have <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" ri:content-title="SysML Plugin Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SysML plugin]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> installed, the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" ri:content-title="SysML Internal Block Diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Internal Block diagrams]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> with custom and predefined aspects are collected under the same <strong>Aspect Diagrams</strong> group.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="aspect_diagram_group.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Aspect Diagram group, highlighted in orange, with predefined and custom UML and SysML aspect diagrams. The illustration displays concepts from SysML Plugin.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f06c25b6-2342-404e-8e9e-e9a5ac9d6d0b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">581406001</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Sample model</strong></p><p>The model used in the figures of this page is <strong>Diagram aspects</strong>. To open this sample:</p><ul><li><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Download <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="diagram aspects.mdzip"><ri:page ri:content-title="Sample models" /></ri:attachment></ac:link>. </span></span></span></span></li><li><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span class="confluence-link">Find in the modeling tool: <em><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">samples\SysML</span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">\<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">diagram aspects</span>.mdzip.</span></span></em></span></span></span><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span></span></span></span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189140782 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00262: Creating an empty Instance Table

- page_id: `189140782`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189140782/Creating+an+empty+Instance+Table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:37:11.272+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Tables > Instance table
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an empty Instance Table

1. In the Containment tree, select an element to be the owner of the Instance Table.
2. Do one of the following: The newly created Instance Table opens on the right side of the application window.
  - From the main menu, select Diagrams > Create Diagram . Type “ins” and press Enter.
  - On the main toolbars, click the Create Diagram button. Type “ins” and press Enter.
  - Press Ctrl+N. Type “ins” and press Enter.
  - Right-click the element and from the shortcut menu select Create Diagram > Other Diagrams > Instance Table .

- If the default name of the Instance Table is not appropriate, type a new one directly in the Containment tree.
- If the owner of the Instance Table is also a classifier, it becomes the default value of the Classifier box in this Instance Table. [ATTACHMENT filename='new_instance_table_with_default_classifier.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an empty Instance Table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Containment tree, select an element to be the owner of the Instance Table.</li><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>From the main menu, select <strong>Diagrams</strong> &gt; <strong>Create Diagram</strong>. Type “ins” and press Enter.</li><li>On the main toolbars, click the <strong>Create Diagram</strong> button. Type “ins” and press Enter.</li><li>Press Ctrl+N. Type “ins” and press Enter.</li><li>Right-click the element and from the shortcut menu select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>Other </strong><strong>Diagrams</strong> &gt; <strong>Instance Table</strong>.</li></ul>The newly created Instance Table opens on the right side of the application window.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4864ee64-0471-41e8-b42e-dcde36bf0813"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>If the default name of the Instance Table is not appropriate, type a new one directly in the Containment tree.</li><li>If the owner of the Instance Table is also a classifier, it becomes the default value of the Classifier box in this Instance Table.<br /> <ac:image ac:title="Owner of the Instance Table becomes classifier by default" ac:alt="Owner of the Instance Table becomes classifier by default"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_instance_table_with_default_classifier.png" /></ac:image></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141854 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00264: Creating an exceptional flow

- page_id: `189141854`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141854/Creating+an+exceptional+flow
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:38:37.722+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Use Case > Use Case Scenario sketch
- labels: ['exception-type', 'exception-flow-step']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

879686455

879686457

879686456

INLINE

To create an exceptional flow

1. Open the Use Case Specification window and click the Use Case Scenario Sketch property group.
2. In the use case scenario, select the basic flow step you want to create an exceptional flow for.
3. In the Exceptional Flow tab, click the Add New button [ATTACHMENT filename='add_new_value_button.png'] . The shortcut menu opens. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. Do one of the following:

- 
  - Select the **Add New Exception Type**command or press Alt+T. The drop down list opens. Type the name of a new exception type. The exception type is created (a new class is created). The created exception type contains an unnamed exceptional flow step.
  - Select the **Add New Exceptional Flow Step**command or press Alt+Enter. If there was an exceptional type created in the exceptional flow already, a new exceptional flow step is created for the selected exception type. If there was no exception type in the alternative flow, a new exceptional flow step is created together with a new exception type. Now you can type the name of the new exceptional flow step.

Types and steps of the exceptional flow are numbered automatically. Use the Up [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and Down [IMAGE alt='' src=''] buttons to change the order of types in the exceptional flow or the order of steps in the particular type. In the Activity diagram, steps are represented according to their order in the Use Case scenario.

879686454

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case diagram' space='']

The shortcut menu opens.</span><br /><span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="adding_exceptional_flow.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><p>Do one of the following:</p></li></ol><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li><p>Select the <strong>Add New Exception Type </strong>command or press Alt+T. The drop down list opens. Type the name of a new exception type. The exception type is created (a new class is created). The created exception type contains an unnamed exceptional flow step.</p></li><li><p>Select the <strong>Add New Exceptional Flow Step </strong>command or press Alt+Enter. If there was an exceptional type created in the exceptional flow already, a new exceptional flow step is created for the selected exception type. If there was no exception type in the alternative flow, a new exceptional flow step is created together with a new exception type. Now you can type the name of the new exceptional flow step.</p></li></ul></li></ul><p><span>Types and steps of the exceptional flow are numbered automatically. Use the Up <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="move_up_selected_row_button.png" /></ac:image> and Down <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="move_down_selected_row.png" /></ac:image>  buttons to change the order of types in the exceptional flow or the order of steps in the particular type. In the Activity diagram, steps are represented according to their order in the Use Case scenario.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="19245a41-c7e4-45f2-9339-15d4764e20ac"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">879686454</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Transition" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="State Machine diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Activity diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Sequence diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Use Case diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189143503 space=MD2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00265: Creating and managing categories

- page_id: `189143503`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189143503/Creating+and+managing+categories
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-06T19:45:53.886+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Using Teamwork Cloud > Managing server projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Using categories and grouping projects into them, you can organize the structure of your server projects' repository according to your needs.

By default, there is a single category *Uncategorized*, but you can create as many categories as you need. A project can be created directly in a selected category and later easily moved to another one. The category can be created in advance or while [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding projects to Teamwork Cloud' space='']. Categories can be renamed and removed. Also, they can have sub-categories.

The TWCloud project categories in the modeling tool dialogs will be hidden if a user does not have the *Manage Categories* or*Read-Only*permission to at least one project inside of these categories.

Read the following to learn how

- 
- 
- 
- 
- 

Categories can also be managed via the Resource application. Learn more about this in .

You need to have the Manage categories permission assigned to the Global scope to be able to create categories.

createTo create a category

1. From the Collaborate menu, choose Projects . The Manage Projects dialog opens.
2. Be sure the Categorized View is turned on in the dialog. If it is not, click [ATTACHMENT filename='categorized_view.png'] on the toolbar of the dialog.
3. Choose one of the following:
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='create_category.png'] on the toolbar of the open dialog.
  - Right-click anywhere in the project list area and then select [ATTACHMENT filename='create_category.png'] Create Category .
4. After the **Create Category** dialog opens, select a **Parent** category and type a new category name. Select an existing category as a **Parent** category to nest a new category within.Select a *Root* node as a **Parent** category to create a new top-level category. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. Click OK to confirm the creation. The category is created. You can find it in the project list ordered alphabetically.

renameTo rename a category

1. From the Collaborate menu, choose Projects . The Manage Projects dialogs opens.
2. Select a category or a project that belongs to the category you want to rename and then do either:
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='rename_category.png'] on the toolbar of the open dialog.
  - Right-click the selection and then select [IMAGE alt='' src=''] **Rename Category**. The *Uncategorized* category cannot be renamed*.*
3. When the Rename Category dialog opens, type a new category name.
4. Click OK . The category is renamed.

moveTo move a project to another category

1. From the Collaborate menu, choose Projects . The Manage Projects dialogs opens.
2. Select the project you want to move and do one of the following:
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='move_to_category.png'] on the toolbar of the open dialog.
  - Right-click the selection and then select [ATTACHMENT filename='move_to_category.png'] Move to Category .
3. When the**Move to Category** dialog opens, select the category to which you want to move the project.
  - To create a new category, select the Create Category button in the Move to Category dialog: [IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. Click OK . The project appears in the selected category.

- You need to have the Manage Resources permission to delete the selected category.
- You need to have the Read Resources/List Resources/List All Resources and Remove Resources permissions to delete all the resources within the selected category.

removeTo remove the category

1. From the Collaborate menu, choose Projects . The Manage Projects dialogs opens.
2. Select the category or the project that belongs to the category you want to remove and then do either:
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_category.png'] on the toolbar of the open dialog.
  - Right-click the selection and then select [IMAGE alt='' src=''] **Remove Category**. The *Uncategorized* category cannot be removed*.*
3. Click Yes to confirm your decision. The category is removed from the server repository together with all the resources in it .

Empty categories are by default shown in the **Manage Projects** and **Open Server Project** dialogs, but you can simply hide them.

emptyTo hide empty categories

- Click [ATTACHMENT filename='show_empty_categories.png'] on the toolbar of the open dialog. Click the button once more to show the empty categories again.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Using categories and grouping projects into them, you can organize the structure of your server projects' repository according to your <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="0451c6b3-39ec-4d32-8c98-6f3a74b67cbe">needs</ac:inline-comment-marker>.</p><p>By default, there is a single category <em>Uncategorized</em>, but you can create as many categories as you need. A project can be created directly in a selected category and later easily moved to another one. The category can be created in advance or while <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Adding projects to Teamwork Cloud" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[adding the project to the server]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. Categories can be renamed and removed. Also, they can have sub-categories. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9ebd8ec1-5ec1-4393-aa4e-8c6e1015f947"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">The TWCloud project categories in the modeling tool dialogs will be hidden if a user does not have the <em>Manage Categories</em> o</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">r </span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Read-Only</em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> permission to at least one project inside of these categories.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>Read the following to learn how</p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="create"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[To create a single category]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="rename"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[To rename a category]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="move"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[To move a project to another category]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="remove"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[To remove the category]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="empty"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[To hide empty categories]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p> </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="edc918a1-2b41-4d4d-9063-2ec9df702777"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Categories can also be managed via the Resource application. Learn more about this in <ac:link><ri:space ri:space-key="TWCloud2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Teamwork Cloud User Guide]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3ff185c0-7342-4825-a23a-92d0fc860495"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You need to have the Manage categories permission assigned to the Global scope to be able to create categories.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="89343c45-2424-4020-a677-9d1171c262b9"><ac:parameter ac:name="">create</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To create a category</p><hr /><ol><li>From the <strong>Collaborate</strong> menu, choose <strong>Projects</strong>. The <strong>Manage Projects</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Be sure the Categorized View is turned on in the dialog. If it is not, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="categorized_view.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the toolbar of the dialog.</li><li>Choose one of the following:<br /><br /><ul><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_category.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the toolbar of the open dialog.</li><li>Right-click anywhere in the project list area and then select <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_category.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><strong>Create </strong><strong>Category</strong>.<br /><br /></li></ul></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">After the <strong>Create Category</strong> dialog opens, select a <strong>Parent</strong> category and type a new category name.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ae28100a-bc48-428c-9d9f-3fdea3dc828a"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Select an existing category as a <strong>Parent</strong> category to nest a new category within.</li><li>Select a <em>Root</em> node as a <strong>Parent</strong> category to create a new top-level category. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="root_node_nested_categories_TWC.png" /></ac:image></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> to confirm the creation.<br />The category is created. You can find it in the project list ordered alphabetically.</li></ol><p> </p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f1c47db4-7792-4b2e-ba26-9b08ad30d3b2"><ac:parameter ac:name="">rename</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To rename a category</p><hr /><ol><li>From the <strong>Collaborate</strong> menu, choose <strong>Projects</strong>. The <strong>Manage Projects</strong> dialogs opens.</li><li>Select a category or a project that belongs to the category you want to rename and then do either:<br /><br /><ul><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="rename_category.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the toolbar of the open dialog.</li><li><p>Right-click the selection and then select <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="rename_category.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> <strong>Rename Category</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3e2a3933-639d-4309-adb3-bdf3a1c06c89"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <em>Uncategorized</em> category cannot be renamed<em>.</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ul></li><li>When the <strong>Rename Category</strong> dialog opens, type a new category name.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br />The category is renamed.</li></ol><p> </p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3282daa0-3f5e-413b-b9f6-fd60f87c6991"><ac:parameter ac:name="">move</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To move a project to another category</p><hr /><ol><li>From the <strong>Collaborate</strong> menu, choose <strong>Projects</strong>. The <strong>Manage Projects</strong> dialogs opens.</li><li>Select the project you want to move and do one of the following:<br /><br /><ul><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="move_to_category.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the toolbar of the open dialog.</li><li>Right-click the selection and then select <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="move_to_category.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> <strong>Move to Category</strong>.<br /><br /></li></ul></li><li><p>When the<strong> Move to Category</strong> dialog opens, select the category to which you want to move the project.</p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To create a new category, select the</span><strong> Create Category</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> button in the </span><strong>Move to Category</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> dialog:</span><br /><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_category_button_in_move_to_category_dialog.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br />The project appears in the selected category.</li></ol><p> </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b0d68d02-ea3c-49ac-95f7-87c9b7177417"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>You need to have the Manage Resources permission to delete the selected category.</li><li>You need to have the Read Resources/List Resources/List All Resources and Remove Resources permissions to delete all the resources within the selected category.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="05ffedaf-49fc-4840-855d-647482274ec4"><ac:parameter ac:name="">remove</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To remove the category</p><hr /><ol><li>From the <strong>Collaborate</strong> menu, choose <strong>Projects</strong>. The <strong>Manage Projects</strong> dialogs opens.</li><li>Select the category or the project that belongs to the category you want to remove and then do either:<ul><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove_category.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the toolbar of the open dialog.</li><li><p>Right-click the selection and then select <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove_category.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> <strong>Remove Category</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="938889ef-613c-4d9a-9dc7-85382930ad60"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <em>Uncategorized</em> category cannot be removed<em>.</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>Yes</strong> to confirm your decision.<br />The category is removed from the server repository <strong>together with all the resources in it</strong>. </li></ol><p> </p><p>Empty categories are by default shown in the <strong>Manage Projects</strong> and <strong>Open Server Project</strong> dialogs, but you can simply hide them.</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ab80dfe7-3d1a-4112-93d1-9abad07af7f3"><ac:parameter ac:name="">empty</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To hide empty categories</p><hr /><ul><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_empty_categories.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the toolbar of the open dialog. Click the button once more to show the empty categories again.<br /><br /></li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189140955 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00266: Creating and managing Legend Items

- page_id: `189140955`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189140955/Creating+and+managing+Legend+Items
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:37:29.905+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming > Legends > Creating Legends
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Alternative method to manage Legend Items

1293695897

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Alternative method to manage Legend Items

1293695899

1293695896

**On this page**

4

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Alternative method to manage Legend Items

1293695898

After [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Legends' space=''], you must create and specify its Legend Items. Legend Itemsdefine different styles used in a diagram or table in a Legend.

##### Creating Legend Items

You can create Legend Items directly from a Legend shape or in the Specification window of a Legend. If you are working with a Legend in a symbol diagram, you can use both methods. However, if you are working with a Legend in a table, you can create Legend Items only in the Specification window of the Legend.

To create a Legend Item from a Legend shape

1. In a diagram, select the shape of the Legend for which you want to create a Legend Item.
2. Click the smart manipulator on the top right corner of the Legend shape, highlighted in the figure below, and select **Create Legend Item**. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] of the newly created Legend Item, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying Legend Items' space=''].
4. Close the Specification window.

The newly created Legend Item is displayed in the Legend shape and applied to diagram symbols according to the properties you specified in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying Legend Items' space='']**Applied To** property group.

To create a Legend Item in the Specification window of a Legend

1. Open the Specification window of the Legend for which you want to create a Legend Item by doing one of the following:
  - In a symbol diagram, double-click the shape of the desired Legend.
  - In a table, hover the mouse pointer over the desired Legend, click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] and select Specification .
2. Click the Legend Items group on the left side of the Specification window.
3. Click the Create button.
4. In the open Specification window of the newly created Legend Item, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying Legend Items' space=''].
5. Close the Specification window.

The newly created Legend Item is displayed in the Legend and applied to diagram symbols or table rows according to the properties you specified in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying Legend Items' space='']**Applied To** property group.

Alternatively, you can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Legends automatically' space=''].

##### Managing Legend Items

The **Legend Items** group in the Specification window of a Legend allows you to create new Legend Items and modify or reorder the existing ones.

To access the Legend Item management area

1. Open the Specification window of a Legend.
2. Click the Legend Items group on the left side of the Specification window.

The Legend Item management area is displayed on the right side of the Specification window. The following table explains how to use the buttons found there to create and modify Legend Items.

| Button name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Up | Select a Legend Item and click the button to move the Legend Item one position up in the list. |
| Down | Select a Legend Item and click the button to move the Legend Item one position down in the list. |
| Create | Click the button to create a Legend Item. The Specification window of the newly created Legend Item opens automatically, allowing you to specify its properties. |
| Clone | Select a Legend Item and click the button to create its copy. The Specification window of the newly created Legend Item clone opens automatically, allowing you to modify its properties. By default, the clone inherits all property values of the Legend Item from which it was cloned. |
| Delete | Select a Legend Item and click the button to remove it. |

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Legend Item management area in the Specification window of a Legend.

#### NOTE: Alternative method to manage Legend Items

Alternative method to manage Legend Items

You can also manage Legend Items in the **Legend Items** property specification cell:

1. Open the Specification window of a Legend.
2. Select the specification cell of the Legend Items property.
3. Complete one of the following actions:
  - To create a Legend Item, click [ATTACHMENT filename='add.png'] .
  - To remove a Legend Item, select the Legend Item you want to remove and click [ATTACHMENT filename='remove.png'] .
  - To reorder Legend Items, click [ATTACHMENT filename='order.png'] and in the open dialog use the Up and Down buttons to change the position of the selected Legend Item.

580309733

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Legends' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Legends' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Legends automatically' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying Legends' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying Legend Items' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189145548 space=MD2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00267: Creating and specifying derived properties

- page_id: `189145548`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189145548/Creating+and+specifying+derived+properties
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-06T19:46:16.247+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > UML Profiling and DSL Guide > Domain Specific Language customization > Using Customization Data > Extending Metamodel with Derived Properties
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To be included in a project as a property extending a UML element, an attribute must be owned by a DSL customization element and stereotyped as «derivedPropertySpecification».

##### Specifying derived properties

To create and specify a derived property

1. Select the customization element for which you want to create a derived property. The derived property will be created for the stereotype or metaclass, defined as a customization target in the selected customization element specification.
2. Select **Create Element** > **Derived Property Specification** from the shortcut menu. The attribute is created. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Type the attribute name. Your Model Browser should look like this at this stage: [IMAGE alt='' src=''] The attribute’s name corresponds to the name of the derived property.Type derived property names in camel case (i.e.,*realizingActivities*).A property name defined in camel case will be displayed in separate words with the first word capitalized. For example, realizingActivities will be changed to *Realizing Activities* (visible in the user interface).
4. Edit property values specifying the derived property in the attribute's Specification window. All properties are specified in the general specification pane. Some are also specified in the **Tags** specification pane. For detailed information, see the properties .

If you do not see the newly created derived property in the element’s [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''], please read the following:

- In the element’s [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] , the specified derived property is visible by default only in the All properties mode. First, try switching to this mode. Also try changing the mode in which you want the derived property to be visible (click the Customize button).
- If you have specified the derived property for the element (with the Used UML Properties property of DSL customization specified) you must add the newly created derived property to the element’s visible properties list. For detailed information, please refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Derived properties visibility' space=''] .

If you need to further customize a customization’s derived property without modifying the existing customization itself, you can create a new customization that can inherit the former one’s properties via a generalization. Then you can redefine the inherited derived property to customize it. The redefining derived property of the new customization overrides that of the original customization.

##### Derived property specification

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Specification window of the derived property

description_tableThe following table describes the properties specified for the derived property.

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Name | The name of the property.This name will be added and visible in the specification of the element whose type is defined as a customization target. |
| Type | The type of the derived property values. You may set a model element type or a data type (e.g., string, boolean, orintegerfrom the UML Standard Profile) as a property value.To display classes and components as property values, select the classifier as a type for the derived property value. |
| Multiplicity | The multiplicity of the derived property values.If the multiplicity is “0..1”, the derived property value will be shown as single.If the multiplicity is “0..*”, the derived property values will be shown as collection. |
| Is Read Only | If true, the derived property is read-only. You cannot edit the result elements for the derived property value.If false, the property is read-write. You can edit the result elements for the derived property according to the expressions defined. |
| Is Ordered | If true, the property values are always displayed in the same order.If false, the property values are displayed in nonpredictable order. |
| Is Unique | If true, the property values are unique. The same element is displayed only once.If false, the property values may be displayed more than once. |
| Expression | One or more expressions of the derived property.An expression defines the criterion for selecting result elements.There are ten possible expression types:Metachain navigation (indirect relations through chains of properties)FindUnionExcludeFilterVarious scriptsImplied RelationType TestProperty TestOperation from Model The criterion for selecting the result elements can also be any combination of the expression types above.For the detailed information, see .Only one Simple Navigation operation criterion can be assigned to the expression of the derived property. |
| Documentation | The text displayed as the derived property description in the element’s and Properties panel. |
| customizationTarget | The specified derived property will be added to one or more element types (stereotypes and/ or metaclasses).The expression calculations begin from the element type of the customization target. |

**Related pages:**

- Extending Metamodel with Derived Properties
- Using Customization Data
- Creating Customization Data
- Defining Expressions

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Your Model Browser should look like this at this stage:</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="derived_property.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0ba5494f-8d67-4947-b341-5ea39c8fbb55"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The attribute’s name corresponds to the name of the derived property.</p><p>Type derived property names in camel case (i.e.,<em>realizingActivities</em>). </p><p>A property name defined in camel case will be displayed in separate words with the first word capitalized. For detailed information, please refer to <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Derived properties visibility" /></ac:link>.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fe5b16f2-6483-4e62-ae01-03a9a126ada3"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(68,71,70);">If you need to further customize a customization’s derived property without modifying the existing customization itself, you can create a new customization that can inherit the former one’s properties via a generalization. Then you can redefine the inherited derived property to customize it. The redefining derived property of the new customization overrides that of the original customization.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3 class="auto-cursor-target">Derived property specification</h3><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="my_derived_property_specification_window.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Specification window of the derived property</h6><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f4cf1036-75fd-4f09-b4a8-783fee3f6362"><ac:parameter ac:name="">description_table</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>The following table describes the properties specified for the derived property.</p><table><colgroup class=""><col class="" /><col class="" /></colgroup><tbody class=""><tr class=""><th>Property</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Name</strong></td><td><p>The name of the property.<br />This name will be added and visible in the specification of the element whose type is defined as a customization target.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Type</strong></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>The type of the derived property values. You may set a model element type or a data type (e.g., string, boolean, orintegerfrom the UML Standard Profile) as a property value.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="86bd63b3-1009-45eb-bfe0-d3bbf518febc"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>To display classes and components as </span><span>property values, select the classifier as a type for the derived </span><span>property value.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Multiplicity</strong></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>The multiplicity of the derived property values.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="00992981-69d2-4101-b56e-1a484480924c"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>If the multiplicity is “0..1”, the derived property value will be </span><span>shown as single.</span><br /><span>If the multiplicity is “0..*”, the derived property values will be </span><span>shown as collection.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p><strong>Is Read Only</strong></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>If <em>true</em>, the derived property is read-only. You cannot edit the result elements for the derived property value.<br />If <em>false</em>, the property is read-write. You can edit the result elements for the derived property according to the expressions defined.</p></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Is Ordered</strong></td><td><p>If <em>true</em>, the property values are always displayed in the same order.<br />If <em>false</em>, the property values are displayed in nonpredictable order.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Is Unique</strong></td><td><p>If <em>true</em>, the property values are unique. The same element is displayed only once.<br />If <em>false</em>, the property values may be displayed more than once.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p><strong>Expression</strong></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>One or more expressions of the derived property.<br />An expression defines the criterion for selecting result elements.</p><p>There are ten possible expression types:</p><ul><li><p>Metachain navigation (indirect relations through chains of properties)</p></li><li><p>Find</p></li><li><p>Union</p></li><li><p>Exclude</p></li><li><p>Filter</p></li><li><p>Various scripts</p></li><li>Implied Relation</li><li>Type Test</li><li>Property Test</li><li>Operation from Model <br /><br /></li></ul><p>The criterion for selecting the result elements can also be any combination of the expression types above.<br />For the detailed information, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Defining expressions" /></ac:link>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9bf36350-11cd-45d0-b712-d7229b99c9a9"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Only one Simple Navigation operation criterion can be assigned to the expression of the derived property.   </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1"><strong>Documentation</strong></td><td colspan="1"><p>The text displayed as the derived property description in the element’s <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link> and Properties panel.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1"><strong>customizationTarget</strong></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>The specified derived property will be added to one or more element types (stereotypes and/ or metaclasses).</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="945286fa-9ad8-49bb-8af2-91e37eab3f6f"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>The expression calculations begin from the element type of the customization target.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><p class="ancestor-link parent-link"><strong>Related pages:</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link parent-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Extending+Metamodel+with+Derived+Properties">Extending Metamodel with Derived Properties</a></li><li class="ancestor-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Using+Customization+Data">Using Customization Data</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Creating+Customization+Data">Creating Customization Data</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Defining+expressions">Defining Expressions</a></li></ul>
